name: BU Student Organizations
description:
With 450+ student organizations, it’s easy to pursue a favorite hobby or get a taste of something different. We’ll help you find students who share your interests—or get the ball rolling with you to create a new group. If you’re looking to dovetail your pastimes with your long-term goals, a chat with us can help.

Drop by and share what makes you tick, or check us out on Facebook and Twitter.
summary: With 450+ student organizations, it’s easy to pursue a favorite hobby or get a taste of something different. We’ll help you find students who share your interests—or get the ball rolling with you to create a new group.

name: Test Club
description:
The test club is used by the Student Activites Office for demonstrations, trainings, and basic information to ensure all organizations are well-informed.

PSA: This is stricly an example club managed by SAO staff only.
summary: The test club is your perfect example of a student organization at BU! This club is used primarily for student organization trainings and workshops that show orgs how its done! Here we teach students how to utilize Engage, submit events, and spend money

name: Archaeology Society
description: Our mission is to learn about, understand, and promote awareness of Archaeology and related fields such as Anthropology, Cultural Hertiage, and Art History all while having an awesome time! We offer an environment in which students can explore and discuss issues relating to Archaeology while participating in a wide variety of activities including workshops, field trips, and lectures. Our club could be your greatest resource for receiving tips on how to become a more competitive archaeologist, getting information on joining a field school, or interacting with celebrated professionals from Boston University and abroad. If you are an Archaeology or Anthropology major, or even if you just have an interest in the exciting field of archaeology, we invite you to explore our website, come to our meetings and activities, or email us to see what we're all about.
summary: Our mission is to learn about, understand, and promote awareness of Archaeology and related fields such as Anthropology, Cultural Hertiage, and Art History all while having an awesome time! We offer an environment in which students can explore and disc...

name: Accounting Association
description: Bridging the gap between the academic and professional worlds, the BU Accounting Association sponsors events that bring the industry to the classroom. Highlights include student internship panels, company information sessions, mock interviews, resume workshops, CPA Becker panels, office tours, and more.
summary: Bridging the gap between the academic and professional worlds, the BU Accounting Association sponsors events that bring the industry to the classroom. Highlights include student internship panels, company information sessions, mock interviews...

name: Alpha Chi Sigma
description:
Alpha Chi Sigma (AXS/ΑΧΣ) is a national co-educational, professional chemistry fraternity dedicated to providing opportunities for study and leadership, as well as forming friendships and connections among students interested in chemistry and related fields. Founded on December 11, 1902, at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, AXS unites collegiate and professional members from a vast range of majors, careers, and professions under the three objects of the fraternity:

 

  1. To bind its members with a tie of true and lasting friendship. 
  2. To strive for the advancement of chemistry both as a science and as a profession.
  3. To aid its members by every honorable means in the attainment of their ambitions as chemists throughout their mortal lives.


Through membership, community service, professional development, and social events, AXS aims to serve as the gateway that brings together students from different backgrounds, interests, and mindsets. If you are interested, please check out our website, Instagram, and Facebook in the contact section below, and keep an eye out for our info sessions at the beginning of each semester. Don’t hesitate to ask us questions at axs@bu.edu!
summary: Alpha Chi Sigma is a national co-educational, professional chemistry fraternity dedicated to providing opportunities for study and leadership, as well as forming friendships and connections among students interested in chemistry and related fields.

name: Panhellenic Council
description:
The Boston University Panhellenic Council is the governing body for the ten sororities chartered on campus. Panhel, with the help of one delegate from each sorority, helps to regulate Greek Life and maintain its growth on campus. We work to provide each member of an affiliated chapter with an opportunity for leadership, scholarship, university involvement, networking and support, and friendship.

Recognized sororities:

- Alpha Delta Pi (ADPi)

- Alpha Epsilon Phi (AEPhi)

- Alpha Phi (APhi)

- Delta Delta Delta (TriDelt)

- Delta Gamma (DG)

- Gamma Phi Beta (GPhiB)

- Kappa Alpha Theta (Theta)

- Kappa Delta (KD)

- Sigma Delta Tau (SigDelt)

- Sigma Kappa (SK)
summary: The Boston University Panhellenic Council is the governing body for the ten sororities chartered on campus. With the help of one delegate from each sorority, Panhel helps to regulate Greek Life and maintain its growth on campus.

name: Guerrilla Marketing Society
description:
Guerrilla Marketing Society (GMS) provides marketing consulting services to local businesses by offering them a marketing campaign plan for their business specifically using guerrilla marketing tactics. Our past partnerships include Expedia, DedCool, Touchland, YiFang, Spotify, and more. The mission of GMS is to contribute to the education of BU students through hands on experience and informative workshops. GMS members are given the opportunity to function as small, non-profit consulting groups for local companies. Students will gain experience in consulting, teamwork, researching, and creating captivating guerrilla marketing strategies.

ALL MAJORS ARE WELCOME. For additional information, please contact us at bugms@bu.edu or visit our website at https://www.bostongms.com/.
summary: Guerrilla Marketing Society (GMS) provides marketing consulting services to local businesses by offering them a marketing campaign plan for their business specifically using guerrilla marketing tactics. Our past partnerships include Expedia, Lady M, th...

name: Consulting Group
description:
The Boston University Consulting Group is a collaborative organization that focuses on career development in the field of consulting for students of all academic backgrounds. We prepare students for a career in consulting through comprehensive workshops, mock interviews, and alumni panels to equip its members with confidence and experience. We also offer pro bono and paid consulting solutions to clients in Boston and its surrounding area. For this area of our club, we bring together engagement teams of dedicated and diverse undergraduates with strong business acumen and a passion for problem-solving. Many of our engagement team members have excelled in internships at renowned firms and contribute these acquired skills to their teams. Our services comprise of strategic recommendations that add value to our clients’ enterprises.

If you are interested in joining our club, sign up for our Mailing List at www.buconsultinggroup.com/mailinglist for more information!
summary: Boston University Consulting Group is a collaborative organization that focuses on career development in the field of consulting for students of all academic backgrounds. We prepare students for a career in consulting through comprehensive training.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
description:
Alpha Kappa Psi is recognized as the premier developer of principled business leaders.

As a professional business fraternity, Alpha Kappa Psi offers an inclusive community with expansive opportunities for networking, mentorship, and professional development.
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is recognized as the premier developer of principled business leaders. As a professional business fraternity, Alpha Kappa Psi offers an inclusive community with opportunities for networking, mentorship, and professional development.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society
description: The Mission of the BMES is to build and support the biomedical engineering community, locally, nationally and internationally, with activities designed to communicate recent advances, discoveries, and inventions; promote education and professional development; and integrate the perspectives of the academic, medical, governmental, and business sectors.
summary: On February 1st, 1968, in Illinois, the Biomedical Engineering Society was created to respond to the growing need for a society in which equal status was given to the fields of biology and engineering.

name: Black Business Student Association
description: Boston University’s Black Business Student Association (BBSA) is an active resource on campus that bridges the opportunity gap for students of color through professional development and educational programming. Our undergraduate members gain a wide breadth of career advice, corporate experience, and a supportive community network of current students, alumni, professionals, and corporate partners. BBSA strives to also educate on the value of diversity and be a strong advocate of diversity and inclusion both within the Questrom School of Business and the greater Boston University Community.
summary: Boston University’s Black Business Student Association (BBSA) is an active resource on campus that bridges the opportunity gap for students of color through professional development and educational programming.

name: herNetwork - Boston University Women in Business
description: herNetwork is the Women in Business club which serves as a networking and mentoring group for motivated and passionate undergraduate BU students. We welcome all genders and majors. With our motto "Women Mean Business" we aim to build a strong and empowering community that invests in ourselves and in one another. Through corporate events, speaker panels, workshops, and our annual Spring Conference we strive to inspire, connect and educate our members in order to help them start a successful career. Please email us at herntwrk@bu.edu to join our mailing list and stay informed of our events and career information. We have many exciting things coming your way! Also, please like our Facebook page herNetwork Boston University and follow our Instagram @hernetworkbu. It will serve as a platform for members to interact with one another and share their thoughts on current issues and our latest events.
summary: herNetwork is an educational and empowering community for all genders and majors. With our motto, Women Mean Business, we aim to build a strong network that invests in ourselves and in one another. Please join us for our weekly events and conference!

name: Geological Society
description:
Boston University Geological Society gives members a holistic view of the earth by sharing the amazing processes and phenomena that occur on our planet, providing students reasons to want to protect it. BUGS encourages people from all backgrounds to attend meetings, excursions, and any other fun events the group organizes to develop this appreciation.

 

In the past, there have been hiking trips, a fundraiser for pollinators, a geode-breaking event, and a vegan taste test, among others. While BUGS can be described as a smaller social group, it is constantly growing in ideas and members. Plans for larger-scale projects are also being created! Joining BUGS is a great way to connect with the Department of Earth and Environment at BU, as well as other students with an interest in geology and sustainability.
summary: Boston University Geological Society (BUGS) is a student-led group of geology and outdoor enthusiasts. BUGS aims to give members an appreciation for the earth and environment while encouraging them to live sustainably.

name: Deaf Studies Club
description: We aim to promote better awareness of the Deaf Community and Deaf Culture by providing students with access to the Deaf Community. Our goal is to allow our members to form bonds within the Deaf Community to better understand the wants and needs. As well, we promote the idea of Deaf as culture, rather than disability. We host exciting events throughout the year, such as dances and performances, that our members as well as members of the Boston Deaf Community can attend!
summary: Aiming to raise awareness about Deaf Culture by providing students with access to education and opportunities within the Deaf Community. Our goal is to create a space for students to practice ASL, build community, and learn about Deaf Culture.

name: Hospitality Association at BU
description:
The goal of the organization is to introduce students to the hospitality industry through networking events, hotel and restaurant tours, and general meetings. The club aims to create a community within the School of Hospitality beyond the classroom. This organization is open to all undergraduate students at Boston University. 

(Formerly the BU American Hotel & Lodging Association Student Chapter)
summary: The goal of the organization is to introduce students to the hospitality industry through networking events, hotel and restaurant tours, and general meetings. The club aims to create a community within the School of Hospitality beyond the classroom.

name: Undergraduate Public Health Association
description: The Undergraduate Public Health Association (UPHA) at Boston University is a student group that: - Provides a community for undergraduates interested in public health from varying backgrounds (Biomedical sciences, International Relations, Anthropology, Pre-med, etc.) to discuss public health issues of interest - Helps undergraduates navigate the wealth of public health-related academic options at BU, from the Geneva internship program at the World Health Organization to the 5-year BS/MPH program. - Hosts public health lectures and discussions, and links students with the numerous public health events in and around Boston - Acts as a liason for students interested in public health to gain hands-on experience with other student groups such as Peer Health Exchange and Project HEALTH and non-profits in Boston
summary: The Undergraduate Public Health Association (UPHA) at Boston University is a student group that:- Provides a community for undergraduates interested in public health from varying backgrounds (Biomedical sciences, International Relations, Anthropology, ...

name: Marine Science Association
description: The Boston University Marine Science Association is a student group committed to learning about different marine science related topics by going on field trips, attending lectures, and exchanging information about personal experiences with marine-related jobs and internships. All who are interested in the ocean and its inhabitants are welcome. Follow us on Instagram: @msaatbu
summary: The Boston University Marine Science Association is a student group committed to learning about different marine science related topics by going on field trips, lectures, and exchanging information about personal marine-related experiences.

name: Undergraduate Psychology Association
description: Students are encouraged to join the Undergraduate Psychology Association, an organization comprised of psychology majors, minors, and other interested students. The association is an independent, student-run organization that sponsors academic, career oriented and social events. to help supplement students’ academic programs.
summary: Students are encouraged to join the Undergraduate Psychology Association, an organization comprised of psychology majors, minors, and other interested students. The association is an independent, student-run organization that sponsors academic, career ...

name: BostonHacks
description: BostonHacks is a collection of students who work towards promoting the hackathon spirit within and outside of BU. Our members are involved in web-, mobile-, and hardware-based development, and come from many different colleges within BU. We cater to students of all skill levels and majors and foster an educational environment where members collaborate and help each other. Our main focus is organizing BU's most prominent annual hackathon in November, where students from all across the country can come to innovate and create projects for 24 hours. Outside the hackathon, we host workshops to help the BU public learn and develop new skills. Sometimes, these workshops will consist of free food and swag!
summary: BostonHacks is a collection of students who organize workshops and hackathons within and outside of BU. We host BU's largest student-run hackathon, BostonHacks. As organizers, we aim to host an event that fosters a meaningful CS community.

name: Debate Society
description: The Boston University Debate Society competes as part of the American Parliamentary Debate Association (APDA) and participates in both American Parliamentary and British Parliamentary debate. Our team welcomes members from all backgrounds, with some members having prior experience with various forms of debate and with others who are completely new to the activity. As part of BUDS, you'll have the opportunity to attend tournaments at other colleges and universities as frequently as you like, with options offered most weekends of the academic year.
summary: The Boston University Debate Society competes as part of the American Parliamentary Debate Association (APDA) and participates in both American Parliamentary and British Parliamentary debate.

name: Student Government
description: The Boston University Student Government (BUSG) is your representative governing body at Boston University. USG's primary goal is listening to you and accomplishing or advocating for the changes you want to see on our campus.
summary: The Boston University Student Government is your representative governing body at Boston University. The Student Government's primary goal is listening to you and accomplishing or advocating for the changes you want to see on our campus.

name: Russian Speaking Society
description: Welcome to the Russian Speaking Society! We are a student-run organization, which aims to bring together people of Russian speaking backgrounds with those interested in Russian language and culture, and the rest of the BU community. We organize interactive events to bring people together interested in the Russian/Soviet culture. (Delicious Russian food is always present.)
summary: Welcome to the Russian Speaking Society!!!We are a student-run organization, which aims to bring together people of Russian speaking backgrounds with those interested in Russian language and culture, and the rest of the BU community. We organize intera...

name: Vibes
description: Vibes, founded in 2000, originated as an all-female hip-hop dance crew at Boston University. Comprising vibrant performers from diverse dance backgrounds, Vibes is dedicated to expanding the art of dance and hip-hop culture within the Boston community through their original choreography and captivating stage presence. While the team takes pride in its roots of female empowerment, Vibes continually seeks to redefine and explore femininity through gender-inclusivity.
summary: Vibes, founded in 2000, originated as an all-female hip-hop dance group at Boston University. Comprised of vibrant performers with diverse dance backgrounds, the dancers strive to take urban dance to a higher level through their original choreography.

name: National Student Speech Language Hearing Association
description: NSSLHA (we pronounce it "NISS-LA") is the student branch of the American Speech Language and Hearing Association (ASHA). To join the national organization, email Doctor Jennifer Bentley at bentley1@bu.edu and she will forward your information to the e-board! All undergraduate students interested in Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology are encouraged to play an active role in the BU NSSLHA chapter by attending meetings and volunteering for fundraising and other activities. Speech Language and Hearing Sciences minors and other students interested in the field are welcome.
summary: BU NSSLHA is BU’s chapter of the National Student Speech Language Hearing Association. Open to all interested in speech, language, and hearing sciences. Join by emailing Dr. Jennifer Bentley (bentley1@bu.edu) to be connected with the e-board.

name: Puerto Rican Student Association
description: The Puerto Rican Student Association welcomes every Boston University student interested in getting involved with the Puerto Rican culture. Our mission is to foster a sense of community among students at BU who grew up with Puerto Rican heritage, whether they are first, second, or third generation. Through events within the Boston University community and the greater Boston area, our members will strengthen and foster new ties with the Puerto Rican culture and traditions. This association will serve as a hub for members to find common ground with other students with similar experiences and/or upbringing. Through meetings and organized social events, members will learn what it means to be Puerto Rican on the island, in the United States, and in Boston University. This club strives to create a fun and welcoming environment by bringing a little piece of that Puerto Rican flavor to BU’s campus and the greater Boston area.
summary: The Puerto Rican Student Association welcomes every Boston University student interested in getting involved with the Puerto Rican culture. Our mission is to foster a sense of community among students at BU who grew up with Puerto Rican heritage, wheth...

name: Verge
description:
Verge is a performing and training dance company, concentrating in Chinese dance and ballet training. Our repertoire spans classical, ethnic, and contemporary Chinese dance. We meet once a week, no auditions required. Just come and dance!

Our rehearsals typically include a warmup session with ballet training and a Chinese dance combo. We perform in the Boston area throughout the year and hold an annual showcase in April.

For more information, please visit our Instagram (@buvergedance), Facebook (/VergeDanceCompany), and Youtube channel. For performance inquiries, please contact vergedancecompany@gmail.com
summary: Verge is a performing and training dance company, concentrating in Chinese dance and ballet training. Our repertoire spans classical, ethnic, and contemporary Chinese dance. We meet once a week, no auditions required. Just come and dance!

name: American Cancer Society on Campus
description: ACS On Campus is a nationwide collaboration of college students, faculty, and staff dedicated to eliminating cancer through a variety of events. In collaboration with the American Cancer Society, we promote awareness and education in the BU community about cancer-related topics, recognize and honor survivorship in Boston, fundraise for ACS so that the money can go towards patient care and research, and advocate to make cancer a top priority in legislation, on-campus, as well as at state and federal levels. Furthermore, we plan the annual Relay for Life event (hundreds of participants!) for a night of celebration, remembrance, and call to action in all aspects of cancer. Join us in the fight!
summary: American Cancer Society On Campus (ACS On Campus) is a nationwide collaboration of students, faculty, and staff dedicated to eliminating cancer. We fundraise, celebrate, and support those who are affected, as well as advocate for awareness.

name: Taiwanese American Student Association
description:
The Boston University Taiwanese American Student Association (BUTASA) is dedicated to promoting and sharing Taiwanese culture with the school community and beyond. We welcome everyone to join our organization to learn more about Taiwan's history, culture, politics, and language through social, cultural, and educational events. We hope that these events will allow others to learn and gain a better understanding of the Taiwanese American identity.

Click here to sign up for our mailing list
summary: BUTASA, Boston University Taiwanese American Student Association, is dedicated to promoting and sharing Taiwanese culture with the school community and beyond. We welcome everyone to join our organization...

name: Helping Hands for the Homeless
description: Helping Hands for the Homeless aims to spread awareness about homelessness in Boston and provide necessary resources to the homeless in the Boston area. Members of this organization have volunteered at local shelters and soup kitchens such as St. Francis House, Pine Street Inn, The New England Center and Home for Veterans, Rosie's Place, Boston Rescue Mission and Sunday's Bread, helping to prepare and serve meals to those living there. We have also worked with several organizations to fund-raise and to collect supplies that will be distributed to those who need them most.
summary: Helping Hands for the Homeless aims to spread awareness about homelessness in Boston and provide necessary resources to the homeless in the Boston area. Members of this organization have volunteered at local shelters and soup kitchens across Boston.

name: Global Medical Brigades
description: Global Medical Brigades is a secular, international, student-operated volunteer organization whose mission is to provide sustained health care to underserved communities throughout the world. Global Medical Brigades is a part of the larger organization known as Global Brigades, which is the world's largest student-led global health and development initiative. Our BU chapter recruits student volunteers and medical professionals, gathers medicine and other supplies in order to lead a week-long brigade. Partnering with GMB and local healthcare professionals, we will be working to provide medical consults, medications, public health education, dental and optometry services, and more to hundreds of community members who otherwise have limited healthcare options. This is important hands-on work that highlights our organization’s commitment to global health equity. Our brigades are essential to fulfill our mission of providing sustained health care to underserved communities throughout the world.
summary: Global Medical Brigades is a secular, international, student-operated volunteer organization whose mission is to provide sustained health care to underserved communities throughout the world. GMB is a part of the larger organization Global Brigades.

name: Singapore Students Association
description: We are a cultural organization dedicated to promoting Singaporean culture in BU. We aim to connect people from all walks of life through our food and events! Also, we hope to make SSA a home away from home for people who identify with Singapore.
summary: We are a cultural organization dedicated to promoting Singaporean culture in BU. We aim to connect people from all walks of life through our food and events! Also, we hope to make SSA a home away from home for people who identify with Singapore.

name: Undergraduate Women in Science and Engineering
description:
Undergraduate Women in Science and Engineering (uWISE) at Boston University strives to connect and support women & non-binary individuals in STEM through workshops, mentoring relationships, social events, and community outreach. uWISE is open to all undergraduate students that support women in STEM fields and can be contacted via our Email: uwise@bu.edu, Website: https://www.buuwise.com/, or Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/uwisebu/.

** Please email us to be added to the email list and follow us on Instagram to stay up to date with our events!
summary: Undergraduate Women in Science and Engineering (uWISE) is an interdisciplinary, student-focused group providing community and a support network for all those who support women in the STEM fields through community outreach, mentoring, and social event

name: TAMID Group
description: TAMID Consulting & Investment Group is a nonprofit organization that develops the professional skills of students through hands-on interactions with the Israeli economy. TAMID integrates the next generation of entrepreneurs and business professionals with Israel through a comprehensive education curriculum, pro-bono consulting for Israeli startups, capital market investment research, and a summer internship program in Israel.
summary: TAMID Consulting & Investment Group at Boston University is an apolitical student-led organization that develops and fosters the future economic relationship between American business minded students and the Israeli economy.

name: Marketing Club
description:
Boston University Marketing Club is committed to providing an opportunity for the BU community to explore all facets of marketing.

Members of BUMKC gain experience by functioning in one of of our four committees: social media, content, client accounts, or case studies. Within each of these committees our members are driven to explore, diversify their knowledge, and grow.

We are Boston University Marketing Club. Discover your brand today.
summary: Boston University Marketing Club is committed to providing an opportunity for the BU community to explore all facets of marketing. Our members are driven to explore, diversify their knowledge, and grow within our four committees.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics & Astronautics
description:
The Boston University branch of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics is a professional society dedicated to supporting BU engineers in finding lifelong resources and opportunities in the field.

What We Do:

1. Networking Opportunities

2. Conferences & Events

3. Professional Development

4. Scholarships

5. Technical Design Competitions and more

Please visit our website for a more in depth description
summary: The Boston University branch of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics is a professional society dedicated to supporting BU engineers in finding lifelong resources and opportunities in the field.

name: Tau Beta Pi Association
description: Tau Beta Pi was founded in 1885 to mark, in a fitting manner, those who have conferred honor upon their alma mater by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as undergraduates in the field of engineering, or by their attainments as alumni in the field of engineering, and to foster a spirit of liberal culture in the engineering colleges. The BU Massachusetts Eta Chapter strives to embody the principles of the Societal Engineer and uphold the honor of the Tau Beta Pi Association.
summary: Tau Beta Pi was founded in 1885 to mark in a fitting manner those who have conferred honor upon their alma mater by distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as undergraduates in the field of engineering, or by their attainments as alumni in th...

name: West Campus Hall Council
description: The West Campus RHA serves to provide the residence of West Campus with various events and activities which serve to foster bonds between residents and make life in West Campus more enjoyable. Our motto is: West: Where Everyone Shines Together.
summary: The West Campus RHA serves to provide the residence of West Campus with various events and activities which serve to foster bonds between residents and make life in West Campus more enjoyable. Our motto is: West: Where Everyone Shines Together.

name: Stage Troupe
description:
Stage Troupe is Boston University’s oldest and largest extracurricular performing arts group for non-theatre majors. For over seventy years, Stage Troupe has been a leader among student-run campus organizations, providing the Boston University community with high-quality shows.

 

Stage Troupe is managed by an executive board of six elected undergraduates and overseen by a student group advisor. Each year, we present six mainstage shows, including the Family & Friends Weekend Show and Theatre in Action in the spring. Every production is selected through popular vote by the membership. General meetings are held monthly with the entire membership. Stage Troupe also offers a variety of special projects to foster new work and creative alternatives to mainstage shows. Every year we produce a one-act festival as well as 24-hour theatre weekends in addition to many other events. Stage Troupe’s special projects aim to cultivate fresh, new ideas and respond to the desires of the membership.

 

We are committed to our motto of “learning by doing,” so prior experience is not necessary to join Stage Troupe. If you have an interest in any aspect of theatre—acting, teching, writing, directing, or producing—you can do it in Troupe! Our group continues to grow and we look forward to the countless opportunities to make Stage Troupe the leading performing arts group on campus.

 

Check out our website and social media (linked below) to see more and find out what we're up to this summer!
summary: Stage Troupe is Boston University’s oldest and largest extracurricular performing arts group for non-theatre majors. With 3 mainstage productions per semester, an annual summer show, and up to 10 special projects a year, there is involvement for anyone!

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: ASME is a not-for-profit membership organization that enables collaboration, knowledge sharing, career enrichment, and skills development across all engineering disciplines, toward a goal of helping the global engineering community develop solutions to benefit lives and livelihoods. Founded in 1880 by a small group of leading industrialists, ASME has grown through the decades to include more than 130,000 members in 158 countries. Thirty-thousand of these members are students. From college students and early-career engineers to project managers, corporate executives, researchers and academic leaders, ASME's members are as diverse as the engineering community itself. ASME serves this wide-ranging technical community through quality programs in continuing education, training and professional development, codes and standards, research, conferences and publications, government relations and other forms of outreach. The Boston University chapter of ASME hosts information sessions and networking events for career advice and insight into academia and graduate school. We also host the yearly FE Exam preparation course enabling students interested in taking the FE exam to have the problems explained by ME faculty. To contact ASME@BU: asme@bu.edu
summary: ASME is a not-for-profit membership organization that enables collaboration, knowledge sharing, career enrichment, and skills development across all engineering disciplines, toward a goal of helping the global engineering community

name: Alpha Delta Pi
description: Welcome to the Rho chapter of Alpha Delta Pi! We have a strong sisterhood and a history of excellence. We are a diverse group of women who make a difference in each other's lives, in the community, and on campus. Alpha Delta Pi was founded in 1851, the first ever secret society for women. Rho chapter of Alpha Delta Pi came to Boston University in 1911 and has a long tradition of sisterhood, with our sisters coming from a variety of backgrounds. With a strong commitment to academic excellence, Alpha Delta Pi has held one of the top GPA averages among all sororities for many semesters. Sisters have a variety of academic interests, ensuring there is always a sister who can help you succeed. Alpha Delta Pi also holds two large philanthropy events each year -- Friday Night Lights, a flag-football tournament in the Fall, and a unique event each Spring. Each year, we raise thousands of dollars for our national philanthropy, Ronald McDonald House Charities. We are lucky enough to be able to directly serve the Ronald McDonald House located in Boston Harbor, which is a facility specifically for families whose children are receiving treatment in Boston. Our commitment to our sorority and to each other is strong. Please explore our website and social media platforms to learn more about Alpha Delta Pi!
summary: Welcome to the Rho chapter of Alpha Delta Pi! We have a strong sisterhood and a history of excellence. We are a diverse group of women who make a difference in each other's lives, in the community, and on campus. Alpha Delta Pi was founded in 1851, the..

name: Omega Phi Alpha
description: Omega Phi Alpha, Boston University's only community service sorority, is focused on our principles of friendship, leadership, and service. Our service is dedicated to the university community, the community at large, the members of the sorority, and the nations of the world. We also complete service every year in dedication to our permanent project of mental health. Through the completion of service, the sisters of Omega Phi Alpha hope to become service-minded leaders that have the confidence, drive, and the perspective to change the world.
summary: Omega Phi Alpha, Boston University's only community service sorority, is focused on our principles of friendship, leadership, and service.

name: Pre Dental Society
description:
The BU Pre-Dental Society serves Boston University students who wish to  pursue a career in dentistry. We are committed to helping our members gain admission into dental school and to expose them to the profession. We provide students with shadowing, volunteering, and application preparation resources. Our panels with dental students, dental school administrators, and dentists give insight into dental school and the field of dentistry. Our society is also a great way to meet fellow students with similar interests and to share information and experiences. If you are a pre-dental student or are considering a career in dentistry, feel free to join our organization, email us to join our email list, and attend our meetings. Please visit our Instagram and Discord pages for more information!

Email: predent@bu.edu
summary: The BU Pre-Dental Society serves students who are interested in dentistry by providing guidance, volunteering, and application preparation resources. Please join our email list and visit our Engage and social media pages!

name: International Affairs Association
description:
The Boston University International Affairs Association is an academic and professional society focused on bringing a global perspective to the social sciences. We have 5 branches and other committees focused on international affairs: our published undergraduate journal, the International Relations Review; our UNA-USA branch, Students for the UN; Boston University's Model United Nations Team, MUN@BU (ranked 4th in the World!); our collegiate MUN conference BARMUN; and our high school MUN conference BOSMUN! Through these branches, we encourage and promote diplomacy, dialogue, and the understanding of different cultures. As the largest student group on campus and one of the most active, we provide unparalleled opportunities to get involved at BU and beyond.

 

How to Get Involved: Explore our main website at www.buiaa.org, or Instagram @bu_iaa. For more instantaneous updates, join our Slack and sign up for the mailing list to receive weekly newsletters. Come to our open meetings for SUN and MUN@BU (Every Tuesday) and check our website for the time and room that we will be in for the semester! Sign up to staff our conferences at barmun.org and bosmun.org. Of course, you can always email us and ask any question you may have at iaa@bu.edu.
summary: The BU International Affairs Association is an academic & professional society that brings a global perspective to the social sciences. We host 5 branches: an international affairs journal, a top-ranked MUN team, two MUN conferences, and a service group.

name: Thai Student Association
description: Thai Student Association at Boston University is a cultural organization that aims to promote Thai cultures in various aspects - Thai traditional dance, Thai cuisine, Thai sports, and the importance of various traditions in Thailand. We are open to the entire BU community who are interested in our organization.
summary: Thai Student Association at Boston University is a cultural organization which aims to promote Thai cultures in various aspects - Thai traditional dance, Thai cuisine, Thai sports, and the importance of various traditions in Thailand.

name: FeelGood World
description:
We are the Boston University chapter of the FeelGood youth movement, a collective of student-run social enterprises that raise money to alleviate world hunger & global poverty, one grilled cheese at a time.

          

Volunteering can be one-time, low-commitment, but if you vibe with us, consider joining our general meetings! All proceeds are donated to The Hunger Project, our parent organization, to be invested in high-need communities across the globe.

        

FeelGood BU represents an opportunity for students to develop entrepreneurial skills, gain volunteer hours, and have fun in a down-to-earth, extremely cool & funny community. 

          

Join our Discord here!
summary: FeelGood BU is a club that raises money to end global hunger, one grilled cheese at a time. Volunteers prepare and sell sandwiches to BU students, with proceeds donated to The Hunger Project. Join for volunteer hours!

name: Callbacks
description: The Callbacks are BU's first and only comedy group that performs original sketch comedy, improvisational comedy, and produces video shorts. We have performed in Metcalf Auditorium, BU Central, Morse auditorium, and across the greater Boston area at Tufts University, Boston College, Brandeis University, and more! The Callbacks also host an annual collegiate sketch comedy festival at BU known as Funderdome! Visit our wesbite at thecallbacks.org and follow us on social media to learn more!
summary: The Callbacks are the only comedy group on campus that performs original sketch comedy, improvisational comedy, and produces video shorts. We are not just sketch. We are not just improv. We're just funny!

name: Mathematics Association at BU
description: The Mathematics Association at BU aims to create a close-knit community among all students interested in mathematics, statistics, and mathematical sciences. MABU dedicated to mathematics accessible at the undergraduate level. MABU aspires to offer a variety of academic, professional, and social events catering to the interests of BU undergraduate mathematics & statistics community.
summary: The Mathematics Association at BU aims to create a close-knit community among all students interested in mathematics, statistics, and mathematical sciences.

name: Mind and Brain Society
description: The Mind and Brain Society (MBS) aims to support an eager undergraduate community with conversations and resources that are fundamental to Neuroscience and Psychology today. Throughout the academic year, MBS hosts lectures, research presentations, educational outreach programs, and social events. The MBS divisions — the Nerve and Synaptic Connection — give members the opportunity to publish their own works, receive mentorship from upperclassmen in the Neuroscience, Neurobiology, and Psychology departments, and gain peer-mentorship experience. More information about our initiatives can be found on our website!
summary: MBS aims to inspire people of all academic disciplines to engage in conversations about humanity by using neuroscience as common ground. We host lectures, student-led discussions, social events, and volunteer opportunities throughout the year.

name: IEEE Student Chapter/IEEE-HKN
description:
BU IEEE Student Chapter and IEEE-HKN Kappa Sigma Chapter

At Boston University, the IEEE Student Chapter and the IEEE-HKN Kappa Sigma Chapter merge to form a vibrant community known as BU IEEE, under the guidance of Professor Min-Chang Lee. Our association, BU IEEE, is deeply integrated with the IEEE Boston Section, benefiting from Professor Lee's leadership roles within various IEEE societies and committees. Originating from the establishment of BU HKN by Professor Lee in 2001, our IEEE-HKN chapter evolved and was officially reinstated as the Kappa Sigma Chapter in 2016, marking a significant milestone in our history. Together, we are officially recognized and supported by Boston University's Student Activities Office and the ECE Department, enabling us to offer a range of enriching activities including lectures, workshops, and social events, specifically tailored for ECE students from Freshmen to Seniors.

 

BU IEEE Membership and Activities

Membership to the BU IEEE Student Chapter is open to all ECE students, offering a platform for academic and professional development through a diverse range of activities. Our engagements extend beyond campus, connecting members with alumni and industry professionals to foster networking and mentorship opportunities. The IEEE-HKN Honor Society, an elite segment of our chapter, distinguishes itself by inviting students who exhibit exceptional academic achievement and embody the IEEE-HKN ideals of Scholarship, Character, and Attitude. Membership to IEEE-HKN is a prestigious honor, recognizing students who not only excel academically but also contribute positively to the community and the field of electrical and electronics engineering.

 

Engagement and Induction

Throughout the year, our chapter collaborates with the department to identify and invite students who meet the rigorous IEEE-HKN criteria for membership. Candidates undergo a period of engagement with the chapter, allowing us to assess their commitment to the core principles of IEEE-HKN. The culmination of this process is our annual induction ceremony, where selected candidates are formally welcomed into the IEEE-HKN Honor Society. This induction not only symbolizes academic excellence but also opens doors to exclusive IEEE-HKN events and networking opportunities, both locally and globally.

 

Connect With Us

For more information about the IEEE Student Chapter or the IEEE-HKN Honor Society at Boston University, please reach out to us at ieee@bu.edu. We are committed to fostering a supportive and dynamic community for students passionate about electrical and electronics engineering and related fields.
summary: IEEE is an open interdisciplinary community center around developing our technical skills and interpersonal relationships through workshops, social events and invited talks. The ECE inducts Juniors and Seniors each year into the honor society on merit.

name: Pre-Medical Society
description: The goal of the Boston University Pre-Medical Society is to support aspiring physicians throughout their undergraduate careers by providing resources and opportunities. We strive to create an inclusive environment where pre-med students feel comfortable asking questions and gaining support from their peers through mentorship. We organize hands-on events, mentorship programs, physician speaker events from various specialties, volunteer events, and more!
summary: The Pre-Medical Society supports aspiring physicians throughout their undergraduate careers by providing resources and opportunities. We strive to create an inclusive environment and provide mentorship, hands-on events, and guest speakers.

name: AdClub
description: Boston University's professional quality, student-driven advertising agency. We blend strategic insights, creative risks, and fresh perspectives to craft bold campaigns for our broad range of clients. Our agency fosters career development through hands-on learning and mentorship, preparing students to excel in advertising and beyond. Open to all, regardless of experience or major, we create real work with real results. Interested in joining? Check us out on our socials or email us today!
summary: Explore the industry or expand your portfolio as a member. We are a place to meet creative peers and be inspired by their capabilities. As a client, get professional work with a dedicated team. Reach out to become a client or learn how to sign up.
