summary: Delta Sigma Pi was founded in 1907 and is America's foremost professional business fraternity. Since its founding, it has become the world's largest business fraternity with more than 230,000 members, and more than 200 active collegiate chapters and co...

name: alpha Kappa Delta Phi
description: alpha Kappa Delta Phi was established at the University of California, Berkeley in the fall of 1989. Four headstrong visionaries: Susan Kim, Fannie Pon, Jill Yoshimura and Reina Yuan, initiated the concept of having an organization that would provide them as well as other young college women the opportunity to experience sisterhood, and to promote what they felt was integral to the development of successful women. After proposing their ideas to women at the Berkeley campus, they along with ten and strong and willful fellow women became the founding sisters of alpha Kappa Delta Phi. Together as the charter class, they dedicated themselves to establishing a strong and lasting organization that would foster not only sisterhood, but scholarship, service, leadership and Asian American awareness as well. The founding sisters: Betty Chu, Karin Co, Susan Kim, Nancy Lee, Sherri Leung, Annie Loo, Belinda Ma, Anita Ng, Serene Ngin, Fannie Pon, Josie Sun, Daisy Wu, Jill Yoshimura and Reina Yuan, came together to share one another's goals for a promising union. These ideals were to later set the foundation and purpose for alpha Kappa Delta Phi. The love and generosity that each member had for the organization was instrumental to the growth of alpha Kappa Delta Phi's reputation as a sorority, as well as to the forging of unbreakable friendships. In its first step towards achieving widespread acknowledgement, alpha Kappa Delta Phi was recognized by the College Panhellenic Association of Berkeley, California on February 7, 1990, which date is celebrated annually as National Founders' Day. The sisterhood has since then spread to prominent university campuses around the nation. To meet the needs of the growing sisterhood, the National Board of alpha Kappa Delta Phi was established in May of 1993 at the National Convention in Santa Barbara, California. It is the sincere wish of the founding sisters that alpha Kappa Delta Phi continue to promote the ideals on which the organization was founded and to provide each woman with the resources, unique sisterly support and understanding to achieve her goals in life. Our mission is to provide sisterhood among women, scholarship, leadership and Asian-awareness in the University and community. Further, also to provide a support group for those in their times of need and to encourage the making of successful women. To accomplish our mission, we will: Create a climate that encourages teamwork, creativity and innovation, and above all, Give our unmoving and absolute support and encouragement to those we hold dear to our hearts.
summary: alpha Kappa Delta Phi was established at the University of California, Berkeley in the fall of 1989. Four headstrong visionaries: Susan Kim, Fannie Pon, Jill Yoshimura and Reina Yuan, initiated the concept of having an organization that would provide t...

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description: Boston University’s Vietnamese Student Association (BUVSA) is a student organization that provides an opportunity for exploring, sharing, and preserving Vietnamese history, culture, and experiences. It also promotes friendship and awareness both among club members and within the Boston University campus. The Association works to advocate unity among the Vietnamese community as well as connecting ourselves with other communities as a whole. We aim to reflect this goal through cultural, educational, and social events.
summary: Boston University’s Vietnamese Student Association (BUVSA) is a student organization that provides an opportunity for exploring, sharing, and preserving Vietnamese history, culture, and experiences. It also promotes friendship and awareness both among ...

name: Alpha Epsilon Phi
description: Alpha Epsilon Phi is a national sorority, founded in 1909 by seven women, dedicated to helping women become the best they can be. We prize individuality, encouraging each member to discover and develop the talents and abilities that make her unique.But just as we value individuality, we also prize togetherness, fostering friendships and a sense of belonging that last a lifetime. When you're an AEΦ, you'll find there's always a sister to laugh when you laugh, to listen when you talk, to lend a hand and be there for you. Mission Statement:The mission of Alpha Epsilon Phi Sorority is to inspire and support exemplary women dedicated to friendship and a lifelong commitment to Alpha Epsilon Phi while building on the vision of our founders.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Phi is a national sorority, founded in 1909 by seven women, dedicated to helping women become the best they can be. We prize individuality, encouraging each member to discover and develop the talents and abilities that make her unique.

name: Engineers Without Borders
description: Established in 2005 at Boston University, Engineers Without Borders (EWB) is a collegiate chapter directly affiliated with the national chapter of EWB USA. EWB USA is a non-profit humanitarian organization that was established to partner with developing communities worldwide in order to improve their quality of life. This partnership involves the implementation of sustainable engineering projects, while involving and training internationally responsible engineers and engineering students. Our current work is with the Ogiek people of Tinet, Kenya, and we have past experience in Chirimoto, Peru, and Naluja, Zambia. We are a group of students from various majors who collaborate worldwide with communities and local partners to design and implement sustainable engineering projects with the aim to create transformative experiences and responsible leaders. Our current projects focus on bringing clean and sustainable water sources -- such as rainwater catchment systems and borehole wells -- to the elementary schools of the region. Explore our website to get involved: https://www.ewbbu.org/
summary: Engineers Without Borders - BU is a collegiate chapter directly affiliated with the national chapter of Engineers Without Borders USA. We work with communities around the world to design and implement sustainable engineering projects.

name: Umoja
description: The purpose of UMOJA is to foster a healthy and supportive environment for Black students and all individuals interested in Black culture. We are committed to uniting students, staff, and alumni through meaningful educational, social, and community outreach programming. UMOJA also strives to provide honest, transparent, and responsive leadership that is adaptive to the evolving needs of our general body. In the coming year, we aim to strengthen our presence as both a resource and a safe space for the student community.
summary: The purpose of Umoja is to create a healthy environment, for black students and all individuals who are interested in black culture, which actively united students, staff and alumni by providing pertinent educational, social, and community outreach pro...

name: Liquid Fun
description:
Liquid Fun is Boston University's short and long form improv comedy troupe. Since our unofficial founding in 1998, we have gone on to win countless tournaments and awards including the 2018-2019 New England Regional College Improve Championship! We perform free shows each month on BU's campus. But we don't stop there. 

We have performed at nearly every college in Massachusetts, Bar/Bat Mitzvahs, comedy theaters, fundraisers and more. Every year we host a 24-hour comedy marathon to raise money for the Great Boston Area Food Bank. 

"Audition" is not a part of the LF vocabulary. We hold open practices, year-round, on Sunday nights from 7-9 PM in BU College of Arts and Sciences B36. No experience necessary! Our open practices include teaching and performing improv. Variety is our middle name despite what you may have heard (“Felix” is just a vicious rumor ). 

It's free. It's fun... it's liquid?
summary: Liquid Fun is BU's premier improv comedy troupe. We perform both long and short form improv and host free shows once a month on campus. We also host open practices every Sunday, where anyone and everyone - regardless of experience - is welcome!

name: Pre-Law Society
description: The BU Pre-Law Society offers a myriad of events for undergraduates who are planning to apply to law school or may be interested in a legal career. Some of our events include hosting visiting law schools, LSAT practice exams and strategy sessions, panels featuring current law students and practicing attorneys, and social events where students can chat informally with law school admissions officers. The PLS works closely with the Pre-Professional Advising Office at Boston University and the Boston University School of Law in order to provide resources to all BU Pre-Law students.
summary: The BU Pre-Law Society offers a myriad of events for undergraduates who are planning to apply to law school or may be interested in a legal career. Some of our events include hosting visiting law schools, LSAT practice exams/strategy sessions, and panels

name: Business & Technology Club
description:
The Boston University BizTech Club aims to:

- Target students who think critically, problem-solve, and strive to understand and utilize technology on an ongoing basis.

- Educate and further develop an understanding of career paths associated with a degree in various fields including but not limited to: Business, Engineering, Economics, Data Science, and more!

- Connecting companies and other organizations with project, internship and job inquiries to talented and driven students.

- Fostering a strong connection with students interested in pursuing a career in tech as well as building strong relationships with technology focused faculty and alumni.
summary: The BU BizTech Club aims to target students who think critically, problem-solve, and strive to understand and utilize technology on an ongoing basis. We aim to educate and develop an understanding of career paths associated with a degree in MIS.

name: Pre-Law Review
description: The Boston University Pre-Law Review is a student-run law journal, open to all Boston University undergraduates. Founded in 1991, the Pre-Law Review has continued to foster awareness and provide commentary on major issues facing the law and the legal profession through its publication. The PLR is published once per semester and features a wide range of topics, as our authors are given the freedom to write about anything as long as it pertains to the law. All of our contributors and staff are undergraduates, many of whom will be applying to law school. However, the PLR staff spans a variety of majors, schools, and class years, as no specific academic or experiential requirements are necessary to work on the PLR. All that is expected of members is an interest in learning more about the law and a commitment to furthering the publication
summary: The Boston University Pre-Law Review is a student-run law journal, open to all Boston University undergraduates.  Founded in 1991, the Pre-Law Review has continued to foster awareness and provide commentary on major issues facing the law and the legal ...

name: Undergraduate Classics Association
description:
This group serves to connect everyone who loves the culture of ancient Greece and Rome! We discuss mythology, history, and all subjects in the field of Classics.

We meet Thursdays 5:00 pm - 6:30 pm STH409

No need to stay the whole time, feel free to pop in and out! Free snacks & tea provided!

Instagram: uca_bu
summary: This group serves to connect students and professors who are involved in Classical Studies, or who just happen to love studying the ancient world!

name: Delta Delta Delta
description: Delta Delta Delta was founded in 1888 at Boston University with the goal to “found a society that shall be kind alike to all and think more of a girl’s inner self and character than of her personal appearance.” As the first chapter of a now internationally respected Panhellenic women’s organization, the Alpha Chapter at Boston University strives to uphold the sorority’s fundamental values of kindness and steadfastly loving one another. Tri Delta gives collegiate women the opportunity to create their own community at BU and forge perpetual bonds of genuine friendship that last far past graduation.
summary: Delta Delta Delta was founded in 1888 at Boston University with the open motto, "let us steadfastly love one another." The Alpha Chapter at Boston University aims to uphold the sorority’s fundamental values of truth, self-sacrifice, and friendship.

name: Minority Association of Pre-Medical/Health Students
description: The mission of the Minority Association of Pre-Medical/Health Students (MAPS) is to address the needs of the minority Pre-Health students at Boston University. MAPS intends to motivate, encourage, and prepare Pre-Health students from a disadvantaged background, an underrepresented minority group, or a medically underserved area to be competitive applicants for health professional schools through a sharing of knowledge, experience, and mentorship. The chapter is dedicated to serving underserved communities through community service activities and volunteering and to improving minority matriculation into all professional health-related programs.
summary: BU MAPS aims to support, encourage, and prepare Minority Pre-health students to be competitive applicants for health professional schools through professional development, career exploration panels, clinical/volunteering experience, and mentorship.

name: Anime Club
description: The Anime Club is a social group in which both anime enthusiasts and dabblers in the Boston University community can come watch, discuss, and learn more about anime. Our main offering is providing weekly anime programming open to the BU community. We gather in CAS B12 at 6:30PM on most Fridays. We also offer on-campus community events and off-campus group excursions throughout the year. We also have a large library of Blu-rays, light novels, and manga which members can rent throughout the semester.  Join our Discord and say hi: https://discord.gg/8ysYsdFV
summary: The Anime Club is a social group in which both anime enthusiasts and dabblers in the Boston University community can come watch, discuss, and learn more about anime. We provide weekly programming open to the BU community as well as group excursions.

name: Art History Association
description: The Art History Association is an undergraduate student-run organization. We are open to anyone, regardless of your major or knowledge of art history. Throughout the year, we have some great activities, such as museum visits, themed discussions, and guest speakers. We hold career advising sessions to help those interested in an art-related career decide what to do and how to do it. Through regular e-mails, we keep members up-to-date on art-related events around the city, such as gallery openings, new museum exhibitions, lectures, and more. We also work closely with the Graduate Student Art History Association (GSHAA), CFA Student Government, and the Boston University Art Gallery to keep members informed of these great organizations
summary: The Art History Association is an undergraduate student-run organization. We are open to anyone, regardless of your major or knowledge of art history. Throughout the year, we organize museum visits, networking, and social events.

name: Undergraduate Economics Association
description: The Boston University Undergraduate Economics Association (UEA) is the leading economics student organization sponsored by the Economics Department at BU. Our mission is to build a vibrant economics community on campus by offering academic, social, and professional support to all undergraduates interested in economics. We achieve this through hosting discussions with faculty and industry experts, social events, alumni panels, professional development workshops, and more.
summary: Welcome to the Boston University Undergraduate Economics Association! The UEA will offer you the opportunity to gain insight into economic issues and develop the skills that are needed to succeed in economics-related fields.

name: Red Cross Volunteers
description: The American Red Cross is a national, nonprofit organization aimed at the betterment of American life. It participates in disaster services, blood services, citizenship classes, health and safety training, and an assortment of other areas. The American Red Cross aims at educating the public to prevent and assist in disasters. Its volunteers work around the clock to make this mission a success. It does this through services that are consistent with its congressional charter and the principles of the International Red Cross Movement. In addition, BU Red Cross Volunteers will develop and offer services that address critical human, health, and safety needs of not only the BU community, but of the entire greater Boston area!
summary: The American Red Cross is a national, nonprofit organization aimed at the betterment of American life. It participates in disaster services, blood services, citizenship classes, health and safety training, and an assortment of other areas. The American...

name: Alianza Latina
description: Alianza Latina is Boston University's largest Latinx student organization and the Latine Student Union. We are dedicated to providing BU students with programming that fosters community and encourages students to learn more about Latinx culture and identity, regardless of their own race or ethnicity. We implement weekly cultural, social, and professional meetings as well as community service events both on and off campus.
summary: Alianza Latina is Boston University's largest Latinx student organization and the Latine Student Union. We are dedicated to providing students with programming that fosters community and encourages students to learn more about Latinx culture and identity

name: Slippery When Wet
description: Slippery When Wet, lovingly referred to as 'Slipps', is Boston University's best example of teamwork. The group is made up of a rotating cast of Boston University undergraduates that write and perform original sketch comedy shows each semester. Slipps have written and performed dozens of sold-out shows on BU's campus, including such favorites as , "A Rift In Time: Here Comes Rashmi!", "How to Kiss" and this semester's knockout hit, "Scared of Science", an exploration through the fear of the known. We have hosted tens upon tens of events, including comedy shows, dance marathons, and a cappella festivals. We've also performed at sketch comedy festivals around the country, including: University of Pennsylvania Comfest Skidmore National College Comedy Festival Chicago Sketch Comedy Festival Brandeis Sketch Comedy Festival Sketchfest NYC Best of the Best Sketchfest (Portland, OR) Tufts Laughs of Love Comedy Festival We have also been recognized by professional sketch comedy groups, including Pangea 3000, Two Fun Men, Harvard Sailing Team, and The Whitest Kids U Know. Also, some of us are single.
summary: Slippery When Wet, lovingly referred to as 'Slipps', is Boston University's best example of teamwork. The group is made up of a rotating cast of Boston University undergraduates that write and perform original sketch comedy shows each semester.

name: Stand Up Club
description: BU Stand-up Club is an organization for stand-up comedy and those who love it. We aim to organize shows for campus comedians and invite professional comedians based on members' suggestions.
summary: BU Stand-up Club is an organization for stand-up comedy and those who love it. We aim to organize shows for campus comedians and invite professional comedians based on members' suggestions.

name: Mock Trial Organization
description: The Boston University Mock Trial Organization is a student-run organization focused on competing in mock trial at the collegiate level. This encompasses acting as attorney and witnesses to (fictionally) try both criminal and civil court cases in front of actual attorneys. We are a member of the American Mock Trial Association and have a long history of competing at invitational, regional, and national tournaments.
summary: The Boston University Mock Trial Organization is a student-run organization focused on competing as attorneys and witnesses in criminal and civil court cases. We are a member of AMTA and have a long history of competing at the national level.

name: Dancesport Club
description: The Dancesport Club at Boston University aims to share its love of ballroom dancing with the student body. At BUDC, we strive to provide a welcoming and encouraging environment for anyone interested in learning more about ballroom dancing. We're open to everyone, whether you are interested in competitive or social dancing, and whether you've been dancing for 10 years or have never stepped on a dancefloor before. We hire professional coaches who teach lessons at varying levels, host showcases and team bonding events, and travel to competitions. Check out our website and social media for more information!
summary: The Boston University Dancesport Club aims to share its love of ballroom dancing with the student body through lessons, showcases, and competitions. Come check out BUDC if you want to learn more about ballroom dancing!

name: Undergraduate History Association
description: The Undergraduate History Association serves to promote the academic study of history as well as a resource for students who are interested in history. Our goal is to allow those with a like-minded interest in history to discover others with similar passions so that they can learn from each other, discuss various topics, and play fun games with each other. If you have any interest in history, come by and say hello! There are no requirements to join, as long as you like history!
summary: The Undergraduate History Association serves to promote the academic study of history as well as a resource for students who are interested in history.  We organize monthly field trips and events with a historical focus in addition to hosting meetings.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
description: The Public Relations Student Society of America (PRSSA) is the foremost organization for students interested in public relations and communications. We seek to advance the public relations profession by nurturing generations of future professionals. We advocate rigorous academic standards for public relations education, the highest ethical principles, and diversity in the profession. PRSSA is made up of more than 10,000 students and advisers organized into 300 plus Chapters in the United States and one in Argentina. We are headquartered in New York City and led by a National Committee of PRSSA and PRSA members. We have a rich history of support from our parent organization, the Public Relations Society of America (PRSA), which offers professional development, networking opportunities, and news. PRSSA helps you enhance your education, broaden your network and launch your career in public relations.
summary: The Public Relations Student Society of America (PRSSA) is the foremost organization for students interested in public relations and communications. We seek to advance the public relations profession by nurturing generations of future professionals.

name: The Buzz
description: The Buzz is a lifestyle magazine geared toward all Boston University students. The focus of the Buzz is on news and happenings on the BU campus and in the city of Boston—the two communities to which all Boston University students belong. We hope to inform students about what is going on around them and inspire them to explore opportunities for fun and education on and off campus. Sections include: campus, city, culture, fashion, food, music, wellness, travel, and opinion. The Buzz was founded during the Spring 2009 semester by magazine journalism students Allie Vasilakis (COM '11) and Marisa Morrison (COM '11) with the goal of establishing an on-campus magazine of which all students could be a part. Every step in creating the magazine is completed by current Boston University students. From writers to photographers to graphic designers and models, positions on the Buzz's staff are filled by members of our talented student body. The Buzz prints one issue at the end of each academic semester (Fall and Spring semesters) and debuted with its premiere issue at the end of the Fall 2009 semester. Copies of The Buzz are distributed at the GSU Link at indicated times during the semester. If you are interested in advertising with The Buzz, please send an inquiry to the.bu.buzz@gmail.com. Our magazine is printed through Century Type. For more information on Century Type, visit centurytype.com.
summary: The Buzz is a lifestyle publication geared toward all Boston University students. The Buzz focuses on news and happenings on the BU campus and in the city of Boston. We aim to inform and inspire our audience with our print edition and weekly articles.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description: The National Society of Black Engineers aims to maintain the mission "to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community." We are a chapter of the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE) but we accept any member who cares to support our cause or who feels that they may benefit from our efforts. We aim to serve as active members in our university, our community, and in NSBE through events, community service, and attendance of NSBE conferences.
summary: The National Society of Black Engineers aims to maintain the mission "to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community."

name: Allocations Board
description: The Allocations Board is an organization on BU’s campus tasked with distributing a portion of the Community Student Fee (CSF) to on- and off-campus events held by undergraduate student organizations. The AB is composed entirely of undergraduates from across BU’s schools, residence halls, and student groups. Though each member attends every meeting and works through breaks, members receive no compensation other than the joy of supporting and encouraging great events. We hope this site serves both as a resource to groups requesting funding and for students curious about how their CSF is distributed, and how they can become involved in the process. Please contact us, either through this site or at allocate@bu.edu with any questions or concerns.
summary: The Allocations Board is an organization on BU’s campus tasked with distributing a portion of the Community Student Fee (CSF) to on- and off-campus events held by undergraduate student organizations. The AB is composed entirely of undergraduates from a...

name: Undergraduate Philosophy Association
description: Boston University’s Undergraduate Philosophy Association holds weekly informal discussions on a variety of philosophical issues. Meetings cover topics such as artificial intelligence, epistemology, and music. All discussion topics are voted on by general members and all meetings are moderated by the E-Board. Additionally, we host a yearly lecturer, a cross-college philosophy mixer, group outings, symposiums on underrepresented philosophies, and more. The UPA is open to undergraduates from any academic background looking to join us in our love for philosophy. For more information please contact our president, Josephine Perl, at jsperl@bu.edu.
summary: Boston University’s Undergraduate Philosophy Association holds weekly informal discussions on a variety of philosophical issues. In addition, we host lectures, resource meetings, group outings, underrepresented philosophy symposiums, and more.

name: Elementary Education
description: The Elementary Education Club aims to serve as a resource and forum for the all undergraduate students at Boston University interested in elementary education. Through community service, we contribute to the educational needs of children in the greater Boston community. The club serves as an academic resource, allowing for the sharing of student perspectives, and builds a community through meetings, discussions, social events, and volunteer opportunities. We warmly welcome anyone of interest, not just education majors.
summary: We aim to serve as a resource and forum for all undergraduate students at Boston University interested in elementary education. Through community service, we contribute to the educational needs of children in the greater Boston community.

name: Delta Gamma
description:
Delta Gamma at Boston University. For Hope, For Strength, For Life.

 

Delta Gamma Fraternity was founded in 1873 at the Lewis School for Girls by three young women: Anna Boyd, Mary Comfort, and Eva Webb. The greek letters Delta Gamma were chosen to represent our motto "Do Good." Doing Good remains central to our mission as a chapter, as we push ourselves to be the best women we can be. 

 

DG strives to foster high ideals of friendship among women, to promote their educational and cultural interests, to create in them a true sense of social responsibility, and to develop in them the best qualities of character. As a DG, you are exposed to resources and experiences that will empower you to live up to these ideals. 

 

Our chapter prioritizes service as a fundamental part of our college experience. We work with partners such as Perkins School for the Blind and the Massachusetts Association for the Blind and Visually Impaired (MABVI) that align with our philanthropic mission. 


If you are interested in learning more about our chapter or have any questions, please don't hesitate to reach out to Sasha at dgzzpresident@gmail.com
summary:
Delta Gamma at Boston University. For Hope, For Strength, For Life.

Delta Gamma Fraternity was founded in 1873 at the Lewis School for Girls by three young women: Anna Boyd, Mary Comfort, and Eva Webb.

name: Students of Caribbean Ancestry
description: As Boston University's only integrated Caribbean cultural organization, SOCA is dedicated to celebrating and promoting West Indian culture. Through an array of events and discussions, we aim to highlight the richness of Caribbean culture including our history, food, current events, and the arts. We also seek to educate the wider BU community of issues relating to and affecting the Caribbean region.
summary: As BU’s only integrated Caribbean cultural organization, SOCA is dedicated to celebrating the richness of West Indian culture on campus through an array of events and discussions.

name: Rotaract
description:
The Boston University Rotaract Club is an international, young-professional, service organization for adults, ages 18 to 30. Rotaract is the college chapter of Rotary International, an organization centered around community service. Our club is within District 7910 and works closely with the Brookline Rotary Club for volunteering events and networking opportunities.

Our club has a focus on community service with the community of Boston, while also incorporating events to encourage social bonding and professional development. Current/planned events include: Thrive Well cooking program, Headshot Night, Valentine's Day Letter Writing, and more. We are always open to new members, ideas, and opportunities!
summary: Rotaract is a service-oriented, professional development organization that welcomes all members of the BU community. We hope to provide members opportunities to connect with the community of Boston through service outreach projects and partnerships.

name: Pre-Veterinary and Animal Lovers Society
description: Our main goal is to prepare members for veterinary school and provide outlets for volunteering. We aid members in course selection to ensure that they meet all of the undergraduate prerequisites, and we provide information about animal-related volunteer opportunities. However, you do not need to be pre-vet to join PALS. We also help students who are undecided by providing exposure to a wide variety of animals and by meeting with veterinarians who specialize in different fields. By doing so, we hope to present a realistic picture of the profession to members and to provide them with enough information to make their decision. For all non pre-veterinary members, we provide exciting trips, rewarding volunteering experiences and tons of social events. Join the only animal-related group on Boston University's campus!
summary: This club has dual functions as a space for pre-veterinary students to learn about vet schools and jobs, and for animal lovers to meet people with similar interests. We hold vet school info sessions, volunteering meetings, and social events.

name: Student Leadership & Impact Center
description: The mission of Student Leadership & Impact Center (SLIC) centers around our 3 C’s, Connection, Collaboration, and Community, to create engagement and leadership opportunities across campus. We aim to offer support to students who are looking to explore involvement related to their specific interests, whether that be service, identity, academics, performance, politics, recreation, or fraternity & sorority life.
summary: The Student Leadership & Impact Centercenters around our 3 C’s, Connection, Collaboration, and Community, to create engagement and leadership opportunities across campus.

name: Entrepreneur's Club
description: BU Entrepreneur's Club is an organization for students of all backgrounds and majors to become part of the best community of student innovators at BU. We aim to help students build professional networks and start their own ventures by holding events each semester that allow members of our club to meet and network with other entrepreneurial students, VCs, advisory mentors, and inspiring entrepreneurs in the Boston community.
summary: BU Entrepreneurship Club is a place for all students of all backgrounds and all majors to become engaged with entrepreneurship. We focus on holding interesting events each semester that allow members or our club to meet fellow entrepreneurial students,.

name: Islamic Society
description: ISBU strives to aid our members in practicing and becoming educated about their faith, to establish a unifying sense of community and to spread the correct understanding of Islam among students, staff and the greater community, all with the ultimate goal of pleasing Allah (God). Please like our facebook page for updates: https://www.facebook.com/islamicsocietybu Best, ISBU E-Board
summary: ISBU strives to aid our members in practicing and becoming educated about their faith, to establish a unifying sense of community and to spread the correct understanding of Islam among students, staff and the greater community, all with the ultimate go...

name: Chinese Students Association
description: Boston University Chinese Student Association is a student group dedicated to promoting Chinese Culture and fostering friendship in the Chinese Community. We welcome people of all backgrounds and nationalities to join us, to celebrate and learn traditions that have endured for thousands of years. As the largest cultural group on campus, and one of the leading student groups at BU, we offer a range of activities and events for our members. Whether you're interested in cultural extravaganzas, lessons in dumpling making, or dinner outings with your fellow students, we encourage you to come experience the benefits that CSA has to offer.
summary: Boston University Chinese Student Association is a student group dedicated to promoting Chinese Culture and fostering friendship in the Chinese Community. We welcome people of all backgrounds and nationalities to join us, to celebrate and learn traditi...

name: Jalwa
description: Constantly integrating traditional and modern styles of dance from the East and the West, Jalwa pushes limits with its choreography and fusion elements. Jalwa, or "passion" in Hindi, is what unites this team - a passion to dance, a passion to perform, and a passion to amaze the crowd. Incorporating Hiphop, Bollywood, Bhangra, Classical and Contemporary dance styles, Jalwa has competed on stages nationwide, including competitions in Champaign, Atlanta, Cleveland, Dallas, Minneapolis, and Los Angeles in 2022-23 alone! Our devotion to creativity, overall dedication, and undying love for one another is what sets us apart. We are not just a dance team, we are a family. We are Boston University Jalwa - one team, one passion.
summary: Constantly integrating traditional and modern styles of dance from the East and the West, Jalwa pushes limits with its choreography and fusion elements.  Jalwa, or "passion" in Hindi, is what unites this team - a passion to dance, perform, and excite!

name: Fatakada
description: BU Fatakada is Boston University’s premier co-ed Garba-Raas team, featuring top dancers at BU who proudly share their culture with the Boston University community. Garba-Raas, originating from the Indian state of Gujarat, is a traditional folk dance style that has evolved into a competitive dance form due to the efforts of South Asian-American students over the past two decades. We aim to enrich the BU community through our dance and uphold BU’s longstanding presence within the national collegiate Garba-Raas community, as one of the oldest Garba-Raas teams in the circuit.
summary: Our goal is to spread the awareness of Garba/Raas, a traditional culture rooted from South Asia, through various performances. We are proud to exhibit our culture, our creativity, and our hard work to both the BU community and Raas community nationally.

name: Queer Activist Collective
description:
The Queer Activist Collective ("Q") is Boston University's largest LGBTQ+ student organization dedicated to ensuring the awareness, visibility, & full inclusion of the LGBTQ+ community.

 

We are committed to creating programming that is uplifting and affirming for all LGBTQ+ community members. As a majority QTBIPOC & TGNCI Executive Board, we are especially passionate about supporting & advocating for communities who are often excluded from mainstream LGBTQ+ spaces.

 

We host programming that includes events such as mental & sexual health workshops, BU faculty & staff panels, Know Your Rights legal workshops, game nights, allyship workshops, drag shows, & much more. 

 

For questions, more info on how to get involved, help connecting with resources, or ideas on how we can better support you and the LGBTQ+ community at BU, please email us at queer@bu.edu! 

 

Check out our comprehensive BU/Boston LGBTQ+ resource guides here!
summary: The Queer Activist Collective ("Q") is Boston University's largest LGBTQ+ student organization dedicated to ensuring the awareness, visibility, & full inclusion of the LGBTQ+ community.

name: Trans Listening Circle
description:
Trans Listening Circle (TLC) is a weekly-meeting club for people who are transgender, nonbinary, gender non-conforming, questioning, and more to find community on campus.

 

The topics discussed at meetings include gender, self-expression, and real-world problems as they relate to members of the Boston University community. TLC aims to provide resources and (non-professional) support, both in and out of meetings, as well as spread awareness of and facilitate inclusion regarding the incredible diversity on campus.

 

Join us on Mondays at 6:15 PM EST!
summary: Trans Listening Circle (TLC) is a weekly-meeting club for people who are transgender, nonbinary, gender non-conforming, questioning, and more to find community on campus.

name: Navigators Christian Fellowship
description: The Navigators at Boston University is a community of students and friends who want to know God and Jesus Christ, and who want to love and encourage each other while walking through life together in Boston. There are no prerequisites or specific affiliations required to participate in Navigators activities. We encourage you to come just as you are and enjoy friendship and community with us as we seek to understand who God is and why it matters.
summary: The Navigators at Boston University is a community of students and friends who want to know God and Jesus Christ, and who want to love and encourage each other while walking through life together in Boston. There are no prerequisites or specific affili...

name: Red Star Theater
description: A Chinese student-based theater club in Boston, our mission is to create, direct, and perform captivating dramas while fostering cultural exchange. Through our productions, we aim to showcase the rich tapestry of Chinese culture and bridge connections with diverse communities.
summary: A Boston-based theater club of Chinese students, dedicated to creating, directing, and performing our own dramas while promoting cultural exchange.

name: Fashion and Retail Association
description:
The Fashion and Retail Association (F&R) of Boston University is committed to building a pipeline to the fashion and retail industry through a variety of events and opportunities. It is the ideal outlet for undergraduate BU students who are interested in both creative and professional opportunities in the workforce.

Since it was founded in Spring 2004, F&R has grown into a thriving community with over 100 students involved to this day. We are currently in the process of rebranding the association to promote a more inclusive and professional space for students to express themselves and showcase the influence fashion and retail has on campus.

Every year, F&R has a calendar packed with events ranging from professional development panels featuring industry experts, a student-run fashion show, guest speakers, company partnerships, and more! These events open the opportunity for students to not only express their creativity but also build a community of like-minded people and show the university all of the hidden talent it has.

Our weekly general meetings promote a safe space through activities ranging from educational games that spark conversation, like Kahoot and Jeopardy, to professional development workshops, like Linkedin, cover letter, and resume building.

Don’t forget to check out our website for blog posts on the latest fashion news and to learn more about what we do.
summary: Fashion & Retail Association is a Boston University undergraduate organization, dedicated to connecting students to the competitive fashion and retail industry through all business functions, aspects of communication, and creative design.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
description:
The Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers (SASE) was founded in November 2007 to help Asian heritage scientific and engineering professionals achieve their full potential. Organizations existed for other affinity groups - The National Society of Black Engineers, the Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers, and the Society of Women Engineers - and there was a need for a similar organization where students representing all of the pan Asian cultures could connect and support each other.

SASE is dedicated to the advancement of Asian heritage scientists and engineers in education and employment so that they can achieve their full career potential. In addition to professional development, SASE also encourages members to contribute to the enhancement of the communities in which they live.

SASE’s mission is to

  • Prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world.
  • Celebrate diversity on campuses and in the workplace.
  • Provide opportunities for members to make contributions to their local communities.

If you're interested in getting involved, sign up for our newsletter and follow our instagram for updates on events! If you have any additional questions or comments, feel free to reach out to us by email.
summary:
SASE’s mission is to:

Prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world.
Celebrate diversity on campuses and in the workplace.
Provide opportunities for members to make contributions to their local communities.

name: African Students Organization
description: Boston University’s African Students Organization strives to create an inclusive, celebratory, and educational space to uplift African countries, cultures, and people. We serve as a cultural, social, political, and humanitarian support network for African students at BU.
summary: Boston University’s African Students Organization strives to create an inclusive, celebratory, and educational space to uplift African countries, cultures, and people.

name: Knitting Club
description:
This club is for those interested in the fiber arts particularly knitting and crochet. BU Knitting club meets every Thursday from 7-8:30 pm (location TBA)! Whether you knit or crochet, are a newbie or expert, feel free to stop by!

If you're interested in joining us please use the link below to join the mailing list, just joining the group on Terrier Central won't automatically sign you up for it!

The link below leads to our mailing list signup, if you put in your email we'll be able to keep you informed about how and when we'll be meeting, along with other important information

http://eepurl.com/dL0Cok
summary: This club is for those interested in the fiber arts particularly knitting and crochet.  BU Knitting club meets every Thursday from 7-8:30 pm in the GSU! Whether you knit or crochet, are a newbie or expert, feel free to stop by!

name: Shakespeare Society
description: Since its creation in 2003 by a group of Shakespeare enthusiasts, the Boston University Shakespeare Society has worked tirelessly to bring the good word of the Bard to the community. The Society is certainly not your average theater group. We have put on shows, hosted trips to local plays and Renaissance festivals, and brought Shakespeare's words to the wider world. We are always looking for new members--auditions are held at the beginning of every semester and we have other events for those who don't wish to perform. While approaching the BU theater community can sometimes be a daunting and difficult task, the Society prides itself on its great community of members. We make up nearly every school and class at BU, both undergrads and grad students, creating a diverse and extremely fun-loving group of people.
summary: BUSS strives to bring Shakespeare to a diverse audience through performances that range from mainstage productions to small scene festivals, as well as group activities like our Shakespeare book club, BardRead.

name: Volunteers for Inclusion and Accessibility
description: Welcome to Volunteers for Inclusion and Accessibility! We are a group of Boston University student volunteers who actively support individuals with various disabilities such as autism, Asperger's, blindness, and learning disabilities. Our club reaches out to the Boston community by traveling to local schools and libraries to offer our help and support. We participate in activities such as arts and crafts, sports, board games, holiday parties, fundraisers, awareness walks, and much more! We aim to promote disability acceptance and inclusion in all that we do. If you're interested in working with individuals with disabilities, making new friends, and positively impacting your community, we invite you to join our organization! You can also stay updated on our latest news and events by following our Instagram @bostonu_via
summary: Welcome to Volunteers for Inclusion and Accessibility! We are a group of Boston University student volunteers who actively support individuals with various disabilities such as autism, Asperger's, blindness, and learning disabilities.

name: Club Colombia
description: In Club Colombia, Colombian students and those interested in the country come together for activities that create a sense of community such as cooking together, celebrating holidays, watching fútbol games, trying restaurants, and hosting guest speakers.
summary: In Club Colombia, Colombian students and those interested in the country come together for activities that create a sense of community such as cooking together, celebrating holidays, watching fútbol games, trying restaurants, and hosting guest speakers.

name: Filipino Student Association
description:
Mabuhay! Welcome to BUFSA!

BUFSA is an organization which advocates and celebrates the richness of the Filipino heritage! Through community involvement and education, BUFSA aims to expand our club's core values to the rest of BU students, faculty, staff, and alumni: diversity, inclusivity, acceptance, embracing and sharing Filipino culture. BUFSA is home for all students from all walks of life, and are always welcoming more members to join our family!

A few weekly and annual events we have include:

Fam Din: at 6PM/7PM on various days and in various locations TBA in the weekly newsletter.

Dance Practice for Upcoming Shows (Traditional and Modern): Learn dances from Tinikling to hip hop and everything in between. Everyone's invited, no experience needed! Practices are on weekends, time and location TBA.

Halo-Halloween: Members dress up in costume and watch our annual Halloween video, meet new people and participate in our costume contest! We incorporate our love for food by making halo-halo, a Philippine dessert of shaved ice and a variety of different toppings (jellies, fruits etc) and enjoy a night of fun and friends!

ISA: The World Is One: A cherished tradition, ISA is our annual and BU's longest running multicultural show that aims to show the BU community and Greater Boston Area the beauty that comes with diversity, and the amazing talents different cultures and communities of BU has to offer.

Speaker Series: our annual speaker series is where we invite different Filipinos paving the way in their fields to speak of their experience and share insight and encouragement that they may have for our students.

BUFSA always strives to showcase the Filipino culture and the values rooted in it, like community, resilience, and love. We hope to share these with you, and see you soon!
summary: BUFSA is an organization which advocates and celebrates the richness of the Filipino heritage! Through community involvement and education, BUFSA aims to expand our club's core values to the rest of BU students, faculty, staff, and alumni...

name: Sikh Association at Boston University
description: SABU's mission is to encourage an on-campus community that spreads awareness of the Sikh religion and inspires others to engage in community service. By bringing together Sikhs at Boston University, we can participate in events together, attend Gurudwara services near Boston, and discuss the prevalence of our beliefs and values in helping us cope with today’s issues.
summary: The Sikh Association at Boston University strives for awareness of Sikhi on campus and the general Boston area. Through workshops, weekly sessions, events, and community building days, we hope to facilitate a safe space for students.

name: Gaming Club
description:
BU Gaming Club is all about casual and competitive  gaming. We welcome all types of gamers, whether you play one hour a week or twenty. We host a variety of fun events and hold meetings every week. Join us and make friends while improving your gameplay! We also have competitive esports teams across a variety of games. Join our Discord server to learn more!

discord.gg/BostonU
summary: BU Gaming Club is all about casual and competitive gaming. We welcome all types of gamers, whether you play one hour a week or twenty. We host a variety of fun events, lots of competitive E-Sports teams (we would love to have you at tryouts!, and more!

name: Armenian Students Association
description:
The Armenian Student Association (ASA) brings together students who share an interest in Armenian culture, heritage, and community. Our mission is to strengthen cultural identity among Armenian students while also educating the broader campus about Armenian history and traditions. We celebrate our roots through events centered around food, music, dance, and important historical milestones.

Located in Boston, home to a vibrant Armenian community, the ASA takes full advantage of local cultural institutions and partnerships. Whether you're looking to connect with your heritage or simply learn more about Armenian culture, the ASA offers a welcoming space for all students to engage, celebrate, and grow together.
summary: The Armenian Student Association connects students to Armenian culture, history, and community. We celebrate our roots through food, music, and tradition, and invite others to explore Boston’s vibrant Armenian scene.

name: Outing Club
description:
The Outing Club strives to bring the joy of the great outdoors to the BU community. From urban explorations to trekking the White Mountains, we're 100% driven by the desire to take adventures beyond the BU campus. Our mission? To promote an appreciation for the natural environment through outdoor recreation. But we also just love to have fun.  Check out linktr.ee/buoutingclub to follow us on instagram, and join our email list where we send weekly emails about our upcoming trips and make sure to come to weekly meetings where we will go over previous and upcoming trips as well as a weekly event that can range from structure building in the wild to planning meals on long backpacking trips.

 

Our first meeting is on Monday 9/8/2025 at 8:15p in COM 101

All other meetings are Mondays at 8p in SCI 109
summary: The Outing Club strives to bring the joy of the great outdoors to the BU community. From urban explorations to trekking the White Mountains, we're 100% driven by the desire to take adventures beyond the BU campus. Check out linktr.ee/buoutingclub

name: miXx K-Pop Cover Dance Crew
description: The word "miXx" is a stylized version of the word "mix," where we aim to mix together people of different cultures and backgrounds to create one group filled with people who love and appreciate K-Pop. The large "X" stands for "cross-cultural communication," as we aim to learn more about and enjoy a part of Korean culture through K-Pop and dance. We were founded by two Boston University alums. We are currently active in both Japan and Boston. Those who are interested in K-Pop or who would like to learn more about it, those with dance experience or those without, those who like singing or would like to start, or those who are interested in learning more about Korean culture are strongly encouraged to join! With the system we have established, our club accommodates those with busy schedules, as you can make this club's time commitment as intense or as lax as you would like according to what you can manage. We look forward to your participation in our club!
summary: The word "miXx" is a stylized version of the word "mix," where we aim to mix together people of different cultures and backgrounds to create one group filled with people who love and appreciate K-Pop. The large "X" stands for "cross-cultural communicat...

name: Finance and Investment Club
description: The BU Finance and Investment Club offers all students at Boston University an insight into the world of finance and an opportunity to learn by practical experience. The Club hosts interactive tutorials that explore topics to an extent above and beyond the undergraduate and even the MBA level. They also host the University's only undergraduate live investment fund with 1.1 million assets under management, sourced largely from alumni donations and internal appreciation. Your research through the club will determine real money investment decisions for our portfolio. Members graduate full of hands-on experience and with a network of contacts within the industry. For additional information, please go to our website and sign up for the mailing list (on the home page). Feel free to request membership on the engage website, but please also sign up for our mailing list. Contact buinvest@bu.edu if you have questions or concerns.
summary: The BU Finance and Investment Club offers all students at Boston University an insight into the world of finance and an opportunity to learn by practical experience. The Club hosts interactive tutorials that explore topics to an extent above and beyond...

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description:
The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE-BostonU) is an organization dedicated to the empowerment of the Hispanic community in Boston and Boston University through STEM access, awareness, support, and development. We reach our goals by providing our members the support they need to succeed, both academically and professionally through various workshops and Study Nights geared towards bridging the gaps in those fields!

 

Not only is SHPE-BostonU a professional organization, but we also encourage and foster the feeling of a familia of Latinx students in STEM at Boston University. We have social events to get to know one another in a more relaxed atmosphere such as mixers with other SHPE chapters or going to try Hispanic food around Boston. And we also host events that celebrate the beauty of the Latinx culture and community such as Cafecitos which are culturally geared discussions on topics such as mental health or colorism!

 

SHPE-BostonU welcomes everyone from any background, race, ethnicity or major to join our organization! As long as you also want to empower the Hispanic community in STEM, you are eligible and more than welcome to join! Welcome to your familia!
summary: The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE-BostonU) is an organization dedicated to empowering the Hispanic community through STEM access, awareness, support, and development. Come and meet your new familia!

name: Sabor Latino
description: Sabor Latino's mission is to ignite a passion for Latinx culture through the mesmerizing fusion of music and dance. Completely student-run and representing a vibrant facet of the Latinx community at Boston University, they are committed to spreading awareness and celebrating diversity. From the infectious rhythms of bachata, salsa, merengue, and reggaeton to a world of diverse Latin dances, Sabor Latino aims to captivate hearts, spotlight Boston's vibrant community, and forge lasting connections through our dynamic performances.
summary: We are a dance group focused on Latin American rhythms as well as a fusion of modern dance styles and traditional Latin dances. In addition to dance, we aim to foster strong ties between our members and the Greater Boston community through social events.

name: Hawaii Cultural Association
description: The Hawaii Cultural Association aims to educate the Boston University community about Hawaiian culture through sharing our foods, stories, and performances. We help create a home-away-from-home for students from Hawaii, helping them adjust to college life in Boston. Join us in spreading the aloha spirit!
summary: The Hawaii Cultural Association seeks to promote awareness and understanding of the unique culture of Hawai'i to the Boston community. We also do whatever we can to help students far away from home adjust to life on the east coast.

name: Hellenic Association
description: The BUHA goal is to enlighten the Boston University campus and the greater Boston area with the language, art, culture, history, politics, economies, beauty and majesty of modern and classical Hellenism. We welcome and counsel all new hellenic students!
summary: The BUHA goal is to enlighten the Boston University campus and the greater Boston area with the language, art, culture, history, politics, beauty, and majesty of modern and classical Hellenism. We welcome and counsel all new hellenic students!

name: Environmental Student Organization
description:
The Environmental Student Organization (ESO) strives to raise awareness about environmental justice issues and climate mitigation to create beneficial environmental change on BU's campus and in the surrounding community of Boston. Our weekly meetings are a place where all students can come together and learn something new about the environment. ESO works hard to create an atmosphere where driven students have the opportunity to congregate, socialize and make changes. Specifically, we are focused on making environmentalism INCLUSIVE and ACCESSIBLE, promoting activism at all scales and highlighting environmental justice. We participate in many environmental activities in the Boston area including urban gardening, weekend hikes, partnering with local zero waste businesses, and much more! Let's make BU a more environmentally friendly campus together! Check out our social media for our meeting time and follow us on Instagram @bu.eso.

If you have any questions or want to be added to the mailing list, please email us at eso@bu.edu.
summary: The Environmental Student Organization (ESO) strives to raise awareness about environmental justice issues and climate mitigation to create beneficial environmental change on BU's campus and in the surrounding community of Boston.

name: Society of Women Engineers
description: The BU chapter of Society of Women Engineers (SWE) is a collegiate section in National SWE. We're part of region F, which is also known as the New England Region. At BU SWE, our mission is to serve and empower women in STEM, promote the engineering profession as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrate the value of diversity. Our community is open to any and all students who wish to empower women in STEM. Click the link to our website below to learn more!
summary: The BU chapter of Society of Women Engineers (SWE) is a student section in National SWE. We're part of region F, the New England Region. Our mission is to empower women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders.

name: Rocket Propulsion Group
description:
BURPG was founded in 2003 as the BU Rocket Team. We are a primarily undergraduate student organization devoted to the testing and development of liquid rockets and rocket engines. We are open to all students who share a love of aerospace and learning. By giving our members hands-on experience and team-based projects, we create some of the most capable engineers, computer scientists, and designers. Our current goal is to launch the largest liquid rocket ever launched by a university.

NOTE: WE DO NOT USE ENGAGE TO CONNECT WITH NEW MEMBERS. TO JOIN,  VISIT http://burpg.org/new-members AND FILL OUT THE FORM
summary: BURPG is a primarily undergraduate student organization devoted to the testing and development of liquid rockets and rocket engines. We are open to all students who share a love of aerospace and learning.

name: Asian Student Union
description: Boston University's Asian Student Union (ASU) is an all-inclusive organization that aims to educate members about the different aspects of Asian and Asian American culture. Our goal is to showcase talent in our community, inform the student body of social justice matters that are relevant to our world today, and collaborate with organizations that share a similar vision. Ultimately, we aim to represent our students and foster unity and family within our diverse Asian community and beyond.
summary: Boston University's Asian Student Union (ASU) is an all-inclusive organization that aims to educate members about the different aspects of Asian and Asian American culture.

name: Hong Kong Student Association
description: Boston University Hong Kong Student Association is student-run organization dedicated to promoting greater Cantonese culture on campus. Through events such as Dim Sum outings and Chinese New Year celebrations, our members can get a glimpse of Hong Kong in the city of Boston. We also collaborate with other cultural organizations and our alumni to highlight inclusivity and diversity, connecting our members with the larger BU population. We aim to provide opportunities for people of all interests and identities to come together and become a part of our growing community. If you have a passion for the Cantonese culture, miss “Home Kong” a little, or simply want to contribute to our mission, please consider BUHKSA!

summary: Welcome to the Bangladeshi Student's Association! BSA celebrates and honors the culture of Bangladesh, a thriving nation with a rich cultural history. We are the only Bangladeshi club on campus and encourage everybody in the BU community to join!

name: Association for Women in Mathematics: Student Chapter
description:
BUAWM works to increase the access that underrepresented groups have to the study of mathematics and to a supportive math community, with a focus on women undergraduate math students (but non-majors are welcome!). We aspire to be an organization that embraces diversity, inclusion, and equity so that our members can enjoy a welcoming, collaborative community. We accomplish this through hosting social, academic, & professional math events, as well as hosting initiatives & projects that promote our mission. These events & projects range from faculty panels to movie nights to collaborating with the departmental Antiracism Speaker Series to an introductory course study-buddy system. We work jointly with MAA.

Whether you have a passing interest in mathematics, or a passion for the subject, or an interest in math activism, we'd love to have you!
summary: Association for Women in Mathematics (AWM) is a welcoming space for self-identified women interested in math, hosting social, academic, & professional math events and promoting equity in & access to the beautiful world of mathematics for ALL.

name: Indonesian Student Association
description:
ISABU (Indonesian Students Association at Boston University) is a student-led cultural organization with the purpose of uniting the Indonesian community within the university as well as to promote the Indonesian culture with the community in the Greater Boston Area. Our events set the stage to build a close-knit community for our fellow Indonesian students as well as provide a home-away-from home. We engage in different collaborative projects with other associations within and outside Boston University to provide our members with opportunities to get involved with wider communities and meet new people.

The Indonesian Students Association at Boston University serves as a gateway for all students who have an interest in the culture and aesthetic of Indonesia. With our focus on cultivating a developed Indonesian community in Boston University, ISABU aims to connect every individual on campus and provide a valuable and cultural learning experience.

name: Data Science Association
description:
We are an undergraduate academic club committed to providing students with high-quality data science tools and resources they need to make an impact in the ever-changing world of big data. By creating opportunities, expanding accessibility, and building communities, we support the growth of our members and help build an active and energetic environment for passionate data scientists.
Whether you are a student at CAS, SMG, SHA, COM, ENG, or any other College at Boston University, you will encounter data science and statistics. You might deal with raw data, read and learn from summarized data relevant to your field of study, or even take courses in data science or statistics. The ubiquity of data science and its applications to academic and professional disciplines have no limits. Outside of the academic realm, data science is becoming an increasingly indispensable part of our everyday lives. When you read an article online, or research a topic for your class paper, you are very likely to see data science methods or statistical measurements used in support of the arguments of interest. For these reasons, we believe that data science literacy is crucial in the world of information and technology that we now live in. It is essential to succeed academically, build a foundation for your career, and to preserve your integrity as a consumer and supplier of information. These beliefs and principals are the ones that we carry with us in hope of being accepted and received by you. These are the beliefs and principals that make up the core of the Boston University Data Science Association.

Our goals are to:
● Become your gateway to the plethora of data science and statistical events happening in the Boston area and Boston University
● Build a framework within which you may explore your own interests, while learning and practicing data science/statistics and data science/statistical tools
● Connect you with professionals and experts in data science/statistics and its applications
● Create and inform opportunities for your personal development in areas such as research, internship, communication, presentation, and group work
● Bring together students of diverse backgrounds and interests to socialize, connect, network, and have fun!
summary: BUDSA is committed to providing students with data science tools and resources they need to make an impact in the ever-changing world of big data.

name: Unofficial Project
description: Founded in Spring 2005 at Boston University, Unofficial Project (UPro) is dedicated to delivering performances that stay true to artistic integrity and highlight the creativity of each member. Our team is made up of dancers from diverse backgrounds, and we take pride in the wide range of talent and styles represented within. As both a team and family, UPro continually strives to build and strengthen our connections within the Boston dance community. UPro annually hosts OVRDRV Jam and Dance Showcase with the goal of bridging the gap between the freestyle and choreography communities in Boston and beyond. OVRDRV invites various professional dancers to headline the showcase, including Anthony Lee and Josh Price.
summary: Founded in the Spring of 2005, Unofficial Project started off as a side project of dancers. Since its debut, UPro has expanded and developed to preforming at showcases and competitions all across the greater Boston area.

name: Zen Community
description:
We practice meditation from the Zen Buddhist tradition. Meditation helps you develop concentration, wisdom, and peace of mind. Even if you know nothing about meditation, you are welcome to join BU Zen. Weekly meetings consist of a mind-body exercise, a relaxation practice, 15 minutes of sitting meditation, and a short talk about Buddhist philosophy and practice. Meetings conclude with tea, cookies, and time to socialize. BU Zen's advisor is Barbara Feldman, a yoga instructor at BU and the Abbot of the Cambridge Zen Center. 

When: Wednesdays 7:30-9pm

Where: Robinson Room at Marsh Chapel. If you're not yet on our mailing list, email buzen@bu.edu.

Remember to wear comfortable clothing All levels of experience are welcome. Beginner's mind preferred! Please come on time.
summary:
We are a (mostly) student led club that practices Zen mindfulness. If you are interested in mindfulness, Buddhism, or seeking at bit more inner clarity, this is the club for you@

Meetings: Wed 7:30PM @ Robinson Room in the Marsh Chapel basement

name: Delta Tau Delta
description: Delta Tau Delta is a values-based organization. Our mission and values are offered to the public as a representation of the core values contained in our Ritual. All Delt men live by a common mission: "Committed to Lives of Excellence." All programs offered by the Fraternity reflect our values, and are designed to help the men of our Fraternity reach the level of excellence for which we all strive. The four fundamental principles of Delta Tau Delta are Truth, Courage, Faith and Power. Truth is vital in the relationship between an organization and the outside world. If an organization cannot be trusted, it will perish. Truth begins within the organization. In order to truly become brothers, men must be truthful to themselves and to one another. They must hold one another accountable. Courage takes heart. Those who are courageous thrive. As you navigate through life, you will be confronted with many difficult decisions. Courage helps a leader understand the best option and take it, regardless of the popularity of that choice. Courage has a distinct relationship with truth. One must know the truth in order to stand for it. Faith is belief. Faith grows and develops and changes. It means trusting yourself and believing your actions are appropriate and right. Faith will help you trust decisions made and believe that information told to you is correct. A lack of faith drives a wedge through an organization and through friendships. Power begins as inner strength. Power is a combination of talents and inherent abilities and wisdom. It is increased when the power of many individuals is united through an organization or a brotherhood. The power of an organization, when combined with courage, faith, and truth, leads one into the future and ensures success. Since our founding in 1858, the Fraternity has spread to nearly 200 campuses, with more than 130 active chapters and colonies comprised of roughly 8,000 students. More than 165,000 men have joined the brotherhood of Delta Tau Delta since its founding.
summary: Delta Tau Delta is a values-based organization. Our mission and values are offered to the public as a representation of the core values contained in our Ritual. All Delt men live by a common mission: "Committed to Lives of Excellence." All programs off...

description:
We hold meetings monthly and spread awareness of key global health issues. We also have guest speakers come to our meetings to enrich our general members and give them insight into diplomatic and IR related careers. We have had Professor Mark C. Storella share his experiences of being the Ambassador to Zambia. 

 

We also hold monthly fundraisers to raise money for UNICEF's initiatives and organizations that support children. Our most successful fundraiser was the "Be Golden Fundraiser". We raised upwards of $5,500 dollars for Memorial Sloan Kettering Hospital in New York City that went towards progressing Pediatric Cancer Research. 

 

Our Executive Board is devoted to UNICEF's mission. Every action is for every child. We hope you will join us in our efforts to create a better world for children. Thank you for your consideration and we hope you have a great day!

name: Italian Students Association
description: The aim of the Italian Students Association, with the mottto "Be Italian, BU", is to foster a strong sense of community between Italians at BU and those members of BU interested in Italian culture. We provide resources through organized events, talks, and meals, but also design a tailored introduction to Boston for incoming students from Italy. The organization aims to create an environment in which the greater Italian community can unite to discuss their views about the culture, current and historic events of Italy. The association will also create a bridge to faculty and alumni, thus stimulating debate and creating a network for Boston University students in Italy. The organization aims to share the Italian culture with students from all around the world and give them access to the Italian way of life through discussions and targeted guest speakers. The group respects the opinions of all members. In order to create an atmosphere in which the group members can share their passions, we will organize dining events and soirees through which the community can share poetry or watch movies in their original language. We will also organize excursions to engage Italian opera pieces and other muscial, literary, culinary, and philosophical resources. Most importantly, through our faculty and alumni we would like to become a regional platform for guest speakers from around the world.

description: The American and New England Studies Graduate Student Association was founded in the spring of 2012 to meet a growing desire for greater community networking and support, to foster collegiality and creative thinking, and to represent student interests in an official manner to the university as well as to the larger community of American Studies graduate programs and scholars both here and abroad.The Association hosts student colloquia (The Americanist Forum), facilitates networking amongst groups and individuals with similar scholarly interests and goals, hosts professional development opportunities, and works to build interdisciplinary, scholarly community at Boston University.

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description:
LEADERSHIP. FRIENDSHIP. SERVICE. 
 
Alpha Phi Omega (APO) is the largest gender-inclusive community service organization in the United States. Our pillars of Leadership, Friendship, and Service guide us as we give back to our campus, community, and country all while making lifelong friendships and gaining essential leadership and professional skills.
 

At BU, the Zeta Upsilon chapter of more than 100 active members unites through our shared passion for ethical volunteerism, commitment to active citizenship, and interest in Boston’s unique social justice issues. 

 

We offer a variety of service opportunities to address topics ranging from hunger and homelessness to community initiatives and public education. With partners such as the American Red Cross Food Pantry, Community Servings, Women’s Lunch Place, East End House, and the Boston Bruins Foundation, there’s always a way to make a responsible and effective difference locally!

 

You can learn more about us at https://www.apo-zu.org and follow our Instagram @apozu to see what we’re up to! Pledging is open at the beginning of each semester. Please email pledgemaster.buapo@gmail.com or president.buapo@gmail.com for more information.
summary: Alpha Phi Omega (APO) Zeta Upsilon is BU's chapter of the national gender-inclusive community service fraternity. We work to promote active citizenship and ethical volunteerism in the city of Boston while building a diverse and meaningful community.

BU Japanese Student Association (BUJSA) is a student-run organization dedicated to promoting greater Japanese awareness and diversity on campus through general meetings and collaborative events with other cultural groups on campus, and other intercollegiate JSAs.

 

We host exciting events throughout the academic year, such as movie and game nights, food outings, and our annual Haunted House, Sushi Formal, and year-end "Omatsuri" Cultural Festival. You can join our events as a general attendee or help plan events as an E-Board member. We aim to work not only with local charities and the Circle K International Service Partners (e.g. UNICEF), but also with other student organizations on campus in any capacity in which we are needed. BU Circle K International is sponsored by the Somerville Kiwanis and Greater Boston Young Professionals, who wish to work closely with us and will be regularly inviting members to volunteer with some of their ongoing service projects. We strive to be a driving force for community service on campus and to bring a positive influence to the Greater Boston Area.
