name: Qazaq Student Association
description:
Welcome to Qazaq Student Association at Boston University! We're a vibrant and inclusive organization dedicated to promoting awareness and fostering appreciation of the Kazakhstani community nestled within the bustling city of Boston. Our mission is to cultivate a rich tapestry of cultural exchange, education, and social engagement, creating a warm and inviting space where individuals from all walks of life can come together to celebrate diversity and strengthen community bonds.

We believe in the power of meaningful connections and the transformative potential of cross-cultural exchange. Through a diverse array of intellectual, cultural, and social events, we strive to provide a platform for dialogue, learning, and celebration. Whether you're interested in exploring the rich history and traditions of Kazakhstan, engaging in thought-provoking discussions on contemporary issues, or simply connecting with fellow members of the community, there's something for everyone at QSABU.

From educational seminars and workshops to vibrant cultural festivals and holiday celebrations, our calendar is filled with opportunities for growth, connection, and celebration. We also organize conferences and special events tailored to the unique needs and interests of students and families from Kazakhstan, providing valuable resources and support to help them thrive in their new home away from home.

Above all, QSABU is committed to creating a safe and inclusive space where individuals of all backgrounds feel welcome, valued, and respected. We believe that by coming together with open hearts and minds, we can bridge divides and build a stronger, more cohesive community for all.

Join us as we on this enriching journey of discovery, connection, and celebration. Together, let's make a difference and build a brighter future for the Kazakhstani community in Boston and beyond
summary: We're dedicated to fostering awareness, appreciation, and inclusivity within Boston's Kazakhstani community. Through diverse events and celebrations, we aim to create a safe space for cultural exchange among students and to organize beneficial events!

name: Arts Administration Student Association
description: The Boston University Arts Administration Student Association (AASA) is a Graduate student group that was formed in the spring of 1995 to provide opportunities for social interaction, educational enrichment and career development for Arts Administration students. Our mission is to cultivate a sense of community among Arts Administration students while creating educational enrichment and career development opportunities. As part of this mission, the association works to make new students feel as comfortable and confident as possible from the moment they arrive. AASA can be a valuable instrument in raising the profile of the Arts Administration program among industry professionals, as well as an indispensable resource for developing personal and professional relationships with fellow students. We hope that you will choose to lend your time and talents as active members of AASA as we endeavor to make the association a more visible part of the BU Arts Administration program and the arts community in Boston. We hope to hear from many of you!
summary: The Boston University Arts Administration Student Association (AASA) is a Graduate group that was formed in the spring of 1995 to provide opportunities for social interaction, educational enrichment and career development for Arts Administration students

summary: DREAM is a non-profit mentoring program that pairs college students and children from affordable housing communities in Boston. If you're interested in becoming a DREAM mentor, please contact budreamrecruit@gmail.com!

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
description:
Kappa Alpha Theta prides itself in fostering the growth of leading women! We value personal excellence, commitment to service, intellectual curiosity, and leadership potential. For our chapter, scholarship is our highest aim, we are proud of our sisters' commitment to this tenent.

 

We also prioritize service to others through our work with Court Appointed Special Advocates, Guardian Ad Litem (CASA GAL). CASA GAL is an organization that pairs trained special advocated with children navigating the court system. These advocated act as both a mentor and voice for the child through this difficult experience. Often, these Advocates are the only person looking after and supporting the child. The work of CASA GAL is deeply important to Kappa Alpha Theta and especially to the Eta Chi chapter at BU. We are proud to support the local Boston CASA chapter and give back to our community. We commit ourselves to hosting events each semester such as Mocktails and Painting and a Theta Dodgeball Tournament, Throw for Theta, to encourage others to get involved, as well as making it fun.

 

The Eta Chi chapter also strongly feels the importance of sisterhood, which we enjoy through movie nights, craft making, ice skating events, and more.

Check out our Instagram (@thetabostonu) or contact our VP Administration (buthetacao@gmail.com), to find out more about our wonderful community of leading women!
summary: Kappa Alpha Theta exists to nurture each member throughout her college and alumnae experience. Now, more than ever, the Eta Chi chapter at Boston University feels the importance of sisterhood along with our strong commitment to scholarship and service.

name: Kappa Kappa Psi
description: The Theta Beta Chapter of Kappa Kappa Psi at Boston University is a gender inclusive service fraternity dedicated to the improvement of the Boston University Bands.
summary: The Theta Beta Chapter of Kappa Kappa Psi at Boston University is a national service fraternity dedicated to the improvement of the Boston University Bands.

name: Mexican Students Association
description: The Mexican Students Association at Boston University is dedicated to celebrating and preserving Mexican culture, identity, and community on campus. Our mission is to create a welcoming and empowering space for students of Mexican heritage—and anyone interested in our culture—to connect, share experiences, and feel at home, even while far from home. Through cultural events, community-building initiatives, and collaborative efforts with other student groups, we aim to promote pride in our roots and strengthen the visibility of the Mexican and Latino presence at BU.
summary: MEXAS @ BU celebrates Mexican culture and creates community for Mexican and Latino students through events, support, and tradition-sharing. We welcome everyone interested in Mexican culture—there’s a place for you here, no matter your background.

name: Organization of Pakistani Students
description: The Boston University Organization of Pakistani Students is a student group seeking to celebrate and share our interest in the cultural, political, and social fabric of Pakistan and its South and Central Asian neighbors. The club was founded in the fall of 2002 with the hopes of rekindling the spirit of celebrating all that is Pakistan begun by the Pakistani students associations of the past. Students of Pakistani heritage have been an active part of the Boston University community for decades, and it is the goal of the OPS to continue their strive to establish a clear portrayal of Pakistan and its culture and to be part of the multi-ethnic fabric of BU and the city of Boston. So join us in showing your Pakistani Pride and demonstrating how great Pakistan is! Pakistan Zindabad!
summary: The Boston University Organization of Pakistani Students is a student group seeking to celebrate and share our interest in the cultural, political, and social fabric of Pakistan and its South and Central Asian neighbors.The club was founded in the fall...

name: Kidney Disease Screening and Awareness Program
description: The Kidney Disease Screening and Awareness Program (KDSAP) is a student-run organization that provides free kidney health screenings and health education to underserved communities in Boston and in the Greater Boston Area. We provide programs geared towards improving the health of underserved populations. According to the National Kidney Foundation, 1 in 3 American adults is currently at risk for developing chronic kidney disease. What's worse is that 1 in 9 American adults already have kidney disease and don't know it yet. KDSAP aims to raise awareness about chronic kidney disease and help prevent its onset by providing free kidney screenings to underserved communities. There are multiple components to this program, including community health screenings, community health education events, and opportunities to participate in colloquia on and off campus. KDSAP was founded in 2008 by students at Harvard University under the direction of Dr. Li-Li Hsiao. There are currently 20 chapters of KDSAP in the Unites States and Canada.
summary: The Kidney Disease Screening and Awareness Program (KDSAP) is a student-run organization that provides free kidney health screenings and health education to underserved communities in Boston and in the Greater Boston Area. We provide programs geared to...

name: Christians on Campus
description: Our purpose is to help all the students gain a deeper understanding of the Bible to guide their lives. We have found that meeting together with other pursuing believers - including undergraduates, graduates, and families of the BU community - is the start to understanding and enjoying God's Word in our lives. We also have found that with an open and exercised spirit, all the mysteries of the Bible can become our daily reality. For questions or more details, check out our Instagram @buchristiansoncampus or email us at buchristiansoncampus@gmail.com.
summary: Like our name, our goal is simple: to help students gain a deeper understanding of the Bible to guide their lives and find their true purpose and meaning in God, and to be rooted in a strong Christian community to grow in the critical college years.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta
description: Alpha Epsilon Delta, the only national pre-health honor society, was founded in 1926 at the University of Alabama and is currently one of the 57 members of the Association of College Honor Societies. The primary objectives of AED include promoting the pursuit of scholarly excellence, establishing a network among pre-health students and health educators and professionals, and creating opportunities for personal, intellectual, and professional development. AED serves to prepare students for a life of service. The Boston University Massachusettes Gamma chapter of AED was established in 1979. Members of BU's chapter of AED have organized and participated in social events, informational series, and volunteer projects each school year. These activities have been organized in order to foster a sense of community for the students in AED, to promote pre-health careers, and to benefit the greater Boston community.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Delta, the only national pre-health honor society, was founded in 1926 at the University of Alabama and is currently one of the 57 members of the Association of College Honor Societies. The primary objectives of AED include promoting the ...

name: Treblemakers
description: The BU Treblemakers were formed in 1996 around three fundamental ideals: fun, love, and music.  Since then, generations of Trebs have rallied around these values to become one of Boston University’s most irresistible all-gender a cappella groups. Over the years, many generations of Trebs have participated in producing nine studio albums and three EPs, most recently: “Afterglow,” which is streaming on all platforms. Today, the group is known for its diverse repertoire, energetic performances, and talented soloists, recently earning the title of “Best Mixed Voices Collegiate Lead Vocal” in the 2025 CARA Awards. Follow us on Instagram @butreblemakers to stay updated on our performances and latest content!
summary: The Boston University Treblemakers were formed in 1996 around three fundamental ideals: fun, love, and music. Sticking close to those values, the Treblemakers have become a tight-knit group with a large presence on Boston University's campus. Dozens of...

name: Cru
description: Welcome to Cru! Cru at BU is a local chapter of the national movement of Cru. We are a community for students from all backgrounds who desire to grow and experience the love of Jesus together. We meet weekly in large groups with other students around Fenway and in small discipleship groups (G3's) throughout the week. We also offer a number of opportunities throughout the year to connect with each other and share Christ.
summary: Welcome to Cru! Cru at BU is a local chapter of the national movement of Cru. We are a community for students from all backgrounds who desire to grow and experience the love of Jesus together. We have large group and discipleship groups (G3's) weekly.

name: INK Illustration Club
description:
Welcome to INK!

Formerly the Illustration Narrative Collaborative, INK is a creative club at BU aimed at artists, writers and storytellers from any medium or background. Whether you’re a beginner artist or a wizened industry expert, we have a variety of workshops, critique nights and drawing challenges to help you learn and improve.

Come and showcase your talent in our club semester projects or collaborative event! We also host game nights, dinners, art contests, and visit conventions like Anime Boston and MICE. Join us and make friends in BU's art community! :)
summary: Are you interested in art, webtoons, writing, storyboarding, animating, or character design? Join INK!! We are a creative club and community of artists and writers from all backgrounds and mediums. We host workshops and events open to everyone!

name: CAS Student Government
description:
The mission of the Boston University College of Arts and Sciences Student Government (CAS StuGov) is to advocate for and represent the interests of the CAS student body. We seek to foster relationships within the CAS and Boston University communities by connecting students to resources in order to improve the quality of student life and encourage cross-discipline communication and relationships. In the BU community, CAS Student Government endeavors to be a connection point for all clubs, organizations, departments, and students that are found within the College of Arts and Sciences.

As the largest school at BU, it makes our job as student leaders fun and diverse. Our main duties include: programming events, advocating for CAS student issues, improving transparency between administration & students, as well as providing resources for students to use and take part in. We have worked on issues ranging from Credit/No Credit to free menstrual products in all campus buildings. We have also organized large-scale campus events and traditions, such as the Comm Ave Carnival and '80s Roller Skating Night! We are always looking for new ideas and more involvement in CAS StuGov, so if you are interested in joining, contact any of our eBoard members, or stop by one of our many meetings & events!

Follow us on Instagram: @cas_stugov
summary:
The College of Arts & Sciences Student Government advocates for and represents the interests of the CAS student body. Our organization aims to host events, create initiatives, and foster an amazing family community for all in CAS! 

Insta: @cas_stugov

name: Delta Kappa Alpha
description:
Established March 16, 1936, Delta Kappa Alpha is a National, Gender Inclusive, Professional Cinema Fraternity for cinematic arts students of character. As a National organization with over 75 years of history and alumni, our members are connected to the top cinema students from top cinema schools at universities such as USC, NYU, UCLA, Chapman, UT Austin, LMU, Columbia University, and many others. DKA is grounded in timeless personal and professional values and principles, and as an organization, our vision is to be recognized as the premier institute of upstanding entertainment industry leaders. Not exclusive to film studies majors, DKA welcomes all genders, undergraduates and graduates, who are dedicated to fostering lifelong character, collaborative and creative storytelling, ethical and productive business practices, philanthropic action, and fraternal bonds by and between students of the cinematic arts.
If you have any interest in joining Delta Kappa Alpha or have any questions about us, you can contact our Recruitment Chairs at recruitment@bu.dka.org.
summary: Established March 16, 1936, Delta Kappa Alpha (DKA) is a National, Gender Inclusive, Professional Cinema Fraternity for cinematic arts students of character.

name: Anthropology In The Works
description:
We are Anthropology in the Works, a student collective of anthropologists, educators, and activists in training. We envision a world where Anthropology is accessible and understandable beyond academia and theory. We strive to teach how it can be embodied and integrated into personal life and public engagement.

We are dedicated to learning more about the traditions and customs of different cultures by hosting and participating in events around campus and the Boston area. We also offer students the opportunity to meet with and get to know the dedicated BU Anthropology Department. Through excursions, discussions, presentations, and celebrations we hope that any student interested in Anthropology can experience firsthand what it means to be an Anthropologist.
summary: We are a student-run group dedicated to learning more about the traditions and customs of different cultures by hosting and participating in excursions, discussions, presentations, and celebrations around campus and the Boston area.

name: Pi Kappa Alpha
description: The Lambda Nu Chapter was colonized in late November of 2004. The group was made up of a diverse group of approximately 30 men with a lofty set of goals to meet in order to be chartered. Over the next year and a half, the colony, under the leadership of President Chase Classen, grew in both size and scope. The chapter was chartered on April 8th, 2006 with 63 brothers. Since receving our charter in the Spring of 2006, the Lambda Nu Chapter has gone on to be recognized as a fraternity at Boston University. We involve ourselves in multiple arenas, inlcuding athletics, philanthropy, community service, and social. Some of our more noted achievements in the past year include approximately 2000 hours of community service and nearly $38,000 raised for charity. In each of our first three years of existence, we won the Fraternity of the Year Award at Boston University. We hope to earn back this tittle by the end of this school year. History of the Pi Kappa Alpha Fraternity The Pi Kappa Alpha International Fraternity is a Greek letter, secret, college, social fraternity. It was founded at the University of Virginia on March 1, 1868. The Fraternity is composed of men who share similar ideals of friendship, truth, honor, and loyalty. The Fraternity's ideals are expressed in the written words and symbols of a secret ritual. These ideals and members' ability to maintain the visions of the Fraternity's founders are the great moral legacy of Pi Kappa Alpha. More information on the fraternity can be found at www.pikes.org.
summary: The Lambda Nu Chapter was colonized in late November of 2004.  The group was made up of a diverse group of approximately 30 men with a lofty set of goals to meet in order to be chartered. Over the next year and a half, the colony, under the leadership ...

name: National Society of Collegiate Scholars
description: The National Society of Collegiate Scholars (NSCS) is an honor society inviting high-achieving freshmen and sophomores. Members must have a GPA of 3.4 or above and rank in the top 20 percent of their class. NSCS is proud to offer more money for undergraduate scholarships than any other honor society nationwide. But more than that, NSCS is a growth opportunity, leadership training ground, and resource. Students who join meet people on campus and at other schools they might not have otherwise
summary: The National Society of Collegiate Scholars (NSCS) is an honor society inviting high-achieving freshmen and sophomores. Members must have a GPA of 3.4 or above and rank in the top 20 percent of their class. NSCS is proud to offer more money for undergr...

name: Hindu Cultural Association
description: From its conception, the Boston University Hindu Cultural Association aims to promote awareness of the Hindu religion and to foster a greater understanding of Hindu heritage among the Boston University community. We strive to broaden and enrich the experiences of our community by providing a platform through which people can freely practice, discuss, and learn more about Hinduism. We also aim to raise awareness about social, political and religious issues affecting Hindus in the 21st century.
summary: The BU Hindu Cultural Association mission is to foster an understanding of Hindu traditions, to address issues affecting Hindus, and to promote discourse between the BU Hindu community and others of all genders, races, and religions.

name: Boston University Bhangra
description: Boston University Bhangra is a competitive, all-inclusive bhangra team, founded in 1999, that is dedicated to raising awareness about South Asian culture both on and off campus through a folk Indian dance form, bhangra. The team looks to foster a safe space for members of all genders, ages, and backgrounds to become not just skilled dancers, but moreover, confident artists.
summary: Boston University Bhangra is a competitive, all-inclusive bhangra team, founded in 1999, that is dedicated to raising awareness about South Asian culture both on and off campus through a folk Indian dance form, bhangra.

name: American Society of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology
description:
The American Society of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology is a national organization that works to construct a network of undergraduate students and faculty for the advancement of research, education, and science outreach.

 

Our student chapter provides networking and career development opportunities at regional and national conferences and access to research and science outreach through grant and award funding to all of our members.

 

If you want to learn more about what we do, check out our website and social media linked down below. For those who want to join, simply email us at asbmbu@bu.edu or wait for our info sessions at the beginning of each semester. Chemistry, BMB, Biology CMG, and related majors are all welcome, and feel free to contact us if you have any questions!
summary: The American Society of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology works to construct a national network of undergraduate students and faculty for the advancement of research, education, and science outreach. Our mission is to provide networking  and career de...

name: Aural Fixation
description: Founded in 1997, Aural Fixation is a treble-voiced a cappella group at Boston University. We perform original arrangements of the best that the music world has to offer. From throwbacks to power ballads and everything in between, we take pride in our ability to find the meaning behind the songs we sing. To us, the act of making music is an opportunity to celebrate each other, while empowering all minoritized groups. Aural Fixation is built upon making great music and life-long friendships. Check us out on YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, and our website!! http://auralfixationbu.com/
summary: BU's Treble-Voiced A Cappella Group

name: Hillel Students Organization
description: Hillel, the center for Jewish life on campus, is the largest, most exciting, and most versatile organization at Boston University. Students work hard to build connections and strengthen relationships with other campus communities. They have made Hillel an integral part of BU's social, cultural, educational, political, and religious life.
summary: Hillel, the center for Jewish life on campus, is the largest, most exciting, and most versatile organization at Boston University. Students work hard to build connections and strengthen relationships with other campus communities.

name: Allegrettos
description: The Allegrettos are a student-run, all-gender a cappella group at Boston University. Founded in 1994, the group performs throughout the northeast region, records music, and competes in competitions such as the ICCAs. In 2016, the Allegrettos performed for President Barack Obama and First Lady Michelle Obama at the White House! The Allegrettos bring a unique blend of alternative-pop, R&B, and gospel influence to their repertoire. Passionate about recording and sharing their artistry, the group has released a growing catalog of EPs, albums, and singles—available on all major streaming platforms. Frequent competitors in the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella (ICCA), The Allegrettos have a long list of awards, most recently earning 2nd Place in the 2024 ICCA Northeast Quarterfinals and advancing to the Semifinals, winning outstanding soloist and choreography.
summary: The Allegrettos are a student-run, all-gender a cappella group at Boston University. Founded in 1994, the group performs throughout the northeast region, records music, and competes in competitions such as the ICCAs.

name: Bulletproof Funk
description: Bulletproof Funk is Boston University's one-of-a-kind dance group dedicated to street dance. We focus on breaking (b-boying and b-girling also known as breakdancing), popping, and locking but do not limit ourselves to these styles. Although we love to perform and put a lot of effort into producing the best shows that we can, we created our group primarily as a way for us to individually practice and hone our skills. This explains the "open group, invite only performance" mentality that we hold. We encourage those who are interested in learning any of the aforementioned dance styles to come to our practices and talk to any of the members, as we are always willing to help others learn. We have performed at many colleges and events, usually combining with Boston College's Conspiracy Theory dance group under the moniker "Bulletproof Theory." Our past performances range from student group cultural shows to opening up for Boston rap icons Mr. Lif and Akrobatik.
summary: Bulletproof Funk is Boston University's one-of-a-kind dance group dedicated to street dance. We focus on b-boying (breakdancing), popping, and locking but do not limit ourselves to these styles.

name: On Tap
description: BU’s only tap dancing group! We are a small group of passionate tap dancers that bring talent from all over to form this close community. We are a student run, student choreographed performing group that rehearses twice a week. We perform in numerous dance shows on campus, as well as our annual showcase at the end of the year.
summary: Boston University's only tap dancing group!  We are a student run, student choreographed performing group that rehearses twice a week.  We perform in numerous dance shows on campus, as well as our own show at the end of the year.

name: South Campus Hall Council
description: South Campus Residence Hall Association is an organization built to direct and guide matters affecting student life in the South Campus residence halls in cooperation with the administration of the college and the apartment/residence hall councils. It is dedicated to planning and implementing programs to encourage community, education and development, and works to promote better communication and understanding between residence hall students and the college administration. South Campus Residence Hall Association is committed to hosting new, exciting, as well as traditional events throughout the academic year to build a sense of community among its residents.

name: Sigma Kappa
description:
Welcome to Boston University's chapter of Sigma Kappa! We strive to empower women and our community through various philanthropic and sisterhood-building events. As the oldest existing chapter of Sigma Kappa, the Delta Chapter is dedicated to the values established by our founders – friendship, service and loyalty, and personal growth. As members of the Greek community at BU, we are also dedicated to putting forth concerted efforts on expanding diversity, equity, and inclusion within our chapter. 

We're always happy to answer any questions, and invite you to browse our Instagram (@sigmakappabu) for our chapter's latest information! If you are interested in recruitment or have questions about what it's like to be a Sigma Kappa, please do not hesitate to email us (sigkapprez@gmail.com) for more information!
summary: At Sigma Kappa, we strive to empower womxn and our community through our core values-- friendship, service, loyalty, and personal growth. If you are interested in Fall Informal Recruitment or have questions about our chapter, please reach out to us!.....

name: Film Society
description:
BUFS is dedicated to bringing you the best movies that the world has to offer, whether it's an intense foreign drama or lighthearted comedy. With an emphasis on laid-back meetings, screenings, and discussion, BUFS is as much a social club as it is a movie club. BUFS welcomes film majors, film fans, or even anyone just mildly interested in movies; all are welcome! So come join us this year: watch some movies and have some fun! 

Please email us at thebufilmsociety@gmail.com so we can put you on our email list! We meet weekly on Wednesdays at 7pm.
summary: BUFS is dedicated to bringing you the best movies that the world has to offer, whether it's a intense foreign drama or lighthearted comedy. We aim to introduce members to new films but also to allow members to rewatch old favorites with the club.

name: Tau Beta Sigma
description: Tau Beta Sigma is a multi-gendered national sorority founded to help promote and serve college and university bands across the country. As members we strive to help the Director of Bands with any work that will help to further the program and also always attempt to demonstrate leadership, a positive attitude, and enthusiasm at any band events or activities. Our primary goal is to help every band member have the most positive musical and social experiences possible here at BU.
summary: Tau Beta Sigma is a multi-gendered national sorority founded to help promote and serve college and university bands across the country.

name: Reformed University Fellowship at Boston University
description: RUF is a Christian community at BU open to students from all backgrounds. We believe in genuine friendships, sincere pursuit of spiritual growth, and positive engagement with the campus and world. Join us at one of our regular events!
summary: RUF is a Christian community at BU open to students from all backgrounds. We believe in genuine friendships, sincere pursuit of spiritual growth, and positive engagement with the campus and world. Join us at one of our regular events!

name: Her Campus Boston University
description: Her Campus is the leading media platform dedicated to empowering college women around the globe. Founded by and for college women, Her Campus is your ultimate destination for navigating the unique experiences that come with this transformative stage in life, whether it’s curating the trendiest dorm on campus, landing your dream internship, or prioritizing your mental health while balancing a crazy-packed schedule. Her Campus Boston University is a community that uplifts, informs, and connects college women on topics like pop culture, wellness, style, politics, health, and more. Check us out online at HerCampus.com/BU for more information. Follow us on Instagram or Twitter: @HerCampusBU.
summary: Her Campus is the number one media site in the United States for college women, by college women.

name: Psi Chi
description: Psi Chi is the National Honor Society in Psychology, founded in 1929 for the purposes of encouraging, stimulating, and maintaining excellence in scholarship, and advancing the science of psychology. The Boston University Chapter of Psi Chi, the International Honor Society in Psychology, is an organization of Psychology students interested in maintaining excellence in scholarship, as well as volunteering and coming together to help the community and to advance achievements in the field of Psychology. The BU chapter holds meetings each semester and participates in activities, such as hosting guest speakers, coordinating fund-raising and social events, etc. Psi Chi also collaborates with the Department's Undergraduate Psychology Association to sponsor various events throughout the year. Membership is open to both graduate and undergraduate students who are concentrating in Psychology and who meet the minimum qualifications which are: - successful completion of 3 semesters for undergraduates, or 1 semester for graduate students - completion of a minimum of 3 psychology courses - an overall GPA of 3.3 - a psychology GPA of 3.0 For questions and other concerns about the application and/or the organization, please email psichi@bu.edu. We look forward to your interest and expanding the Boston University Chapter!
summary: Psi Chi is the National Honor Society in Psychology, founded in 1929 for the purposes of encouraging, stimulating, and maintaining excellence in scholarship, and advancing the science of psychology. The Boston University Chapter of Psi Chi, the Interna...

name: Dance Theatre Group
description: Dance Theatre Group, a Boston University student organization established in 1972, is dedicated to building a community that values dance as an art form. Each semester, DTG offers weekly master classes in a variety of genres taught by the professionals in the greater Boston dance community. DTG also produces an end-of-semester performance that showcases student and faculty choreography. Throughout the semester, choreographers meet weekly with their casts and strive to create pieces that focus on non-verbal self-expression. Choreographers are also given the opportunity to benefit from mentor feedback at several points throughout the semester, learning to incorporate suggestions from three mentors with professional dance experience into their pieces. DTG gives its members the chance to not only grow as technicians but to also develop one’s artistic voice. We are BU students that are passionate about dance, and we relish the chance to do what we love and share it with each other and our audience! For more information, visit the following pages: Fitrec Website: http://www.bu.edu/fitrec/dance/dance-theater-group/ Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/budtg Instagram: @bostonudtg Tik Tok: @bostonudtg If you have any questions, please e-mail DTG at dtg@bu.edu!
summary: Dance Theatre Group, a Boston University student organization established in 1972, is dedicated to building a community that values dance as an art form. Each semester, DTG offers weekly master classes in a variety of genres taught by professionals.

name: Alzheimer's Buddies
description:
Alzheimer's is a prominent disease in the United States, affecting over 5 million Americans, with no cure.

As a member of this organization, you will have the opportunity to interact with residents at a local memory care facility, to reflect on these experiences in journals and group meetings, and to discuss the latest developments in Alzheimer’s research.
summary: Alzheimer's Buddies is dedicated to connecting Boston University students with dementia residents in order to build friendships that reduce the social isolation experienced by this population.

name: Theta Tau
description: Theta Tau is a professional co-ed engineering fraternity founded at the University of Minnesota in 1904. It is the oldest, largest, and foremost fraternity for engineers that has grown to over 35,000 members and 91 chapters and colonies across the nation since its inception. The purpose of Theta Tau is to instill and maintain a high standard of professionalism among its members, embrace a strong bond of fraternal fellowship, and foster the intellectual pursuits of engineering.

name: Taiwanese Overseas Students Association
description: The main purpose of Taiwanese Overseas Students Association (TOSA) is to provide Taiwanese international students service, advice, and networking opportunities and share Taiwanese culture on BU campus. We assist students with Taiwanese background to adapt to American culture, engage with domestic American students, and enrich their college experience. We create opportunities to enhance the relationship between TOSA members and the BU community. While we present cultural events and activities to all BU students that stimulate a better understanding of Taiwanese culture, we also add cultural diversity to Boston University.
summary: The main purpose of Taiwanese Overseas Students Association (TOSA) is to provide Taiwanese international students service, advice, and networking opportunities and share Taiwanese culture on BU campus. We assist students with Taiwanese background to ad...

name: Dheem: Indian Classical Arts Association
description: Dheem is Boston University's Premier Competitive Award Winning Indian Classical Dance Team. Dheem has been established in the Boston area for more than a decade. Performing in the styles of Bharatnatyam, Odissi, and Kathak, Dheem aims to preserve and celebrate its cultural heritage and raise awareness about relevant social justice issues. Through Boston University and surrounding colleges, Dheem provides a setting for classical dancers to collaborate and foster their respective dance forms.
summary: Dheem, Boston University's Classical Indian Arts Association, is dedicated to preserving classical dance and music forms at Boston University. Dheem acts as a forum for students who are interested in practicing and preserving these differently forms.

name: Turkish Student Association
description: Boston University Turkish Student Association revamped itself to provide services to BU students. Our goal is to bring students together, enjoy Turkish culture, and build long lasting friendships that will transcend borders.
summary: Boston University Turkish Student Association revamped itself to provide services to BU students. Our goal is to bring students together, enjoy Turkish culture, and build long lasting friendship that will transcend borders.

name: Project Sunshine
description:
Project Sunshine is a nonprofit organization that provides free educational, recreational, and social programs to children facing medical challenges as well as their families. 

We contribute to their cause in helping pediatric patients and their families through many of the activities and service trips we take in Boston. Such activities include creating Sunshine Activity kits for patients in the hospital and making winter holiday cards. We also take service trips to Boston Medical Center to work with outpatient children and Boston House to make dinner for the families staying while their children are treated at local Boston hospitals.
summary: Project Sunshine is a nonprofit organization that provides free educational, recreational, and social programs to children facing medical challenges as well as their families.

name: Edge Dance Company
description: The Edge Dance Company at Boston University was founded in 2001 and has been a leading jazz/lyrical group on campus. Edge is a group of technically trained dancers with roots in "studio style" dance and performance/competition experience. Edge intends to promote the art of dance at BU and in the surrounding community while continuing to improve their skills as dancers. Follow our Instagram page @bu_edge and look for us at Splash and at Auditions in the fall!
summary: The Edge Dance Company at Boston University was founded in 2001 and has been a leading jazz/lyrical group on campus. Edge is a group of technically trained dancers with roots in "studio style" dance and performance/competition experience.

name: Off The Cuff Magazine
description: Off The Cuff is Boston University's only award-winning fashion, arts, and culture publication. During the year, we do photoshoots and magazines that train creatives in fashion, makeup, photography, art direction, etc.
summary: Off The Cuff is Boston University's only award-winning fashion, arts, and culture publication.

name: Photography Club
description: Boston University's photography club started in 1997 as a group of amateur photographers who wanted to share their passion with similarly minded people. BUPC hosts a large variety of events for its members such as photo walks around campus as well as one larger event, like a trip outside of Boston, each fall and spring. Also, we host speakers to talk about some of the issues surrounding photography and we hold workshops revolved around both digital and analog skills. The club is a great resource for all photographers, and all are welcome regardless of talent, interest, or gear! So come by any of our upcoming meetings or email us at photobu@bu.edu for more information!
summary: The Boston University Photography Club started in 1997 as a group of amateur photographers who wanted to share their ideas about their art with others who shared their passion. The BU Photo Club hosts a large variety of events for its members such as p...

name: Pre-Physician Assistant Club
description: The Boston University Pre-Physician Assistant Club is a unique undergraduate student group that focuses on providing students with opportunities to gain knowledge and experiences in the medical field through community service and by shadowing PAs. Our mission is to inform students of the history and the purpose of the profession, provide information about the admission requirements for PA schools and on the PA application process.
summary: The Boston University Pre-Physician Assistant Club is a unique undergraduate student group that focuses on providing students with opportunities to gain knowledge and experiences in the medical field through community service and by shadowing PAs.

name: Fusion
description:
Established in 1993, Fusion is one of Boston University's dance troupes. Comprised of approximately 30 talented BU students, Fusion acts as a source of creative expression and personal growth through dance.

Fusion provides a space for its members, members of the BU community, and beyond to grow and learn as dancers through consistent training and various performance opportunities. By constantly encouraging ingenuity, originality, and a sense of community, Fusion fosters lasting relationships between strong and versatile dancers.
summary: Established in 1993, Fusion is Boston University's leading on-campus hip-hop dance troupe. Comprised of many talented individuals, Fusion acts as a source of creative expression and learning through dance. We inspire audiences by maintaining a standard.

name: G-Power
description: G Power(GP) is willing to try various type of dance and infuse them together to create something new, something we feel and what we want to present to all the audience. From power jazz, to hip hop, to k-pop, we are willing and also able to show a unique side of girls. The most crucial thing about GP is that we, as a club, are only open to female dancers, and intend to help every girl who wants to be confident and stunning not only on stage as a performer but also in life as an independent and strong woman. We provide a perfect platform for those who love dancing and performing, make all the members more confident and skilled. To GP, there is no obstacles and impossibility. To us, everything is possible, and we believe in the potential in each one of the girls who love dancing. To us, G stands for Girls, for gifted and for Gorgeous. We dance, to express who we are. We fight, for our dreams. G-Power is not only a name of us but also the spirit of who we are.
summary: G-Power (GP) is a dance team dedicated to exploring and blending diverse dance styles—from power jazz and hip hop to K-pop—to create bold and expressive performances.

name: Phi Chi Theta Business Fraternity
description: Phi Chi Theta Zeta Chapter is a professional co-ed business fraternity for all majors and minors at Boston University. Originally established in the 1920s, the Zeta chapter was one of the eight original founding national chapters of Phi Chi Theta. Unlike other professional organizations on campus, our mission centers on social and digital entrepreneurship, providing the professional and social resources needed to navigate the startup world. Like a startup, we do not limit ourselves to business-minded individuals. Our commitment to academic and professional diversity is evident amongst our organization. We currently boast ten separate majors across five colleges: Questrom, CAS, COM, SAR, and ENG. We also differentiate ourselves via our focus on utilizing management and business as a channel for fostering positive social change. Our organization's emphasis on technology and ethical leadership parallels the Questrom School of Business' newly revised curriculum, implemented in 2013.
summary: Phi Chi Theta Zeta Chapter is a professional co-ed business and technology fraternity for all majors and minors at Boston University. Originally established in the 1920s, the Zeta chapter was one of the eight original founding national chapters of PCT.

name: Physical Therapy Association (Graduate) - 32375
description: BUPTA is an organization that consists of all students involved in the Doctor of Physical Therapy program here at Boston University. We organize social events for the PT students, get involved in the local community, and raise money for the nationwide Marquette Challenge which funds physical therapy research and is sponsored by the Foundation for Physical Therapy. We strive to keep our members in touch with ways to better themselves and their future careers in the area of physical therapy by providing educational seminars and opportunities.
summary: BUPTA is an organization that consists of all students involved in the Doctor of Physical Therapy program here at Boston University. We organize social events for the PT students, get involved in the local community, and raise money for the nationwide ...

name: Women in Computer Science
description: We are dedicated to creating a supportive community for young computer scientists of all genders and deepening awareness and interest in the many areas of CS. We feature speakers on a variety of CS topics, host career-related events, and bring together and collaborate with individuals passionate about computer science, math, engineering, business, and related fields. Computer science touches so many facets of our lives - from the virtual assistants on our smartphones to the algorithms running our medical equipment to the software that sends rockets into the stars - that it’s impossible to not be “into computers.” We hope that we can help all students, regardless of gender, to see that computer science can be for everyone - dreamers, tinkerers, coders, and thinkers - and to discover all that CS has to offer.
summary: WiCS encourages women to participate fully in Computer Science by offering a supportive community, mentorship, social activities, networking opportunities and professional events.

name: Spoon University
description: Spoon University provides students with the chance to learn, share and talk about food via an online food publication, ultimately building a community around food. We aim to take advantage of Boston’s incredible food scene and share some of our experiences through photographs, articles and social media. Our members are always sharpening their writing, photography, marketing and social media skills; all valuable experience that can be placed on their resume. BU’s chapter of Spoon is one of many around the world! Spoon University has three main goals: (1) to empower students to create something meaningful and long-lasting on campus (2) to give students experience in food, journalism, online media and marketing (3) to create a strong community on campus focused on food. There is a $15 fee for each member, which is justified by the many benefits Spoon University provides. As a member, you have the chance to get exclusive access to food events and promotional giveaways and gain national recognition for your hard work and content that is published. We’ve worked with many different restaurants in Boston, whether it was an icing class with Magnolia or a promotional partnership with Jugos Supremo, our members are sure to experience and explore the food of Boston!
summary: Spoon University is an online food publication open to writers, photographers, and marketers. We aim to cultivate a food community on campus and in the city of Boston, while teaching members real-world skills.

name: Saudi Cultural Club
description: The Saudi Cultural Club brings together Saudi and non-Saudi students who share a deep interest in Saudi Arabia’s rich culture and history. Our mission is to build a vibrant, inclusive community through engaging events, traditional and modern cultural celebrations, and career-focused initiatives. We strive to foster a strong sense of belonging, mutual understanding, and meaningful connection among our members. By celebrating heritage, promoting dialogue, and encouraging collaboration, we aim to keep the community connected and create a lasting impact on campus.
summary: We unite Saudi and non-Saudi students through cultural events, celebrations, and career-building opportunities. By promoting belonging, understanding, and meaningful connections, we create a vibrant, inclusive community that celebrates Saudi heritage.

name: Global Engineering Brigade
description:
Global Brigades is an international nonprofit that empowers communities around the world to meet their health and economic goals through university volunteers. Since water is fundamental for everyday activities, without sufficient clean water the health of entire communities is affected. The engineering chapter focuses on the design and implementation of water systems in rural communities to address these dire needs of families that have little or no access to clean water. Each year GEngB goes on a Brigade in country and/ or virtually to work with communities and local experts to design and implement a clean water system.

We also focus on local outreach and attend volunteering events in the Boston area that are involved with food banks, trash clean ups and much more! Join us and look through our website: https://bugengb.wixsite.com/website
summary: Global Brigades is an international nonprofit that empowers communities around the world to meet their health and economic goals through university volunteers.

name: 180 Degrees Consulting
description: 180 Degrees Consulting is the world’s largest student-run consultancy, dedicated to helping nonprofits and social enterprises improve their services, expand their reach, and ultimately maximize their impact. We are a team of motivated, enterprising students from diverse academic backgrounds, united by our passion to make a difference. 180 DC associates not only gain practical experience and first-hand exposure to Boston’s social enterprises but also receive professional training from major consulting firms. By connecting passionate individuals and fully equipping them to find effective and innovative solutions to real-world challenges, we develop a community of leaders and social-thinkers. For more information, sign up for our mailing list here or navigate to our website or Instagram: @180dcboston.
summary: 180 Degrees Consulting is the world’s largest student-run consultancy, dedicated to helping nonprofits and social enterprises maximize their impact. For more information on how to get involved, sign up for our mailing list.

name: Chess Club
description:
The purpose of BU Chess is to act as a community hub for chess enthusiasts at Boston University. The club will provide chess resources and a place to play for undergraduates.

 

BU Chess aims to promote the game of chess to the Boston University community, provide a friendly environment for chess players to engage, and formulate a BU Chess Team to compete in the Collegiate Chess League, other Collegiate Tournaments, and regional tournaments. 

 

Follow us on instagram: https://www.instagram.com/buchessclub/
summary: The purpose of BU Chess is to act as a community hub for chess enthusiasts at Boston University. The club will provide chess resources (such as chess textbooks, chess boards, etc.) for undergraduates

name: Graduate Taiwanese Student Association - 23300
description: Boston University Taiwanese Student Association (BUTSA) is an organization that is dedicated to promote Taiwanese culture and history for graduate students. Our main purpose is to unite students with interest in Taiwanese backgrounds to come together through various events. Some previous events include, job-search workshops, barbecues, and cultural festival celebration. We welcome not only those with Taiwanese background, but for all those who are interested in Taiwanese culture. Studying and enjoying the experience in USA are our main goals here at Boston University. Join BUTSA and have fun! We welcome people of all backgrounds and cultures. BU is the place to Be You! We are looking forward to see you all!
summary: Boston University Taiwanese Student Association (BUTSA) is an organization that is dedicated to promote Taiwanese culture and history for BU graduate students.

name: Center for Gender Sexuality and Activism
description: The CGSA is an open student-run safe space in the basement of the GSU. Each evening, a variety of student groups meet in the space. Free resources available to each and every student include condoms, tampons, bagels every Friday, arts and crafts supplies, a well-stocked library, the Womb Room, and plenty of comfortable seating.
summary: The CGSA is an open student-run space in the basement of the GSU. Each evening, a variety of student groups meet in the space. Free resources are available to every student, as well as comfortable study and sitting space.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc.
description: Epsilon Chapter became Massachusetts’ first undergraduate chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated when it was chartered on May 3, 1924 at Boston University. Since 1924, Epsilon immediately began its legacy of outstanding service and leadership through its innovative programs geared towards solving socio-economic issues within the community. With enthusiasm, strength and pride, the chapter continued to push Alpha Kappa Alpha into the public eye as a premier service organization dedicated to serving all of Mankind. As the Greater Boston City Chapter, the Everloving, Everlasting Epsilon Chapter is dedicated to serving its constituents. It is composed of Babson College, Bentley College, Boston College, Boston University, Brandeis University, Emerson College, Emmanuel College, Pine Manor College, Simmons College, Suffolk University and Tufts University.
summary: Epsilon Chapter became Massachusetts’ first undergraduate chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated when it was chartered on May 3, 1924 at Boston University. Since 1924, Epsilon immediately began its legacy of outstanding service and leaders...

name: Alpha Phi
description: Founded in 1872 by ten forward thinking women at Syracuse University, Alpha Phi is now one of the oldest and largest women's fraternities in North America. The Eta Chapter of Alpha Phi was founded in 1883 and recently celebrated its 125th anniversary at Boston University. Alpha Phi is one of the largest sororities on Boston University's campus. Although sororities do not have houses at BU, many sisters choose to live together on and off campus. Alpha Phi is visible on campus through attendance at sporting, philanthropy and greek events in addition to hosting multiple philanthropy events. Our three largest annual events are Ivy Man, Alpha Phifa, and The Red Dress Brunch, benefit the Alpha Phi Foundation and the American Heart Association. Alpha Phis are not only philanthropic, but scholarly too! Alpha Phi hosts study hours every week and have an alumni mentorship program which connects current members with alumna.
summary: Founded in 1872 by ten forward thinking women at Syracuse University, Alpha Phi is now one of the oldest and largest women's fraternities in North America. The Eta Chapter of Alpha Phi was founded in 1883 and recently celebrated its 125th anniversary

name: Undergraduate World Languages & Literatures Association
description: As a member of BUWA, you'll be a part of the diverse community that is World Literatures & Languages. We aim to bridge the gap between students spread out across the department, as well as facilitating communication between undergraduates and faculty. Becoming a resource for students looking to further their academic and/or career-oriented goals is also one of our priorities. We like to think of our group as a meeting point for all students passionate about good books, foreign languages, and discovering a global community.
summary: As a member of BUWA, you'll be a part of the diverse community that is World Literatures & Languages. We aim to bridge the gap between students spread out across the department, as well as facilitating communication between undergraduates and faculty.

name: Sisters United
description:
Sisters United strives to empower young Black and Brown women at Boston University through the provision of mentorship opportunities, on-campus engagements, and off-campus excursions.

Be sure to follow us on instagram @sistersunitedbu and subscribe to our newsletter to stay updated with all our events and general body meetings!
summary: Sisters United strives to empower young Black and Brown women at Boston University through the provision of mentorship opportunities, on-campus engagements, and off-campus excursions.

name: Real Estate Club
description: BUREC is at the forefront of real estate education and home to BU’s student managed real estate fund. Members gain a deep understanding of various facets of the industry, positioning them for success in private equity, development, or other investment-related businesses. Please contact bureclub@bu.edu for more information.
summary: The Boston University Undergraduate Real Estate Club strives to educate the Questrom School of Business and greater Boston University undergraduate community about the real estate industry by creating opportunities for learning and professional network.

name: Lambda Chi Alpha
description: Lambda Chi Alpha at Boston University is a fraternity committed to fostering leadership, academic excellence, and a strong sense of brotherhood. Our members engage in various philanthropic efforts, campus activities, and social events, creating a well-rounded and supportive community. We pride ourselves on upholding the values of loyalty, duty, respect, and service, providing an enriching college experience that extends beyond graduation. Join us to be part of a brotherhood that empowers you to grow and succeed.
summary: Lambda Chi Alpha was founded here at Boston University by a law student named Warren A. Cole. We are the Alpha Chapter. As a fraternity we seek to offer the best fraternal experience, focusing on personal character development through leadership, schol..

name: Delta Phi Epsilon International
description:
Delta Phi Epsilon International is an almost century old International Business & Foreign Service Society founded in 1919 at Georgetown University. Boston University's organization was founded in 1922 through the School of Business Administration. The society fosters a community of professionals who want to pursue an international career in business trade & development, economic policy, international relations, law, journalism etc. At Boston University we aim to help young students develop professional skills and become future world leaders, as well as advocates of world justice, peace, & prosperity. The society also seeks to develop a strong community of peers through a social and professional network of like-minded individuals.

http://www.dpebostonu.com/

https://www.instagram.com/dpebostonu/ 

Primary Contact: Maia Lane, president.dpe.bu@gmail.com
summary: Delta Phi Epsilon International is an almost century old International Business & Foreign Service Society founded in 1919 at Georgetown University. Boston University's organization was founded in 1922 through the School of Business Administration.

name: Kappa Sigma
description: Since established at the University of Virginia on December 10 1869, Kappa Sigma Fraternity has initiated more members than any other fraternal organization in the country. The Mu Psi Chapter of the Kappa Sigma Fraternity was founded at Boston University on November 14th, 1987. We are a values-based men’s fraternity guided by the four pillars of Fellowship, Leadership, Scholarship, and Service. We are currently the largest, most diverse fraternity on campus. We pride ourselves on our various philanthropic efforts across campus and the greater Boston area. With brotherhood at the core of everything we do, our organization cultivates connections and life-long friendships for college and beyond.
summary: The largest and most successful fraternity on campus, Kappa Sigma upholds centuries of tradition through the pillars of fellowship, leadership, scholarship, and service.

name: Financial Modeling Club
description: BU Financial Modeling Club’s primary goal is to bridge the technical gap between formal education and the skills needed to land a job in the finance industry. The club is open to all whether you want to go into private equity, investment banking, or just learn more about excel! BUFMC’s peer-led weekly meetings, and mentorship pairings with alumni and upperclassmen at top companies such as Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, and many more ensure that students are equipped with all of the tools needed to land a top role.
summary: BUFMC’s primary goal is to bridge the technical gap between formal education and the skills needed to land a job in the finance industry. The club is open to all, whether you aspire to a career in private equity, investment banking, or just like Excel!

name: Korean Communication Association
description: Korean Communication Association (KOMMA) works to provide information and networks to its members and all Koreans at Boston University through mass media projects. While its previous projects focused on unilateral communication like magazine, radio, or Instagram trivia, KOMMA began its video projects in Fall 2022. While self-PR through online platforms- both for a company and individual- is crucial for its success, KOMMA's mission is to educate and assist the members in effectively improving their social media skills, focusing on YouTube and Instagram. The use of various media platforms allows students to learn about content-creating programs, gain experience in the field, and build connections.
summary: Korean Communication Association (KOMMA) works to provide information and networks to current and future Korean students at Boston University through mass media projects via YouTube and Instagram.

name: Students for Israel at BU
description:
BU Students for Israel (BUSI) is a student organization dedicated to educating and engaging students in Israeli culture, politics, and history. We host Israel-related events and speakers, lead meaningful discussions on a host of topics, and celebrate Jewish holidays as a community of diverse backgrounds and perspectives. As the only pro-Israel club on campus, we are committed to bringing a wide breadth of political and cultural ideas to the broader Boston University community, while supporting the Jewish homeland.
​
summary: BU Students for Israel is a student organization dedicated to representing and supporting Israel on campus. We plan events with strategic campus partners to educate students about Israel and celebrate Israeli culture.

name: Chemia
description:
Chemia is the undergraduate chemistry student organization at Boston University. Through direct collaboration with the chemistry department, we foster a community for students in the molecular sciences to support each other, provide peer mentorship, and grow as scientists. Ultimately, we aim to guide and support students, share our excitement about chemistry, and expand the community of scientists.

 

We offer free tutoring for all undergraduate chemistry courses, including general, organic, inorganic, biochemistry, and physical chemistry. We also host events such as information sessions about research opportunities and graduate school, industry tours, meet and greets, professional development, and professor talks.

 

Our events are open to anyone with an interest in chemistry. Follow our Instagram @buchemia for the most up-to-date information, or email us at buchemia@bu.edu if you have any questions!
summary: Chemia is the undergraduate chemistry student organization at Boston University. We foster a community for students in the molecular sciences to support each other and host events for students to grow as a chemist. We aim to share our love for chemistry.

name: Latin American Student Association at Boston University
description:
LATAM is a welcoming space for students from all around Latin America. Our goal is to create a sense of community within BU, where Latin American students can meet, connect and make lifelong friends through different social, recreational and professional events.

________

¡Un espacio hecho por Latinos, para Latinos!
summary: LATAM is a welcoming space for people from all around Latin America. Our goal is to create a sense of community within BU, where Latin students can meet build friendships and network for future needs. We will debate, fundraise, and raise awareness.

name: Students for Reproductive Freedom
description: BU Students for Reproductive Freedom advocates for healthy/safer sexuality, sex positivity, abortion access, comprehensive sexuality education, and push for legislation that expands access to reproductive healthcare. As a registered Planned Parenthood campus organizing group, we are responsible for representing them at BU. On campus, we host a variety of events and pass out hundreds of condoms and other sexual health education resources. Reproductive justice is not an isolated movement, and we want to raise awareness about how socioeconomic status, culture, race, gender, sexuality, immigration, environmental issues, etc., all impact autonomy and justice.
summary: BU Students for Reproductive Freedom advocates healthy/safer sexuality, sex positivity, abortion access, comprehensive sexuality education, and push for legislation that expands access to reproductive healthcare.  As a registered Planned Parenthood cam..

name: Sustainable Ocean Alliance
description: The Sustainable Oceans Alliance is a youth-led organization that empowers students to become leaders in preserving the health and sustainability of our oceans. With education, resources and collaborative opportunities, SOA aims to inspire students to create measurable impact by incentivizing social and environmental investments and fostering cross-sector partnerships. Our vision is to provide a platform for innovative cross-sector collaboration through which business practices, effective legislation, and cutting edge technology can catalyze solutions.
summary: The Sustainable Oceans Alliance is a youth-led organization that empowers students to become leaders in preserving the health and sustainability of our oceans.

name: Global China Connection
description: Boston University Global China Connection dedicates to create a career development platform and a professional networking environment for BU students through hosting career skill training sessions and professional summits/panels with corporations around the world. The goal of Boston University Global China Connection is a major gateway between Boston University and China. Boston University Global China Connection will partner with 30 other Global China Connections chapters worldwide to provide opportunities for BU students to network with their peers who share a mutual interest.
summary: Boston University Global China Connection dedicates to create a career development platform and a professional networking environment for BU students through hosting career skill training sessions and professional summits/panels with corporations.

name: Choral Society
description:
BU Choral Society is a non-audition, chorus where singers of ALL levels of experience are welcome! We sing a variety of fun and accessible music, culminating in multiple performances throughout each semester. 

We hope to be a space for you to decompress and experience the joy of making music together. BU Choral Society aims to ignite musical growth, inspire life long friendships, and most importantly to have fun! We hold bonding events throughout the year such as karaoke nights, attending live concerts, and enjoying restaurant outings. 

Follow our Facebook “BU Choral Society,” and our Instagram @buchoralsociety. Contact us at choral@bu.edu if you have any questions!
summary: BU Choral Society is a non-audition, co-ed chorus where people of ALL levels of experience are welcome! We sing a variety of fun and accessible music, culminating in a themed concert at the end of each semester.

name: NMDP
description:
Every three minutes, someone is diagnosed with a life-threatening blood disease such as leukemia. For thousands of these patients, a blood stem cell transplant is their best—or only—hope. However, 70% of patients in need of a transplant do not have a fully matched donor in their family. As a result, patients turn to NMDP (formally known as Be The Match), the non-profit organization that operates the world’s largest and most diverse registry of potential blood stem cell donors. The more registry members, the greater the likelihood that patients will find their lifesaving donor. All it takes to join the registry is a swab!

NMDP is a student-run organization that partners with the national NMDP organization to offer hope to individuals with blood diseases or blood cancers. We seek to increase and diversify the NMDP registry and increase awareness about the importance of cellular therapy. NMDP Campus Chapters was established by the national nonprofit organization NMDP, operated by the National Marrow Donor Program. There are currently over 100 NMDP groups at universities and colleges across the nation with the goal of expanding NMDP’s lifesaving mission—delivering cures for blood diseases. Save lives with us!
summary: We are an organization at Boston University (BU) partnering with the world's largest blood stem cell donor registry, NMDP, which provides lifesaving transplants for patients with blood cancers. Our mission is to diversify and expand the NMDP registry

name: Sports Business Club
description: The Boston University Sports Business Club (BUSBC) is an undergraduate organization for students looking to gain knowledge of and exposure to the business side of the sports industry. This encompasses a variety of functional areas such as sports media/journalism, sports management, sports law, data/analytics, finance, marketing/advertising, and more. BUSBC hosts guest speakers, industry panels, and other sports-oriented events throughout the school year in order to enhance students’ understanding of the various fields available, as well as generate networking opportunities. Additionally, BUSBC has partnered with BU Athletics to form the Sports Consulting Group, a division of the club that provides consulting work for our NCAA Division I teams to give students hands-on experience in the sports industry.
summary: The Boston University Sports Business Club (BUSBC) is an undergraduate organization for students looking to gain knowledge of and exposure to the business side of the sports industry through office visits, guest speakers, and hands-on experience.

name: Undergraduate Architecture Association
description: The UAA is an organization for undergraduate students interested in architecture, design, urbanism, and real estate. Our goal is to foster a community that supports students in furthering their study of the built environment through themed discussions, professional panels and workshops, events, and field trips.
summary: The UAA is an organization for students interested in architecture, design, urbanism, and real estate. Our goal is to foster a community that will support students in furthering their study of the built environment through discussions, panels, and trips.

name: Naturally
description: The Boston University Chapter of BU Naturally founded April 29th, 2019. We are a natural hair based organization that promotes diversity, inclusivity, and love of all natural hair types on campus and in the community.
summary: The Boston University Chapter of BU Naturally founded April 29th, 2019. We are natural hair based organization were we promote diversity, inclusivity, and love of all natural hair types on campus and in the community.

name: Chordially Yours
description: Hi! We're Chordially Yours, a treble-voiced a cappella group founded in 2000 by Beth Heald ('03)! You can call us Chords for short. We believe in using music to empower and celebrate women and nonbinary people. We've taken part (and placed) in a number of competitions and events, including the Faneuil Hall A Cappella Fest, the ICCAs, SheSings (by the Women's A Cappella Association), WGBH's Sing That Thing!, a performance in Downtown Disney, and even opened for Lena Dunham at the Boston stop of her Not That Kind of Girl book tour! We also have three EPs on Spotify and Apple Music, with more to come. If you're interested in learning more about Chords, check out our website chordiallyyours.com and feel free to shoot us an email at chordially.yours@gmail.com! You can also follow us on Facebook, Twitter @ChordiallyYours or Instagram @ChordiallyYoursBU
summary: Hi! We're Chordially Yours, a treble-voiced a cappella group founded in 2000 by Beth Heald ('03)! You can call us Chords for short. We believe in using music to empower and celebrate women and nonbinary people.

name: BosTones
description: Each semester, the BU BosTones brings a fresh energy to the Boston a cappella scene. We are a co-ed group of undergraduates belting out sizzling arrangements and killer harmonies. We have placed first at the Northeast International Collegiate A Cappella semi-final, becoming the first international finalist from BU since 2005. We perform a winter and spring concert each year, plus benefit concerts, guest appearances, campus events, and more! Going forward, we hope to soon record our next studio album and continue to represent Boston University on an international level. Check out our website: thebostones.com, or email us at thebostones@gmail.com! We'd love to hear from you - the bostones!
summary: Each semester, the BU BosTones bring a unique soul and energy to the Boston a cappella scene. We are a co-ed group of undergraduates focused on delivering the hottest arrangements and a lethal sound.

name: Astronomical Society
summary: BUAS unites undergraduates of all majors who are interested in all things astronomy. Join other passionate students in learning about the universe around us through fun events including speakers, games, crafts, and even stargazing or observing trips.

name: Blockchain Club
description: We are an inclusive, multifaceted community dedicated to equal education in the web3 space. We aim to foster creativity, build a diverse network, and shape a decentralized future together. Through research, development, and building companies - BU Blockchain aims to act as a springboard for all students to unleash their potential in the field.
summary: BU Blockchain Club invites all members within the Boston University community to engage themselves within the exciting and emerging market of blockchain, which is growing exceptionally and having more real world applications every single day.

name: College Democrats
description: BUCD seeks to inspire Democratic change in government by motivating young progressives to get involved in the political sphere. We have an engaged presence on the Boston University Campus, organizing action around initiatives from the national to university level. BUCD commits to action in and around Boston, Massachusetts, and the country, pushing for value-based change. We have meetings every Thursday at 7 pm on campus to discuss, debate, and educate ourselves on local and national politics, while engaging in civic action to make an impact. Join our slack!
summary: BUCD seeks to inspire Democratic change by motivating young progressives to get involved. We meet weekly where we have everything from speakers to debates and political action. We are a community of Democrats working as a force for positive change.

name: Jains In Voice and Action
description: JIVA: Jains In Voice and Action is Boston University's Jain student initiative, founded a couple years ago by Jainy Savla, '07 and Purvi Shah '06. JIVA's goals are to increase awareness of the Jain way of life in the university community, promote Jain ideals among the student body, and foster friendships among Jain students. JIVA organizes many educational and interactive activities for its members including guest speakers at BU, temple trips, Jain discussions, celebration of Jain festivals, and frequent social events.
summary: JIVA: Jains In Voice and Action is Boston University's Jain Student Initiative. JIVA aims to increase awareness about the Jain way of Life, promote Jain ideals among the student body, and foster friendships among Jain students.

name: Diversity in Law Association
description: The BU Diversity in Law Association (DLA) strives to create a space where students, particularly minority students, can learn about how law can affect their lives and how they can contribute to society through a legal career. As members of this organization, we would have the opportunity to hear professionals in the legal field share their experiences relating to the law and social justice issues. DLA will also disseminate information on career opportunities, scholarships, and law school preparation. Members can also take part in group visits to courthouses, law offices, and other venues related to the organization’s purpose. DLA needs the participation of passionate pre-law students, activists, and other individuals interested in promoting diversity in law.
summary: The BU Diversity in Law Association (DLA) strives to create a space where students, particularly minority students, can learn about how law can affect their lives and how they can contribute to society through a legal career. As members of this organiz...

name: In Achord
description:
Established in 1990, In Achord is Boston University's premiere all-gender a cappella group. Recently, In Achord has been featured on the BOCA 2021 and 2023 (Best of College A Cappella) albums. In Achord competes in a cappella tournaments around the nation, most notably, the International Competition of Collegiate A Cappella (ICCA). In 2016, In Achord won the ICCA Northeast Quarterfinal hosted at the Berklee College of Music, and then went on to place fourth at the Northeast Semifinal while getting the opportunity to sing on Boston Symphony Hall's stage.
