In addition to being a competitive a cappella group, In Achord is also a family, and loves to have fun along the way! In their free time, the members of In Achord can always be found studying, hanging out, and exploring Boston together. The group also loves to gig throughout the school year, whether that be performing with groups at neighboring universities, singing at weddings, or hosting our very own Beanpot of A Cappella every spring. 

In Achord strives to be creative with our music while having a good time, emphasis on the good time. Over our 30 years as a student group at Boston University, In Achord has recorded several studio albums, with our most recent EP Theoretically, Forever. We pride ourselves on our R&B & Pop-inspired repertoire, our in-house arrangements, and our talented soloists. With a set ranging from Beyoncé to Allen Stone to Hozier to Couch, In Achord loves to perform at all events!
summary: Established in 1990, In Achord is Boston University's oldest (OG if you will) all-gender acappella group. The group is a semiannual host and competitor in the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella (ICCA).

name: Boston Political Review
description:
The Boston Political Review is Boston University’s premier political publication founded in 2014.​

We are a nonpartisan student-run platform for the BU community to express its voice and to give our readers a refreshing source of unbiased information. 

The BPR typically consists of five sections: Global Politics, U.S. Politics, Local Politics, Social & Business. and Health & Science. ​

Our writing staff strikes a harmonious balance between a typical news story and an academic paper, as their articles contain elements of traditional student journalism and analytical deep dives.

The BPR is committed to creating an informed student body and maintaining a publication with integrity that upholds the key tenets of student journalism.
summary: The Boston Political Review is a platform for undergrad and grad students to produce non-partisan work covering politics. The review's combination of academic and traditional articles has garnered the attention of thousands of viewers across the globe.

name: Eritrean and Ethiopian Students Association
description:
We want to extend our warmest welcome to the Boston University Class of 2029!

Our organization promotes cultural awareness of diverse ethnic backgrounds and provides a welcoming space on campus for Eritrean and Ethiopian students. Through supporting grassroots projects and activism in our Eritrean and Ethiopian American community, as well as giving back through partnerships with local non-profit organizations, we foster unity, active participation, acceptance, and integration among all Eritrean and Ethiopian Boston University students.  We wish to educate members about the different aspects of Eritrean and Ethiopian culture through showcasing talents and performances (via our dance team), and inform the student body of social justice matters that are relevant to our world today. Ultimately, we aim to act as a collective voice for students and foster respect through uniting the voices of our diverse Eritrean and Ethiopian community.  

Follow and DM us on insta to connect with the community, or if you have any questions about BU! instagram.com/bu_eesa
summary: Promoting cultural awareness of the diverse ethnic backgrounds of Eritrea and Ethiopia while providing a welcoming space on campus for students descended from these countries. Check out our instagram and connect!

name: College Republicans at Boston University
description:
The Boston University College Republicans (BUCR) is a student organization dedicated to promoting conservative values and fostering a free marketplace of ideas on campus. Our weekly meetings focus on engaging with current political issues from a Republican perspective, aiming to find common ground and strengthen our understanding of differing viewpoints.

We also host a range of events, including debates and watch parties, to unite conservative students and create a stronger political culture at BU. Follow us on Instagram for updates on upcoming events and activities!
summary: BUCR hosts weekly meetings and events to promote conservative values and the free marketplace of ideas. We invite all Terriers to engage in discussions on current political topics, aiming to foster understanding in today’s polarized climate.

name: CFA Student Government
description: The Student Government of the College of Fine Arts serves a community of musicians, actors, visual artists, and scholars. We ensure that our students maintain an active voice within the greater BU student body. CFA Student Government plans social and community building events for the college. We serve as a liaison between students, faculty, and administration. Student Government also organizes philanthropic events for its study body. We wish to share with the world the dazzling talents of our musicians, actors, visual artists, and scholars.
summary: The Student Government of the College of Fine Arts serves a community of musicians, actors, visual artists, and scholars. We ensure that our students maintain an active voice within the greater BU student body. CFA Student Government plans social and c...

name: Beta Psi Omega
description: We are a professional co-ed biology fraternity that provides leadership opportunities, expands social networks, and generates a community of equally driven students.
summary: We are a professional co-ed biology fraternity that provides members with leadership opportunities, fosters academic excellence, expands social networks, and generates a community of unique and driven students.

name: Inner Strength Gospel Choir
description: Boston University’s Inner Strength Gospel Choir (ISGC) is an award-winning, non-denominational, student-run organization devoted to glorifying God through song and praise. Established in 1973, ISGC is a diverse choir comprised of forty-plus members from all walks of life, with different experiences and beliefs. We come together each Sunday evening of the academic year to sing songs of thanks and praise and to support and encourage one another in our daily lives. Each year ISGC hosts three large events: Fall Concert, Spring Concert and our Annual Tour. Fall Concert and Spring Concert are held on campus at Marsh Chapel. For our Annual Tour, we travel to various locations around the country to share our ministry through song and community service and to proudly represent the Boston University Community. We've toured in Dallas, South Carolina, New York, SanFransico, and many other cities around the country. If you are interested in joining ISGC or simply want to learn more about the choir, please attend one of our weekly rehearsals. We are a non-audition choir, so everyone is welcome to join. Rehearsals are held each Sunday evening of the academic year from 7:00 to 9:00pm at Marsh Chapel. We'd love for you to meet us, and for us to meet you!
summary: Boston University’s Inner Strength Gospel Choir (ISGC) is an award-winning, non-denominational, student-run organization devoted to glorifying God through song and praise.

name: Board Games Club
description:
Board Games Club is a catch-all organization for all your non-digital gaming needs. If it can be played on a table (or even not), someone in our club will be happy to play it with you! You can also join our mailing list or click here to join our discord to keep up with any future events, meetings, and information related to BGC. You can also follow us on Instagram at @buboardgames. 

 

(1) General meetings are held 7-11pm on Thursdays and are open to BU community members and members of the public with BU connections (i.e., current students, staff, or alumni). Up-to-date information on club meeting location is sent shortly before meetings, both through the mailing list and through Discord.

During general meetings, members are free to play from the library of games maintained by the club or to bring games from their own personal collections. These games typically range from lighter party games  (Codenames, Spyfall, Avalon, etc.) to medium and heavy weight games (Betrayal, Power Grid, Terraforming Mars, etc.).

 

​(2) We try to host Trivia Nights, Escape Rooms, and Murder Mystery Events yearly plus many more events to keep our community engaged and entertained. If any of those events sound interesting to you, you should follow us or join our discord and we will keep you updated!

 

(3) Pitch nights for Tabletop Role Playing Games are held periodically, typically at the beginning of each semester. These events are open to all experience levels and gaming backgrounds and are a great way to jump into the hobby. Interested DM/GMs pitch their campaigns to interested players and are matched into parties based on preferences, schedules, and commitment levels. Other TTRPG offerings are held throughout the semester based on member interest.

​⠀

(4) Card game specific events and meetings are held contingent on member interest. Card games such as Magic: The Gathering, Yu-gi-oh, Netrunner, and Pokemon TCG are often played at general meetings alongside traditional board games, and tournaments for the most popular of these games are held when possible.

​⠀

If you are interested in any other non-digital gaming categories (such as Wargaming, longer/legacy games like Diplomacy, traditional games like Mahjong and Go, etc.), BGC is happy to work with you to promote and organize related events or generate interest at general meetings. Feel free to contact us if you have something specific you’d like to play.
summary: Board Games club is a catch-all organization for all your non-digital gaming needs. If it can be played on a table, someone in our club will be happy to play it with you!

name: Medical Ethics Society
description: The BU Medical Ethics Society is an organization for undergraduates who express interest in the healthcare field. Together we explore the ethics behind science, research, and policies of healthcare. The Society aims to educate young adults beyond the classroom through peer discussion, guest speakers, and much more!
summary: The BU Medical Ethics Society is an organization for undergraduates who express interest in the healthcare field and ethics associated with it.

name: Malaysian Cultural Association
description: Malaysian Cultural Association has a two-pronged approach with community outreach, which is to act as a support system for Malaysian students at Boston University and to introduce and teach about Malaysian culture to the student community.
summary: Malaysian Cultural Association has a two-pronged approach with community outreach, which is to act as a support system for Malaysian students at Boston University and to introduce and teach about Malaysian culture to the student community.

name: Amnesty International Boston University
description: Amnesty International is the world's largest human rights organization that seeks to defend human rights on a global scale and preserve the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. Boston University Amnesty International seeks to raise awareness and educate the wider BU community about human rights abuses and take direct action to protect people whose rights are being violated.
summary: Amnesty International is the world’s largest human rights organization that seeks to protect and defend human rights on a global scale and preserve the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. AIBU is Boston University's chapter of Amnesty International.

name: Upsilon Pi Epsilon
description: Upsilon Pi Epsilon (UPE) at BU is an honor society dedicated to promoting excellence in technical and computing disciplines. UPE at BU will help prepare members for the industry through professional development, meaningful and interesting projects, and a supportive professional network.
summary: Upsilon Pi Epsilon (UPE) at BU is an honor society dedicated to promoting excellence in technical and computing disciplines. UPE at BU will help prepare members for the industry through professional development, meaningful and interesting projects, and...

name: One Voice
description:
One Voice is Boston University's multicultural, multi-genre a cappella group, originally founded as Kol Echad in 1995. As their name implies, they aim to deliver a musical experience that embraces a mix of styles and cultures within the ever-popular collegiate a cappella sound.

 

The group is a mainstay throughout the Northeast and often sings at cultural centers and universities, including Harvard, Brandeis, Rutgers, and Tufts. In 2022 and 2024, the group had the opportunity to compete at the ICCA Northeast Quarterfinals.

​
summary: One Voice is Boston University's multicultural, multi-genre a cappella group, originally founded as Kol Echad in 1995.

name: ENG Student Government
description: ENG Student Government (ENGGOV) is aimed at fostering loyalty, promoting school and class interest, establishing unification among the various classes, and acting as a liaison between students and the administration.
summary: ENG Student Government (ENGGOV) is aimed at fostering loyalty, promoting school and class interest, establishing unification among the various classes, and acting as a liaison between students and the administration.

name: Epsilon Eta
description: Epsilon Eta at Boston University is a multi-gender professional environmental fraternity that promotes a healthy and sustainable environment and connect students to industry, community service, and academia in pursuit of these goals.
summary: Epsilon Eta at Boston University is a multi-gender professional environmental fraternity that promotes a healthy and sustainable environment and connect students to industry, community service, and academia in pursuit of these goals.

name: CGS Student Government
description: The College of General Studies Student Government Association (CGS SGA) is an organization which strives to represent the CGS student body and better connect them to the BU community through events, advocacy and communication. Through voicing the interests, concerns and proposals of the student body, we aim to inform the decision-making of the CGS administration, to encourage the participation of student leaders in policy making, and to bridge the gap between faculty and the student body to create a more cohesive, politically-active, and unified community.
summary: The College of General Studies Student Government Association (CGS SGA) is an organization which strives to represent the CGS student body and better connect them to the BU community through events, advocacy and communication.

name: Mustard Seed
description: Matthew 17:20 says that faith as small as a mustard seed can move mountains. As a collegiate a cappella group, we strive to touch and move mountains in people's hearts. We hope to entertain and inspire our audiences at BU and in the local Boston area through our stories and songs.
summary: Matthew 17:20 says that faith as small as a mustard seed can move mountains. As a collegiate a cappella group, we strive to move mountains in people's hearts. We hope to inspire our audiences at BU and in the local Boston area through story and song.

name: The College of Communication Student Government
description:
The College of Communication Student Government is the governing undergraduate student body of COM. COM Student Government serves as a liaison for students, faculty, administration, and alumni. Our mission is to promote unity, academically and socially, among all COM students and Boston University:

  • We push to create a better environment for our students and faculty for an everlasting effect.
  • We strive to provide students with an effective voice in COM.
  • Create social media content and web series to promote COM and give students an outlet to create.
  • We plan various events throughout the year with the primary focus of promoting DEI and networking opportunities for all COM students. Some of these events in the past have included COMapalooza, the Commonwealth Film Festival (BU's Student Short Film Festival), the Annual COM Town Hall (a platform for COM students and staff to get engaged in discussion), Cheers and a Chat with Dean DiChristina, and more!

Our general meetings are held on a weekly basis and are open to all COM undergraduate students. This Fall 2025, we will have biweekly meetings occurring on Wednesdays from 6:00-7:00 PM in COM 101. So if you're a COM student looking to get involved, COM Student Government is for you! We'd love to meet you and hear your ideas! Don't hesitate to contact us for more information and meeting times at comgov@bu.edu.


Connect with us for more information and updates:

  • Website: https://www.comstugov.com/
  • Instagram: @comstugov
summary: The College of Communication Student Government is the governing undergraduate student body of COM, providing resources and opportunities to all COM students.

name: SAR Student Government
description: Sargent Student Government is an organization for any and all Sargent College undergraduates. Each class within Sargent has a board of officers. Over the course of the year, SAR student government is responsible for organizing events- both recreational and academic. In the past, SAR Student Government has strengthened the bonds between classmates and faculty by organizing events such as Coffee and Conversation with the Dean, Sargent Celtics Night, Sargent Night at the Sox, Formal and guest speakers. Students who participate meet and gain knowledge and advice from each other as well as working with other colleges and faculty. Our mission is to create and strengthen relationships by providing fun and educational events for everyone in Sargent College.
summary: Sargent Student Government is an organization for any and all Sargent College undergraduates. Each class within Sargent has a board of officers. Over the year, SAR StuGov is responsible for organizing events- both recreational and academic.

name: BPM
description:
The name "BPM" not only represents our college organization but also carries a deeper meaning. BPM stands for "Beats Per Minute," which is a fundamental element in music. It refers to the tempo or speed at which a musical piece is played, providing the rhythmic foundation that keeps the music moving and grooving. In choosing this name, we emphasize our dedication to delivering energetic and captivating performances that resonate with our audience and keep their hearts racing in time with the beat.

In doing so, we help students who want to learn how to write their original songs and produce music. We do this by providing music software (FL Studio, Cubase, etc.), hosting information sessions open to BU students, and helping students one-on-one during weekly meeting sessions. We also match members (singers) with producers to collaborate and make their own music together.

Second, we host yearly and semester-based music performances to provide stage presence and experience. We perform a total of 15-20 songs, with more than half of the songs being fully original pieces from our singer-songwriters, producers, sound engineers, and instrumentalists. We host busking performances across the Boston area to gain more stage experience, have fun, and collaborate with other Korean organizations to promote music. The genres change based on the individual preferences of our artists, but the most popular ones are Pop, K-Hiphop, and R&B.

In conjunction with that, we collaborate with other Korean organizations such as KOMMA, QK, and KISA to host events that teach students how easy and fun it can be to produce their own music.

At BPM, we believe in the power of music to transcend boundaries and connect people from diverse backgrounds. Through our performances, we strive to showcase the vibrant sounds of Korean music and culture, while also exploring and celebrating various genres. Our repertoire spans a wide range of musical styles, ensuring that there's something for everyone to enjoy and appreciate.
summary: BPM is a music performance group composed of singers, rappers, instrumentalists, producers, and sound engineers who write, perform, and produce songs of all genres including K-pop, Hip-Hop, and acapella. We aim to spread Korean culture through music.

name: Camp Kesem
description: Kesem is a nationwide community, driven by passionate college student leaders, that supports children through and beyond their parent's cancer. A program of Kesem, Camp Kesem operates free summer camps for children who have been impacted by a parent's cancer. Kesem’s mission of supporting children through and beyond a parent’s cancer, and our vision of ensuring that every child impacted by a parent’s cancer is never alone, can only be realized through an ongoing, steadfast commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion in every part of our organization. Camp Kesem at Boston University was founded in 2017 and held their first camp in August 2018. As of 2020, we support over 50 campers.
summary: Kesem is a community, driven by passionate college student leaders, that supports children through and beyond their parent's cancer. A program of Kesem, Camp Kesem operates free summer camps for children who have been impacted by a parent's cancer.

name: School of Hospitality Administration Student Government
description: The School of Hospitality Administration Student Government (SHAgov) is a forum for the academic and social interests of the students of SHA. SHAgov coordinates programs and events within the school and represents SHA as part of the larger Boston University student community. SHAgov is also the umbrella organization for the various trade associations and student groups organized within the school. Any student pursuing studies in the School of Hospitality Administration is automatically eligible for membership in SHAgov, and we encourage active participation from the student body as we pursue our shared initiatives for our school and its communities.
summary: The School of Hospitality Administration Student Government is a forum for the academic and social interests of the students of SHA. SHAgov coordinates programs and events within the school and represents SHA as part of the larger BU campus.

name: Association of Latino Professionals for America
description:
ALPFA is a national non-profit organization that has become the largest Latino Association for business professionals and students with more than 92,000 members nationwide, this includes 43 professional and over 150 student chapters. ALPFA connects students with hundreds of its Fortune 1000 partners, providing them access to 50,000 paid summer internships. In addition, ALPFA has hosts events at a local, regional, and national level to develop students from college to career-ready professionals through mentorship, leadership training and development, job placement and philanthropy.

Please email us at alpfa@bu.edu or find us on social media @alpfabu for more information!
summary: ALPFA is a national non-profit organization and the largest Latino Association for business professionals and students with 92K+ members nationwide, connections with hundreds of Fortune 1000 partners, and provides access to 50K paid summer internships.

name: Wheelock Student Government
description: Undergraduate students are represented within the Wheelock College of Education and Human Development and Boston University by an active student government. In addition to planning and providing educational, social, and professional functions, the executive board is the voice of undergraduate students in BU Wheelock. Undergraduates, through the student government, are also active participants in the Boston University Student Senate. Visit the BU Wheelock Facebook page or Instagram for more information. To contact the BU Wheelock Student Government, email: sedsg@bu.edu.
summary: Undergraduate students are represented within the Wheelock College of Education and Human Development and Boston University by an active student government. In addition to planning and providing educational, social, and professional functions, . . .

name: LOCK Honorary Service Society
description: The LOCK Honorary Service Society is the oldest student organization at Questrom, established in 1924 by Professor Charles Stratton and Lyman Phillips. Many of the most prominent Questrom affiliates, including former Dean of Questrom Louis E. Lataif and the former Dean Ken Freeman, are LOCK members. LOCK was founded and still operates on the belief that those who have the privilege of scholarship must share what they have gained. LOCK members aim to fulfill that responsibility by providing their peers in Boston University with tutoring help in all required Questrom courses, tutoring nights in the dorms, study halls during finals period, and student success panels at local high schools. LOCK also aspires to foster a sense of community service through volunteering events in the greater Boston community.
summary: LOCK members aim to provide their peers in Boston University with tutoring help in almost all required Questrom courses. LOCK also aspires to foster a sense of community service through volunteering events in Boston.

name: Questrom Student Government
description: As members of the Boston University Questrom School of Business Undergraduate Student Government, our primary responsibility is to the Undergraduate Students in the Questrom School of Business to ensure that we are creating an inclusive environment that enhances the Questrom undergraduate community and student life. This includes 1. Enhancing students’ academic, career, and social experiences, 2. Advocating on behalf of the undergraduate population, and 3. Facilitating communication between Questrom students, faculty, administration, alumni, and the greater Boston University community.
summary: Questrom Student Government's mission is to enhance the undergraduate student experience in the Questrom School of Business to ensure that we are creating an inclusive environment that enhances our undergraduate community and student life.

name: Forté
description: BU Forté, formerly known as the BU Sweethearts, is a tight-knit group that sing and speak up “loud and proud” for what they believe in. Forté strives to create a fun and inclusive space for all women, non-binary, genderqueer and gender non-conforming people who are passionate about music. They stay busy with rehearsing, planning diversity and inclusion conferences, busking on the streets, fundraising, recording music and, most importantly, goofing off and having fun together! Forté's sound reflects the diversity of its members who come from various ethnic backgrounds and have an array of passions in addition to singing. Together, these unique individuals have shaped Forté into becoming the one-of-a-kind a Cappella group it is today.
summary: BU Forté, formerly known as the BU Sweethearts, is a tight-knit group that sings and speaks up for what they believe in. Forté strives to create a fun and inclusive music space for all women, non-binary, genderqueer and gender non-conforming people.

name: Suno
description: "Suno", or "Listen" in Hindi, is Boston University's premiere South Asian/Western Fusion A Cappella team. Established in 2001, its members have been working to create music that blends cultures. Suno’s mix of south asian and western music goes beyond lyrics and notes on a page; it redefines what it means to be part of a team and more importantly, a part of a family. This team has had the privilege of performing and competing all across the country from Sahana 2018 in Los Angelos to All American Awaaz in Chicago last year. However, their recent album “Iss Baar Suno” is their new platform of expressing multiple generations of members and various eras of their music. We also host showcases featuring our team and other a cappella and dance teams from BU and from other universities. Contact us to learn more, and remember: just listen!
summary: "Suno", or "Listen" in Hindi, is Boston University's premiere South Asian/Western Fusion A Cappella team. Established in 2001, its members have been working to create music that mixes south asian and western music and also perform around the country.

name: Terpsichore
description: We are Terpsichore (terp-SIK-oh-ree), Boston University’s premier a cappella group! Affectionately known as Terps, we are a tight-knit, inclusive group of treble-voiced people dedicated to sharing our love of music by creating and performing complex arrangements of a wide variety of music. Our repertoire includes songs from artists such as Lorde, Beyoncé, Rachel Chinouriri and more! Interested in auditioning? Find up-to-date information our website!
summary: We are Terpsichore (terp-SIK-oh-ree), Boston University’s premier a cappella group! Affectionately known as Terps, we are a tight-knit, inclusive group of treble-voiced people dedicated to sharing our love of music.

name: PERIOD.
description:
Welcome to PERIOD. at BU, a youth-fueled nonprofit that strives to eradicate period poverty and stigma through service, education, and advocacy.

Fall 2025 Interest Form/Mailing List: https://forms.gle/TapuDjLmBPh14cYn9

 

What We Do: Service, Education, and Advocacy

 

Education & Discussion

- Education & discussion on various topics (period stigma, sustainable menstruation, abortion access, reproductive disorders, etc)

 

Fundraiser & Donations

- Period poverty fundraiser

- Menstrual product drive

- Product & financial donation to local shelters

 

Outreach & Advocacy

- Education outreach for local youth

- "How much does BU know?" video project

- Reproductive rights legislation advocacy

 

Fun PERIOD. Activities

- Reproductive rights jeopardy

- Paint and sip arts and crafts

- Movie & snacks night ... and many more!​

 

Learn more about us at www.periodatbu.com and www.instagram.com/periodatbu.

 

https://youtu.be/pgYNBzZgBFI?feature=shared
summary: PERIOD. is a youth-fueled nonprofit that strives to eradicate period poverty and stigma through service, education, and advocacy with the distribution of menstrual products, promotion of youth leadership, and championing of menstrual equity in policy.

name: Egyptian Club
description: The Boston University Egyptian Club is an organization seeking to celebrate the Egyptian culture. It is open to all BU students and staff, regardless of their ethnicity, political affiliations, religion, and sexual orientation; as we hope to boost relations between people of different backgrounds, educate the public about Egypt as a whole, and collectively promote prosperity and a better future for Egypt. It is also open for Egyptian students at BU to network with one another and with students of different backgrounds as well. We seek to achieve our primary objectives by occasionally holding cultural and informational events pertaining to Egyptian society so that we may spread our message to as wide an audience as possible, thereby raising awareness and credibility for our cause. We hope to build a warm, and welcoming community for all!
summary: The Boston University Egyptian Club is an organization seeking to celebrate the Egyptian culture. It is open to all BU students and staff, regardless of their ethnicity, political affiliations, religion, and sexual orientation.

name: GroupIRean: Korean Diplomatic and Political Association
description: In 2010, the founder of GroupIRean came together to provide a venue for students at Boston University to explore international relations, social and political affairs of the relatively under-studied region, the Korean peninsula. The name GroupIRean indicates that we have gathered as a ‘group’, with passion in International Relations (‘IR’) of Korea. As a member of this organization, you will have the opportunity to explore the minor region of Korea in depth through open discussion sections, our think tank project (Information Center of Essence), through attending and hosting guest lectures on global diplomatic issues.
summary: In 2010, the founder of GroupIRean came together to provide a venue for Koreans, Korean Americans and non-Korean students of Boston University to explore international relations, social and political affairs of the relatively under-studied region, the ...

name: On Broadway
description: Boston University On Broadway (OB) is BU's premiere musical theatre organization, dedicated to bringing quality musical productions and theater-related events to the Boston University community. Founded in 1994, OB has become a fixture of artistic life on campus, putting on a variety of shows that have ranged from Broadway classics to the edgy and the avant-garde. We pride ourselves in being a group that teaches our members to be involved with whatever aspect of a production the choose. There are opportunities for actors, singers, dancers, directors, music directors, choreographers, producers, and any technical role you can think of. Please visit our website to learn more about us! http://bostonuniversityonbroadway.com/
summary: Boston University On Broadway (OB) is BU's premiere musical theatre organization, dedicated to bringing quality musical productions and theater-related events to the Boston University community. Founded in 1994, OB has become a fixture of artistic life..

name: The Dear Abbeys
description:
The Dear Abbeys has long been known as the premier all-male a cappella group of Boston University since its foundation 33 years ago.

With a dynamic repertoire of in-house arrangements of songs ranging from classic rock to R&B to modern pop, the Abbeys keep it fresh and fun everywhere they go. After winning the International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella (ICCA’s) in 2005, the Abbeys have performed in stadiums, on street corners, and everywhere in between. Other accolades include winning the 2015 All A Cappella Live Competition and the 19th Annual Faneuil Hall A Cappella Competition. Their edgy pop arrangements can rock fashion shows, and their classy style is frequently heard at weddings, corporate events, charity functions, high schools, and the greater Boston community.

Beyond Beantown, the group has enjoyed sharing their talents around the country. Through yearly tours, the Abbeys have spread music to students and audiences in New York, DC, Princeton, Philadelphia, California, New Orleans, and even Alaska. The Abbeys aim to inspire, inform and entertain—introducing young thinkers to the arts with the hope of initiating positive change. In the past they have been so lucky as to team up with musical heavyweights like Steven Tyler, Ben Folds, NOTA, Pentatonix, and Ball in the House. To date, the Abbeys have produced twelve studio albums, with more on the way. Check out our latest album, Under A Rest, now available on all streaming platforms! Recently, the Abbeys have also focused their creative energy on short films and music videos, all of which can be found on YouTube @dearabbeys (The Dear Abbeys). 

The Dear Abbeys hold auditions every Fall semester in mid-September, where further information can be found on the @dearabbeys instagram.
summary: The Dear Abbeys has long been known as the premier all-male a cappella group of Boston University since its foundation over 30 years ago. From classic rock to R&B to modern pop, the Abbeys keep it fresh and fun everywhere they go.

name: Hack4Impact
description: Background: Hack4Impact is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that was founded by University of Pennsylvania.Hack4Impact has many chapters at other universities, including Columbia University, Georgia Tech, and Boston University. The mission of Hack4Impact is to develop software for other nonprofits to meet their social and humanitarian needs.
summary: Background: Hack4Impact is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that was founded by University of Pennsylvania.Hack4Impact has many chapters at other universities, including Columbia University, Georgia Tech, and Boston University. The mission of Hack4Im...

name: Student Elections Commission
description:
The Student Elections Commission (SEC) is the body of the Judicial Branch of BU Student Government that oversees and runs the elections for the Executive Board, At-Large Parties, student resource body, and for some college governments. The SEC is composed of three Co-Chairs and seven Commissioners. Together, we're in charge various operations from drafting election rules to advertising to event planning. SEC's mission is to ensure impartiality, fairness, and fun in all aspects of the electoral process and provide a platform for future BU leaders.

 

The SEC requires a diverse team of enthusiastic individuals, ready to apply their unique experiences and skills. Undergraduate students who feel comfortable with logistics, budgeting, marketing, and/or crisis management are encouraged to apply. It is important to note that experience in these areas can be helpful, but it is not necessary to succeed in this role.

 

The SEC will meet at least once every two weeks in the Fall semester. These meetings can last from one to two hours depending on the workload for each meeting. In the Spring Semester, the SEC will meet at least once per week, since all election events, including campaigning and voting periods, take place in the Spring.

 

If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to us through email (election@bu.edu). You can also check us out on our website (http://www.bu.edu/election/) or Instagram (@bostonu_sec).
summary: The Boston University Student Elections Commission administers fair and engaging elections for the BU Undergraduate Student Government. Our team manages candidates, drafts election rules, hosts election events, and promotes the future of StuGov!

name: Business Law Club
description:
The Boston University Business Law Club is organized to bring more awareness to the Law field. Through our classes, we have recognized a need for an organization that encourages people to challenge different ideas in a safe environment. Many people are interested in law, but aren't sure if they want to pursue taking a class in it for an entire semester. We wanted to bridge that gap and share opportunities to engage in the Boston law community as well as offer a stress free environment where people can get a glimpse into what law really entails.

For more information about the club please email bostonubizlaw@gmail.com or DM on Instagram @bubusinesslaw
summary: The Boston University Business Law Club is organized to bring more awareness to the Law field. Through our classes, we have recognized a need for an organization that encourages people to challenge different ideas in a safe environment.

name: Terrier Motorsport
description: Terrier Motorsport is a student-run organization that is focused on the design and development of a fully-electric car, in preparation for the yearly Formula Hybrid & Electric competition. Our team is highly focused on pushing the boundaries of Boston University’s education by offering a hands-on experience that requires teamwork, and a variety of special skills that will prepare our members for their desired after college careers. From engineering to marketing, we are a racing team that has something to offer everyone.
summary: Terrier Motorsport is a team dedicated to the design and manufacturing of an electric race car. Our team is committed to offering a hands-on experience with disciplines ranging from engineering to marketing, designed to prepare you for the future!

name: German Culture Club
description:
The German Culture Club is a Boston University organization open to everyone, created to help all participating individuals learn about what it means to be German both here in Boston and elsewhere. By joining this club, members will gain knowledge and participate in cultural traditions as well as understand more about the country itself, its history and language through meetings and organized social events. Regardless of their reasons for joining the German Culture Club, members will have an opportunity to connect with students pursuing a major/minor, those exploring their heritage, and newcomers who simply have an interest in the exciting culture and traditions of the German people. This club strives to create a fun and welcoming environment by bringing a piece of German-speaking countries to the Boston area.

 

Join our newsletter to receive updates

https://mailchi.mp/364f55b1be48/german-culture-club-signup
summary: The German Culture Club is a Boston University organization open to everyone, created to help all participating individuals learn about what it means to be German both here in Boston and elsewhere.

name: Young Americans for Freedom
description: Young Americans for Freedom at Boston University is dedicated to educating and inspiring fellow students on the principles of individual freedoms, strong national defense, free enterprise, and traditional values. We aim to build lasting connections across campus and the political spectrum by fostering healthy debates, engaging events, thoughtful reflection, and mutual understanding.
summary: Young Americans for Freedom at Boston University is dedicated to educating and inspiring fellow students on the principles of individual freedoms, strong national defense, free enterprise, and traditional values.

name: Music Business Club
description: The Boston University Music Business Club aims to foster a vibrant community of student musicians and those interested in exploring the music industry in any way. Students will be able to apply to become 'student artists' and will be assigned teams consisting of MBC members. Students on artist teams will learn how to manage artists, develop marketing tactics and materials, and create social content for their artists. MBC hosts events such as artist showcases, networking events with industry professionals, and other group bonding events.
summary: The Boston University Music Business Club serves as the hub of BU's music community, connecting anyone and everyone who wants to be involved in the music industry, including musicians, photographers, artist managers, PR professionals, and more.

name: Financial Technology Group
description:
Purpose: FinTech BU stands to raise awareness and opportunities to the Boston University community on the new and exciting field of financial technologies and raise awareness on the wave of disruptive technologies the finance world is undergoing.

Mission: We aim at integrating FinTech BU and its members with the world of financial technologies through partnerships with universities, companies and other fintech related institutions. This initiative will be supported through info sessions, guest speaker lectures, annual conferences and the BU FinTech Accelerator.

Vision: We see FinTech BU as a hub for disruptors and creative thinkers who want to change the way everyday people go about the world of finance; a world currently controlled by large corporations and central banks. We want to provide a space for collaborators to meet and have the chance to work together in projects to revamp the world of finance.
summary: FINTECH BU aims to introduce the Boston University community to the new and exciting field of financial technology and provide resources and opportunities for natural disruptors from all backgrounds.

name: Persian Students Association
description: Persian Student Association (PSA) is a cultural club focusing on Iranian culture and practices. We are determined to create a community of students who are interested in the Iranian culture whether they come from Iranian descent or not. Our goal is to create a safe space for many international students to feel understood, giving them a space to celebrate and share their culture with their peers. We believe that Boston University is a very diverse university filled with many international students with different backgrounds and we aim to gather all students that are interested in different cultures in our club. We will educate, share and promote our cultural events, creating a close knit community that share similar values.
summary: Persian Student Association (PSA) is a cultural club focusing on Iranian culture and practices. We are determined to create a community of students who are interested in the Iranian culture whether they come from Iranian descent or not. Our goal is to

name: Pride in Business
description: Pride in Business is a premier business club dedicated to the support and professional development of LGBTQ+ business leaders of all majors. We are dedicated to providing exclusive professional development opportunities and connecting LGBTQ+ students with the Diversity and Inclusion initiatives at top-tier companies. We also aim to provide a sense of community for queer individuals at Boston University as a whole.
summary: Pride in Business is a premier business club dedicated to the support and professional development of LGBTQ+ business leaders of all majors.

name: Campus Activities Board
description:
BU Campus Activities Board (CAB) is the official event planning board for the undergraduate community. As a board, CAB uses a portion of the student activities fee to put on events that most benefit the BU community, and fulfill the mission of CAB: to bring students together and increase a feeling of connection. Some examples of previous events that CAB has held include Movie Nights, Board Games and Burritos Night, Salem Trip, Stuff Your Own Teddy, Drag Show, as well as discounted Celtics tickets. Additionally, CAB hosts Weeks of Welcome, the Junior Event, and Senior Week.

If you have any event ideas you would like to see happen on campus, feel free to email us at cabbu@bu.edu or contact us on our social medias and let us know! Applications for intern positions open each fall!

Follow us on Instagram @bu_cab to join us and stay updated on our latest events!
summary: This is a student-focused programming board for the Boston University campus. Focused on creating events and programs that will benefit the entire BU community, bringing students together in new ways, and planning fun events!

name: Girls Inc
description: Girls Inc is an organization dedicated to empowering young girls and championing women's potentials and aspirations, to pursue broader futures through the mentorship of college-aged women.
summary: Girls Inc is an organization dedicated to empowering young girls and championing women's potentials and aspirations, to pursue broader futures through the mentorship of college-aged women.

name: Hearts for the Homeless Boston
description:
Hearts for the Homeless (H4H) serves one of the most vulnerable populations by providing education on multiple health risk factors, monitoring of blood pressure measurements, and donating health care and prevention resources to people experiencing homelessness in Boston.  Through partnering with local shelters and community health clinics, H4H seeks to reduce the prevalence of hypertension among these individuals. Aside from serving the community, H4H educates its members on cardiovascular disease and its impact on the community through volunteer experience. This organization shapes our members into compassionate leaders and spreads awareness of cardiovascular disease to underprivileged communities in Boston.

 

Want to get involved?

Join our (email) mailing list here: https://tinyurl.com/5299rj54

Check out our website here: https://sites.bu.edu/h4hboston/

Follow us on instagram: @h4hboston
summary: Hearts for the Homeless is an organization that provides free and informative blood pressure screenings and heart health education events to people experiencing homelessness in the Greater Boston Area.

name: Climbing Club
description: The Boston University Climbing Club is a recreational social group that enables students to discover rock climbing. We regularly climb at fitrec and other Boston area gyms. Weather permitting, we lead outdoor Bouldering and Roped climbing trips to the many New England crags. Welcoming to climbers of all skill levels! Come climb with us!
summary: Welcome to Climbing Club. This is a club for people who want to socialize with other rock climbers at BU. The club brings together climbers for weeknight practices at FitRec and other area gyms, where they can meet others, hang out, and get stronger.

name: Mars Rover Club
description:
What we do:

We are a group of students that aim to build the best Mars Rover to compete in the University Rover Challenge (URC) annually. URC, supported by the Mars Society, is the world’s premier university robotics competition that aims to build the next generation of Mars rovers that will work alongside astronauts on the Red Planet in the future. In addition, we plan on reaching out to other local high schools to teach teenagers about Mars, robotics, and life science.

 

Who we are:

Although most are engineering students in 4 different fields: mechanical, electrical, computer, and biochemical engineering, the team also has businesses and other stem students. In fact, in 2022, about 30% of members are from the College of Art and Science, according to our stats. We currently have 55+ active members committed to our rover team technical project, planning outreach events, and fundraising in the upcoming school year.

 

How we run the Mars rover club and technical team: 

The E-Board members plan and conduct outreach events, raise money and host weekly meetings to help members keep up with the competition schedule. We are assembled into eight sub-teams to work on four different competition missions depending on their interests and skills. These four missions are:

  1. A Science Mission to investigate a site for the presence of current or past life.
  2. A Delivery Mission to deliver a variety of objects to astronauts in the field across rugged terrain.
  3. An Equipment Servicing Mission to perform dexterous operations on a mock lander using a robotic arm.
  4. An Autonomous Traversal Mission to autonomously travel to a site and locate a marker.
summary: The Boston University Mars Rover Club (BUMRC) is developing the next best competition-ready Mars rover capable of traversing rough terrain, interacting with its environment, and analyzing samples for signs of life.

name: Sigma Chi
description: Sigma Chi at Boston University is the Iota Upsilon Chapter of the Sigma Chi International Fraternity governed by the values of Friendship, Justice, and Learning.
summary: Sigma Chi at Boston University is the Iota Upsilon Chapter of the Sigma Chi International Fraternity governed by the values of Friendship, Justice, and Learning.

name: Alpha Sigma Kappa - Women in Technical Studies
description:
Alpha Sigma Kappa (ASK) is a sorority dedicated to those pursuing a STEM degree. It is the mission of ASK to provide a supportive community for women in STEM academically, professionally, and socially. ASK is unique in the sense that it is not an organization dedicated towards a particular science but rather fosters an interdisciplinary environment within the sciences. Women of all backgrounds can come together and find common ground over a passion for science while helping each other thrive academically.

As a chapter, ASK is hoping to grow over the years at BU; there are many opportunities for women to develop organizational and leadership skills that will be invaluable to them post-graduation. ASK is BU’s only women-in-STEM sorority and will foster collaboration between the sciences in a rapidly evolving discipline.
summary: Alpha Sigma Kappa is a social sorority for women in STEM! We are here to provide a supportive community for women in the sciences socially, academically, and professionally.

name: CleanTech Club
description:
BU CleanTech Club aims to create an engineering-focused community of students and faculty interested in the advancement of environmentally sustainable technology.

 

JOIN OUR SLACK: https://join.slack.com/t/bucleantechclub/shared_invite/zt-1gcfukvn5-z1_z7gma9KCRYRBB2o8iBA

 

SUBSCRIBE TO OUR NEWSLETTER

 

"CleanTech" or "cleantech" is an all-encompassing term that involves much more than renewable energy. Advanced materials, sustainable protein, electric vehicles, water treatment, waste management, geoengineering, and indoor farming are some of the many industries in the cleantech space. As this technology continues to play an ever-increasing role in the fight against climate change, the CleanTech Club will offer a hub of discussion and application to better prepare students to face this global crisis. Our goals as an organization are as follows:

  1. Increase the presence of cleantech knowledge within the BU community.
  1. Inspire sustainable innovation through engineering and technology.
  1. Invite students, faculty, and industry professionals to learn from one another.
  1. Impact the local and global environment positively and directly.

As we continue into this age where there is an ever-increasing growth of advanced technology and an equally increasing need to aggressively address global environmental issues, the Boston University CleanTech Club strives to create a space on campus where both the discussion of these issues and the actions necessary to help fix them are encouraged, allowing for students of all disciplines to come together and make a difference.
summary: The BU CleanTech Club aims to create an engineering-focused community of students and faculty interested in the advancement of environmentally sustainable technology.

name: Mixed Student Union
summary: Mixed Student Union was founded with the intention of creating a community for those who feel disconnected from their heritage and/or culture. Our goal is for us to find others who might share our experiences and offer a safe place to exchange them.

name: Art Club
description: The aim of Boston University’s Art Club is to create a fostering artistic community where students can engage with one another through different forms of artistic expression. The mission of the group is to provide a safe, stress-free environment where students, from a beginner level to a professional level, can simply do art. Activities would include painting on a tile, going to a museum, painting an article of clothing, etc. We will meet weekly and each week will have a different activity centered around it. You can contact the president of the club with any questions you may have.
summary: The aim of Boston University’s Art Club is to create a fostering artistic community where students can engage with one another through different forms of artistic expression.

name: Society of Physics Students
description: The laws of physics govern everything in the universe, from the most distant stars and galaxies to our own bodies and brains. Learning about physics is one of the best ways to learn more about the world we live in and how it works, no matter your background in science or math. In Photon, we learn more about the physical world through activities and demonstrations, faculty talks, career building events, and field trips to local museums and laboratories. Most of all, we have fun exploring the laws of nature and learning about the universe. You do not have to be a physics major to join, as the diversity of our group is one of our greatest strengths.
summary: Photon is a student-run organization that spreads the love of physics throughout the undergraduate community. We do field trips, demos, and more! You do not need to be a physics major to join - just interest and enthusiasm in learning about physics!

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
description: Iota Chapter of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated, formally known as the New England Chapter, was the first chapter of any black sorority in the New England Area. Iota chapter was chartered on December 29, 1921, and it is the ninth chapter of the sorority. The sorority’s First National President, Sadie T.M. Alexander, and Virginia Alexander came from Grand Chapter in Washington, D.C. to initiate the women who had hopes of becoming members of Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated is a private, not-for-profit organization whose purpose is to provide assistance and support through established programs in local communities throughout the world.

name: FORGE Design Studios
description:
FORGE Design Studios is Boston University’s pre-professional, student-led design community. Our mission is to "forge" a new design community at Boston University, where students of all backgrounds are able to learn and design together. Through our various events and programs, we bridge the gap between emerging designers and the industry. 

Our programming includes:

• FORGE Labs: a summer-long program that connects students with client-based design projects including brand design, graphic design, and UX/UI design.

• Design workshops: we help you learn essential design through our workshops. Past ones include Adobe InDesign, Photoshop, Intro to UI/UX Design, and more.

• Pre-professional programming: join us for career panels, professional development workshops, and networking events.

Learn more about us by following our social media or visiting our website.

Join our Slack channel! 

Sign up for our newsletter!
summary: FORGE Design Studios strives to prepare students for the growing and innovative field of design through workshops, professional development events, community outreach, and more. We aim to serve as a welcoming on-campus design resource and community.

name: Phi Alpha Delta
description:
With 650 established Pre-Law, Law, and Alumni Chapters in the U.S. and 330,000 members internationally, Phi Alpha Delta is the largest co-ed professional law fraternity in the country, and it is the first law fraternity to have an open membership where any student in good standing at an institution can join and utilize our resources.

As the world’s largest legal organization, Phi Alpha Delta is able to offer a variety of unique member benefits such as:

  • Membership that can transition with you from college to law school and beyond.
  • Senior Transition program which connects pre-law graduates to their new law school chapters.
  • An expansive worldwide network that includes U.S. Presidents, Supreme Court Justices, Cabinet Members, Senators, Governors, and prominent attorneys.
  • National Mock Trial Competition
  • The Society of Scholars
  • LSAT Prep discounts
  • National leadership training events, such as the annual Pre-Law Conference and Leadership Summit.
summary: Phi Alpha Delta’s Pre-Law Program assists undergraduate students in making an informed decision about pursuing a legal career, deciding which law school to attend, and preparing for the rigors of law school.

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi Fraternity
description:
Alpha Epsilon Pi (AEPi) is the world’s Jewish college fraternity, operating chapters on more than 190 college campuses in seven countries. Founded in 1913, AEPi has more than 90,000 living alumni. 

The fraternity’s mission is to develop the future leaders of the Jewish communities – is demonstrated every day through acts of brotherhood, Tzedakah, social awareness and support for Jewish communities and Israel.
summary: AEPi is a the nation's largest Jewish fraternity which provides a community establishing leadership, brotherhood, and social awareness for all its members.

name: Phi Kappa Tau
description:
The Zeta Pi chapter at Boston University began when two men, inspired by the vision of Phi Kappa Tau chapters across the country, set out to create their own chapter and a new fraternal experience beginning with the core values of distinguished leadership and the creation of men of distinction.

Since the fall of 2016, when this all took place, the Zeta Pi chapter has flourished and subsequently expanded its mission. While holding true to the values of the national organization, the Zeta Pi chapter works to define what it means to be a fraternity man.

While these experiences alone help develop leadership skills, our true goal is to promote ethical leadership in both our chapter and our community. In the brotherhood, our resident council is comprised of men from many different nationalities, backgrounds, and experiences. Phi Tau's style of government ensures the opinions, ideas, and views of all our brothers are thoughtfully and meaningfully considered. The democratic nature of our leadership structure lends fluidly to the inclusion of many sentiments and perspectives.

Outside of the chapter, ethical leadership means taking on relevant and impactful problems. Since our founding, our brothers have worked diligently against sexual assault in our community. Rather than taking a passive stance, our brothers have addressed the issue head-on by leading sexual assault awareness and education groups such as BU’s 16K Strong. This involvement keeps our members and other BU students informed, aware, and empowered to make change. In addition to this involvement, the Zeta Pi chapter also weaves ethical leadership into community service by confronting another large issue: climate change. Our numerous events, which have included cleaning up the Charles River and services for Boston’s National Audubon Society, share an emphasis on ecological conservation. Our brotherhood has embraced the opportunity to model the way to a cleaner and more environmentally responsible future.

Each semester, our chapter donates over $2500 to SeriousFun, a non-profit that offers camps to children with terminal or debilitating illnesses. To raise this money, our chapter created The Garden, a venue where Berklee bands and Allston bands can perform and collect donations for SeriousFun. The Garden allows us to raise money for our philanthropy while also creating valuable ties with other individuals and organizations outside of BU.
summary: Phi Kappa Tau (Phi Tau), a national organization with over 97,000 brothers, has worked to instilled leadership in its members since 1906. Simply put, Phi Tau develops men of character into men of distinction.

name: Art for the Heart
description: At Art for the Heart, we use art's power to empower. We engage in purposeful fundraising for local nonprofits and host community-building activities for fellow student creatives to connect and engage with each other while also making a beneficial impact on their community. Throughout the semester, we host very fun and very unique art and volunteer events and also have a Creative Team, a group of artists who create their own independent work to donate for our art sale fundraisers. Our art sales are solely dedicated towards raising money for nonprofits, and you can get involved by volunteering for and donating to them! If you are passionate about art or just want a creative avenue to give, follow us on @bu.art4heart and stop by one of our meetings and make something that makes an impact!
summary: Art for the Heart will help student artists donate art that they’ve created to club art sales that fundraise for a different local charity each semester. Spread joy and help others through any art form! All experience levels welcome!

name: Cybersecurity Association
description: Boston University’s Cyber Security Association (CyS) strives to raise awareness on the value of cyber-aware leadership in the Cyber industry, foster a constructive community for students passionate about the role of cybersecurity in today’s organizations, and those who want to learn more about the field and the impact it will have on organizations in every industry and economic sector. We create future business leaders that understand the value of cybersecurity with the necessary skills to succeed in the field even with a non-technical background.
summary: Create future business leaders that understand the value of cybersecurity with the necessary skills to succeed in the field and raise awareness on the importance that cybersecurity leadership has on protecting organizations from cyber threats,

name: It's On Us
description: "It's On Us" (to stop sexual assault) is a social movement created by Barack Obama and White House Council on Women and Girls to raise awareness and fight against sexual assault on college campuses for both men and women. It's On Us is a rallying cry inviting everyone to step up and realize that the solution begins with us. It’s a declaration that sexual assault is not only a crime committed by a perpetrator against a victim, but a societal problem in which all of us have a role to play. We are reframing sexual assault in a way that inspires everyone to see it as their responsibility to do something, big or small, to prevent it. We are asking everyone involved to create an environment, be it a dorm room, a party, a club or a sports team, or the greater college campus, where sexual assault is unacceptable and survivors are supported. It’s On Us chapters establish a team, plan and host events, and conduct educational programs and training such as bystander intervention courses with their peers on campus in order to raise awareness about sexual violence on campus while preventing it from happening in the first place.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to combat sexual violence and harassment, especially sexual assault, within the BU community. It aims to change campus culture and engage a variety of students on various issues.

name: Bug Club
description: The Bug Club offers a community that engages students in the study of insects, known as entomology. Through discussion panels, guest speakers, group events, and club field trips we explore the various applications that insects have such as in our economy, medicine, as a food source, and in various fields of research. Feel free to hop in a meeting. We don't bite ;)
summary: The Bug Club offers a community that engages students in the study of insects, known as entomology. Through discussion panels, guest speakers, and group events, we explore the various applications that insects have on the ecosystem and our daily lives.

name: Breathe BU
description: The Breathe BU Wellness Club exists to create a yoga-focused inclusive, uplifting space where Boston University students can explore wellness in all its forms—physical, mental, and emotional. From yoga flows and strength workouts to mindful journaling and an online platform, we aim to empower students to build sustainable, joyful habits that support their well-being both on and off campus.
summary: Breathe BU is a yoga-based wellness club at Boston University that blends movement, mindfulness, and community to support students' physical and mental well-being.

name: Automotive Club
description: BU Automotive Club acts as a forum that enables automotive enthusiasts to discuss, debate, participate and fulfill their motor vehicle related desires to the fullest. Podcasts, debates, quizzes, contests are some of the ways through which Terriers would show their passion for vehicles. Additionally, we hope to have weekly releases of articles/podcasts from the members of the club where they would write/talk to express their knowledge about motor vehicles and share their opinions on their desired vehicles to provide a personal touch. We would also organize trips to auto expos, automotive factories or racing events and plan on road trips during the weekends to places in and around Boston. These road trips are planned to take a break from the academic life and quench the thirst for driving many terriers would have! We look forward to conducting many more driving related events in order to fulfill the driving desires of many students at BU. Moreover, we would bring in speakers throughout the semester to interact and have fun conversations with car lovers from our club and around the globe!
summary: Through discussions, events, drives, car shows, group trips, and various other means, the BU Automotive Club strives to act as a forum that enables automotive enthusiasts to fulfill their motor-vehicle-related desires.

name: Girls Who Code
description:
Girls Who Code Boston University is a student group that aligns with the efforts of the national Girls Who Code organization, to close the gender gap in tech. We aim to do this by empowering our community with skill-building, networking events, and mentorship with the national organization.

We regularly host programming workshops, professional development workshops, and social events! We also run a mentorship program and a volunteer program to teach students 3-12 how to code. Anyone is welcome to join regardless of gender or major!
summary: Girls Who Code Boston University is a student group that aligns with the efforts of the Girls Who Code organization, to close the gender gap in tech. We aim to do this by empowering our community with skill-building and networking events.

name: Healthcare Improvement Inc
description: What is the relationship between law and medicine? How do insurance companies and doctors play a role in nationwide healthcare? What is the connection between economics and public health? How will change be implemented to address healthcare disparities? Healthcare Improvement Inc. seeks to answer these questions. This is an interdisciplinary student club that connects the seemingly at-conflict fields of medicine, law, economics, and public health. Our mission is to improve community healthcare and advocate for national healthcare improvements, with an emphasis on patient care.
summary: This is an interdisciplinary student club that connects the seemingly at-conflict fields of medicine, law, economics, and public health. We will provide a forum for students to implement means of improving the national healthcare system and patient care.

name: Biology and Business Club
description: The goal of the Boston University BioBiz club is to provide a community for passionate students to network and explore the nuances of biotech and pharmaceutical companies. We want to be able to provide mentorship and guidance for those planning on entering the industry through both the business and science perspective. We want to foster an environment where there is a fluidity between the STEM and business fields so that students in both areas can see how intertwined both areas are. We also want to bring more awareness to this field in the Boston University community as an interdisciplinary area that more people should explore.
summary: The goal of the Boston University Biobiz club is to provide a community for passionate students to network and explore the nuances of biotech and pharmaceutical companies.

name: The Politica at Boston University
description:
The Politica at Boston University (PBU) is an academic global affairs club with an online podcast, publication, and roundtable discussions. We serve as a platform for students passionate about global affairs to engage in refined dialogue and publish interdisciplinary research on topics relevant to the modern political conversation, with focuses on the studies of law, economics, politics, and activism. We seek to fulfill the vacancy on campus of a space where students can respectfully discuss, collaborate, and publish comprehensive academic analyses centered around current affairs.

At the heart of our ethos is the recognition that fostering an international board of members is crucial for the club’s success. We are open to undergraduate students from all backgrounds and walks of life, as we believe that diversity and inclusivity are essential for the cultivation of insightful research and conversation. We seek to fulfill the vacancy on campus of a space where students can respectfully discuss, collaborate, and publish comprehensive academic analyses centered around current affairs. Researching contentious topics to further inform the student body enables club members to engage in their passion whilst advancing in the pursuits of knowledge and social awareness.
summary: The Politica is a global affairs organization with an online publication, podcast, and roundtable discussions. We believe that diversity and inclusivity are essential for the cultivation of insightful research and conversation.

name: First Generation and/or Low Income Partnership
description:
FLIP @ BostonU Description

FLIP  (First-Generation Low Income Partnership) @ BostonU aims to support our school’s first-generation and/or low-income (FGLI) students. The purpose of FLIP @ BostonU is three-fold: first, to build a community on campus for students who identify as FGLI; second, to create resources and work to maximize equal access to resources for those students who need them; finally, to advocate for and implement institutional change with the help of the institution.

FLIP @ BostonU is also a chapter of FLIP National, a national nonprofit whose core tenet is centering student advocacy and the student's voice. FLIP National outlines seven target areas in which all FGLI issues and solutions fall, including those at Boston University: awareness and visibility, academic development, housing insecurity, food insecurity, professional development, student wellbeing, and community building, and financial support. With this partnership, we join a nationwide network of support and innovation consisting of FLIP National’s twenty other chapters, as well as the greater platform of FGLI and higher education nonprofits. Through our parent organization, we have access to their existing partnerships and initiatives, including a laptop lending library, FGLI conferences, and FGLI class confession pages.

We work closely with the Newbury Center, the First-Generation center on campus, to build community amongst First-Generation and/or Low-Income students on campus, connect students with resources at BU and in Boston, put on programming that meets the needs of students (i.e. academic/professional development, financial resources, mental health, etc.) and generally provide support to students in whatever way they need to help them be successful at BU. 

In the past we have done student panels with prospective and admitted students, professional panels, professional headshots, study nights, social events (i.e. game nights, succulent decorating, scavenger hunts, museum trips, etc.), discussions about our (intersecting) identities and more.

If you're interested in learning more about us you can join our mailing list here: https://forms.gle/9eBcFpxPujMtu1LH7
summary: FLIP @ BostonU is a student-led organization that aims to support first-generation and low-income (FGLI) students at BU. With the Newbury Center and FLIP National, we combat issues such as lack￼ of resources within BU while creating an FGLI community.

name: The Alexander Hamilton Society
description:
The Alexander Hamilton Society (AHS) is a non-partisan, not-for-profit, national organization that seeks to identify, educate, and launch young men and women into foreign policy and national security careers imbued with the Hamiltonian perspective of strong and principled American leadership in global affairs. Grounded in a nationwide foundation of student-driven campus chapters, our network works to revitalize American foreign policy.

Through the vehicle of informed debate, our campus programming is critical to the intellectual and professional development of a new generation of American leaders. We act as a bridge between promising student leaders to internships, graduate programs, and professional opportunities in the worlds of academia, business, finance, policymaking, politics, and journalism. While our focus is on the long term, our alumni have already achieved significant influence in their professional pursuits, rebuilding the ranks of talented citizens looking to serve their country over the course of their lives and careers.
summary: AHS is a non-partisan, non-profit, national organization that seeks to identify, educate, and launch students into foreign policy and national security careers imbued with the Hamiltonian perspective of strong and principled leadership in global affairs.

name: Undergraduate Women in Economics
description:
Boston University Undergraduate Women in Economics serves:

  1. To bring together all minority genders interested in economics to create a community space and support system; 
  2. To create opportunities for minority gender undergraduates to network with upperclassmen, graduate students, alumni, and faculty at BU and other colleges; and
  3. To facilitate discussions on gender and minority empowerment and economic theory on discrimination
summary: UWE is a community of women and minority gender students who are pursuing a degree in Economics or are interested in the field. We host researchers and speakers and start conversations around what it means to be a minority student in Economics.

name: Howard Thurman Center
summary:
Do you have the guts to challenge your own thinking? In a world of locked minds, can you open yours? Our mission is to connect through shared creative experiences, exchange ideas, and explore differences.

Push Your Boundaries!

name: Women In Law
description:
Women In Law at Boston University would strive to fill the gap that exists for young women who are interested in a pre-law path. 

The mission of the Women In Law Club, regardless of the historical and systemic gender inequality they may face, is to develop, nurture, and challenge students to pursue their dreams. We will develop appropriate conversations and events that will provide students with realistic and helpful pre-professional advice. We will strive to build positive interactions between mentors, mentees, peers, and advisors. 

Our vision is to also educate the community on legal issues through society including, but not limited to, employment, health, education, violence, access to resources and more. We will push to be an inclusive organization aiming to promote an environment of academic, professional, and legal equality. It is essential to prepare undergraduates who may be interested in a legal career and by providing a strong community of women to support each other, we can be hopeful they each achieve their dreams.
summary: WIL strives to fill the gap that exists for young women who are interested in a pre-law path. The mission of the club is to develop, nurture, & challenge students to pursue their law oriented dreams.

name: Society of Women in Space Exploration
description: The Boston University Chapter of the Society of Women in Space Exploration is dedicated to providing a safe space for women and minorities interested in space-related fields to collaborate over shared interests and experiences.
summary: The Boston University Chapter of the Society of Women in Space Exploration is dedicated to providing a safe space for women and minorities interested in space-related fields to collaborate over shared interests and experiences.

name: Study of Religions Club
description:
Here at the Study of Religions Club we provide a casual space to discuss all things religion: traditions, beliefs, rituals, politics, and even things that you might not think involve religion! As students in the Religion Department, we want to help make studying religion fun and engaging for everyone! You do not have to be a religion major/minor to join. 

For the upcoming academic year, we have a number of ideas for events. These events include game nights, movie screenings, faculty presentations, and conferences! Please contact us if you have any ideas of events you would like to see!
