summary: The Study of Religions Club (SOR) provides a social environment for BU students to casually engage in discussions about religion and traditions while connecting students with professional and academic resources in the Department of Religion.

name: SLIC - CUBE (Formerly known as Community Service Center)
description:
The Student Leadership & Impact Center combines the work of the CSC and SAO. The Community Service Center (CSC) has served as a welcoming space for service, leadership, education, and reflection. In solidarity with our neighbors and community partners, we aim to:

  • provide the Boston University community with opportunities to address and improve the critical concerns of Greater Boston,
  • serve as a vehicle for personal growth and leadership,
  • encourage students to be active and responsible citizens in neighboring communities,
  • broaden the scope of the educational experience through service and reflection,
  • and serve Greater Boston in a meaningful and mutually beneficial way.
summary: The Student Leadership & Impact Center combines the work of the CSC and SAO. The Community Service Center (CSC) has served as a welcoming space for service, leadership, education, and reflection.

name: Arab Student Organization
description: BUArabs intends to create a community for Arabs at Boston University. We hope to share our passions with Arab students and Non-Arab students interested in our culture. Proud of our heritage, we seek to promote solidarity and identity for all club members. Our social, cultural, and celebratory events are meant to embrace our diverse community. Together, we will create a platform for members to express their ideas and grow academically, socially, and professionally. In doing so, we hope students feel a sense of being back home.
summary: BUArabs' mission lies in building cultural, academic, and industrial bridges between BU and the Arab world. We aim to create a platform to express our ideas and grow professionally, academically, and personally while celebrating our Arab heritage.

name: Adopted and Multicultural Student Organization
description:
The Adopted and Multicultural Student Alliance (AMSA) is a student-led community for adoptees, members of adoptive families, and anyone interested in learning about adoptive experiences and the multicultural identity that often accompanies. This community aims to connect members and provide opportunities for personal development and growth through discussions, social and cultural events, and giving back to the community. 

Originally founded as the International Adoptee Club in 2021, we have expanded our mission to reflect the diverse community that falls under the large umbrella of adoption, including domestic, international, interracial, foster care, or open adoption to name a few.

Our mission is to create a welcoming community where members can connect, share experiences, and explore the adoptive identity. Through open discussions, social and cultural events, and community engagement, we aim to:

  • Build community and connections
  • Foster pride and comfort in the adoptive and multicultural identity
  • Challenge stigmas surrounding adoption
  • Provide guidance and give back to the adoptive community
summary: The Adopted and Multicultural Student Alliance (AMSA) is a student-led community for adoptees, members of adoptive families, and anyone interested in learning about adoptive experiences and the multicultural identity that often accompanies.

name: Oral Health Advocacy-BU
description: OHA-BU is an organization dedicated to spreading awareness about the importance of oral health to the BU community and the local Boston area. At meetings we learn about the prevention of oral related diseases and treatments taught by dental professionals. Our group also partakes in various community service events, as well as fundraises money and oral hygiene products to donate to local charities. OHA-BU is open to all undergraduate BU students that want to create a more equitable society, both locally and globally.
summary: OHA-BU is an organization dedicated to spreading awareness about the importance of oral health through educating, volunteering, and fundraising for the BU community, local Boston area, and global organizations.

name: South Asian Student Association
description: The South Asian Student Association is a cultural organization that represents communities from Afghanistan, Pakistan, India, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh, the Maldives, and Sri Lanka through a variety of professional, social, and service events.
summary: The South Asian Student Association is a cultural organization representing communities from Afghanistan, Pakistan, India, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh, the Maldives & Sri Lanka through a variety of professional, social and service events.

name: Albanian Student Association
description: The Albanian Student Association is a club in which enriches the already diverse campus of Boston University by bringing together students of Albanian descent or those interested in learning Albanian culture, history, and contemporary issues. The Albanian community is very tightly knit and we believe that students should be able to experience this patriotic and family-oriented environment. We look to provide students from all over the world a place that they can call home, where we provide relationships, advice, and much more.
summary: We aim to provide a community for Albanians or students who are interested in the Albanian culture, history, and contemporary issues that will help enrich diversity and raise awareness of situations that affect Albanians worldwide.

name: Women in Neuroscience
description: Website: https://winbostonu.wixsite.com/buwin
summary: WIN empowers Boston University undergraduate women in neuroscience and encourages diversity in the field by providing mentorship, guidance, and professional development resources.

name: Moot Court Organization
description: Moot Court competition (simulated legal argumentation before hypothetical appellate courts, also known as “Mock Supreme Court”) is an educational opportunity to prepare the next generation of legal professionals. This club prepares individuals in team oral advocacy regarding the most pressing Constitutional issues of our time. The purpose of this organization is to educate undergraduate students about the United States legal system, jurisprudence, and the work of American attorneys. We compete and aspire to win at the highest level of competition attainable while offering opportunities to travel nationwide.
summary: The Moot Court team's purpose is to educate undergraduate students about the American legal system, American jurisprudence, and the work of American attorneys, as well as to compete and aspire to win at the highest level of competition attainable.

name: Pre-Health Professionals Club
description:
PHPC is an independently run organization, focused on building a community from scratch. Our goals are to create a supportive, stress-free environment while building our network and gaining valuable insight into different pre-health fields. 

As a group our primary goal is to contribute philanthropically and through community service, dedicating our lives to the service of others. This organization upholds the standards of academic excellence, professionalism, and integrity in all endeavors.
summary: PHPC is a Pre-Health Professional organization striving toward “helping people, together." Members dedicate their lives to the service of others philanthropically and through community service,  while always upholding academic excellence and integrity.

name: Stock Trading at Boston University
description:
We believe that all students at Boston University deserve the right to learn the importance and skills of stock trading. Stock Trading can be complex and daunting, but with the right help it can become one of the best ways to make money with your idle cash. We plan on teaching students various topics within the trading realm including but not limited to Stocks, Options, Fixed Income, and Futures. Current club meetings are Thursday 7:30pm at HAR 211! 

At STABU, we recognize that everyone brings diverse perspectives to the table. Throughout the semester we will teach members how to create trading plans based on their personal characteristics. We also hope to go into the emotional aspect of trading, lessons that will not only be helpful for trading but for life as well. The goal of these teachings is to give students the resources they need to become successful and hopefully profitable traders whilst studying here at Boston University. We also plan on hosting various projects where members can analyze significant stock market events from a technical standpoint. 

At Stock Trading at Boston University we will teach you how to go from working for you money to learning how to make your money work for you!

 

STABU is a club dedicated to teaching students at Boston University how to trade the various avenues of the stock market. In meetings, this organization will deal with topics such as:

  • Intro to stocks
  • Stock Intricacies and studies
  • Introduction to Options Trading
  • Options Intricacies
  • Fixed Income
  • Futures
  • Trading Strategies
summary: Stock Trading at Boston University (STaBU) are a group of students committed to teaching stock trading techniques to the Boston University community. Covered topics include but are not limited to Stocks, Options, Fixed Income, and trading strategy.

name: University Blood Initiative, BU Chapter
description: Hospital blood shortage is a critical, nationwide issue that jeopardizes the care of countless patients. This issue is complex and multifaceted, however, two central components are a lack of diversity in donors and a low number of young blood donors. We seek to educate our generation on the nature of the blood shortage and motivate people to take action. Our organization builds meaningful connections with our local youth, and through this, works to increase awareness and engagement of potential donors. By collaborating closely with local blood centers, encouraging people to donate there, and organizing blood drives here on our campus, we seek to empower and support our nearby hospitals. Through the formation of a sustainable blood supply to these centers, a lasting and impactful relationship is strengthened within the community.
summary: The mission of the UBI is to educate and mobilize the next generation of blood donors to combat the ongoing blood shortage issue. Through our grassroots efforts, we strive to build lasting engagement and awareness with the youth of our community.

name: Veteran Assist
description: Veteran Assist (VA) is a student-run organization that aims to engage students in volunteer opportunities dedicated to helping veterans, with a focus on disabled and underserved veterans, in and around the Greater Boston community. We hope to create meaningful relationships with veterans by assisting them with fundamental needs, both physically and mentally, listening to their meaningful stories, enriching them with engaging activities, and providing them with educational knowledge and resources. Our goal is to educate students about the importance of aiding our veterans, teaching them how to help with the challenges they face after returning from service.
summary: Veteran Assist is a student-run organization that aims to engage students in volunteer opportunities dedicated to helping veterans, with a focus on disabled and underserved veterans, throughout the Greater Boston community.

name: BIPOC Mental Health Collective
description: BIPOC Mental Health Collective is a communal space for racial minorities to nurture their mental health by sharing experiences and resources.
summary: BIPOC Mental Health Collective is a communal space for racial minorities to nurture their mental health by sharing experiences and resources.

name: BU BassLine A-Capella
description: BU BassLine is an all gender a cappella group made by people of color for people of color. Our goal is to empower BIPOC students at BU, helping them find their voice and a community through music. BU BassLine is committed to partnering with clubs and organizations that upholds diversity, equity, and inclusion values. Our repertoire will consist of songs by POC artists with genres ranging from R&B, soul, funk, and more.
summary: BU BassLine is an all gender a cappella group made by people of color for people of color. Our goal is to empower BIPOC students at BU, helping them find their voice and a community through music.

name: After School Newsletter
description: BUAS is a newsletter club dedicated to encourage BU Chinese international students engage in local campus activities, explore Boston culture, and connect with other students through publishing newsletters, social media posts, and pop-up events.
summary: BUAS is a newsletter club dedicated to encourage BU Chinese international students engage in local campus activities, explore Boston culture, and connect with other students through publishing newsletters, social media posts, videos, and events.

name: Fenway Campus Hall Council
description: Fenway Campus Hall Council is meant to support and create community on the Fenway Campus
summary: Fenway Campus Hall Council is meant to support and create community on the Fenway Campus

name: Trading Card Games Club
description:
The goal of the Boston University Trading Card Games Club (BU TCG) is to provide the BU community with the opportunity and means to play and enjoy trading card games (TCGs). TCGs such as Magic: the Gathering, Pokemon, Flesh and Blood, Yugioh, and more have been gaining popularity in the past years with the addition of new products and opportunities for players. We are a safe place for players of all skill in any of these games. We primarily focus on MTG, but among our eboard we know most TCGs and would be down to learn any we don't know with you. For MTG events, we do consistent cube drafts where the winner will get featured on our discord and instagram. Also, about every other week we do a draft of the current set or a slightly older set where you can sign up for free or pay to keep your cards. In addition, we always have a few commander/edh pods going that you can jump in.

To get involved the best thing is joining our discord: https://discord.gg/8Qwp5N8cTc. We always post the events a few days in advance and it's a great place to ask questions and talk about your favorite game. In addition, we just created our instagram called butcgclub where will post event announcements.

We also live stream our events now! Check out our twitch channel at https://www.twitch.tv/butcgclub and hit the follow button. If you are interested in commentating or casting, the mic is open to anyone!

Fall 2025 Meeting Days:

Monday Nights: 6-11pm HAR 326

Saturday Nights: BU TCG General Meeting - 4pm-11pm in CAS 320
summary: The BU TCG Club is a place to play, trade and enjoy any trading card game!

name: The Boston Chapter of The Urban Garden Initiative
description:
The Urban Garden Initiative (TUGI) is an international 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that uses local urban gardening to engage youth in environmental education. The organization runs through individual city-wide chapters, often started by universities, each of which create curriculum and gardens to host workshops, continuous programs, and other events for local schools and community groups. 

In the TUGI: Boston chapter, we prioritize engaging with our community outside of Boston University and practicing local forms of activism through education. Through this chapter, members gain experience with K-8 education centered around environmental science, both through writing and executing lesson plans. Members also gain experience in creating and maintaining urban gardens, while forming connections with the involved communities.

Thus far, we have formed relationships with 3 local Boston Public Schools where we will be running 6-8 week long programs for their students, and are in the process of forming more. At one of these schools, we will be creating a new on-going community garden where this and other programs can take place. Members can take part in writing and carrying out these programs, planning and creating the garden, fundraising, art initiatives, or any other aspect of the process. We will meet bi-weekly and have sign-up sheets for members to volunteer at various events and programs throughout each month.
summary: The Urban Garden Initiative (TUGI) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that uses urban gardening to engage youth in environmental education. In the TUGI: Boston chapter, we create curriculum and gardens to host continuous programs for local schools.

name: Barbell Club
description: Boston University Barbell Club aims to become the most reputable resource for fitness, weight training, and exercise science information as well as be a community and social outlet for training enthusiasts. We are accomplishing these goals through: building a strong community that members can rely on, alleviating the weight training intimidation barrier, and ensuring an inclusive and supportive environment. We also support and encourage all competitive lifting interests through student led teams.
summary: Boston University Barbell Club aims to become a reputable resource for fitness and weight training information as well as build a strong community and social outlet for training enthusiasts.

name: Nigerian Students Association
description:
The Nigerian Students Association of Boston University is committed to fostering a community of Nigerian students on campus. It serves as a home away from home for those wanting to express their culture and explore their roots, while also serving as an open space for others to learn about the culture. It will also serve as a space to educate members of issues Nigeria is currently facing in hopes of promoting social activism among the community.

The goal for BU NSA is to create a safe environment for students to share their love and appreciation for Nigerian culture while celebrating our similarities and differences as university students. We also aim to foster an open and accepting learning environment to give the Black Boston community a platform to elevate their voices.
summary: The Nigerian Students Association of Boston University is committed to fostering a community of Nigerian students on campus. The goal for BU NSA is to create a safe environment for students to share their love and appreciation for Nigerian culture.

name: Recreational Tennis Club
description:
The Boston University Recreational Tennis Club is a college tennis organization dedicated to fostering an inclusive, welcoming, and enjoyable atmosphere for tennis players of all levels. Our club provides members an opportunity to learn the game of tennis and improve their skills through organized practices, lessons, tournaments, and social events. Guided by our core values of community, leadership, hospitality, innovation, and fun, we aim to inspire and promote tennis for everyone.

 

Our club offers structured opportunities to refine your skills and connect with fellow players. We host regular skill-development practices led by experienced players, providing valuable insights to elevate your game, regardless of your current level.

 

BU Rec Tennis recognizes the importance of building a strong network. We organize social events to create a space for camaraderie and foster friendships within the club.

Membership is open to full-time students at Boston University. We encourage players of all backgrounds to join us and become part of a dynamic and supportive tennis community at BU. Stay tuned for upcoming events by following us on our email listed below and our Instagram: @bu_rectennis

 

*TO BE ON OUR EMAIL LIST USE THE LINK BELOW* *Updated as of JUNE 1, 2025*

https://bu.us8.list-manage.com/subscribe?u=f2f24040105fcb0b5a515db95&id=6a9770cd42
summary: The Boston University Recreational Tennis Club aims to promote the sport of tennis to players of all levels. Our club allows members to learn the game, improve their skills, and participate in match play through organized practices and social events.

name: Vakhri Taur Diyan
description:
Boston University's Vakhri Taur Diyan is an all female competitive Bhangra team dedicated to nurturing female artists in a male dominated Bhangra circuit. We focus on cultivating a team that builds and supports its dancers as well as educating the BU community on Punjabi and Sikh culture.

 

Follow our Instagram for information on Summer 2023 tryouts!!
summary: Vakhri Taur Diyan is an all female competitive Bhangra team dedicated to nurturing female artists in a male dominated Bhangra circuit. We will cultivate a team that builds & supports its dancers while educating the BU community on Punjabi & Sikh culture.

name: Girl Gains Boston University
description: We are a nationwide female weight lifting organization with four core values: strength, boldness, people, integrity, and visionary. We provide resources and community for women of all backgrounds and fitness levels. At a campus level, we host workout events, community events, workshops, and informational meetings led by certified personal trainers, nutritionists, and more.
summary: Our mission is to promote female weightlifting while empowering women to feel strong and beautiful in a judgment-free community. Whether you're a dedicated gym-goer or a curious beginner, Girl Gains is here to support you in every stage of your fitness.

name: Mahjong Club
description: We hope to create a space where students can play, discuss, and partake in the culture of Mahjong, the most popular game in Asia, in a recreational and competitive setting. We will achieve this by having students play Mahjong during club meetings and by holding tournament events for more competitive players. We will also host teaching programs for players of all skill levels and have community events revolving around Mahjong.
summary: BUMJC provides the opportunity to learn and play Mahjong, the most popular game in Asia. We hope to spread appreciation of the culture and traditions behind Mahjong and have a community for Mahjong players of all skill levels.

name: Flag Football Club
description: BU Flag Football has been going on for a whole year now. Twice a week, we all meet to play flag football or 7v7 amongst ourselves. BU hasn't previously offered a football related club, so it would be a good addition to the extracurriculars of the school. The club is also a community where we have been doing fun things together such as watching games. We've welcomed new people, and the club has been a great place to make new friends with common interests and play a fun popular sport recreationally while improving together.
summary: A organization in which members of the BU community can meet to play flag football and learn new skills.

name: Society of Sci-Fi & Horror
description: SOSH will serve to foster a community for students who are enthusiastic about science fiction and horror motifs including but not limited to conspiracies, magic, and the supernatural. Our goals include hosting weekly laid-back meetings, screenings and discussions and working to network and collaborate with other student organizations in the BU community. SOSH welcomes anyone intrigued about horror and sci-fi in books, movies, or any other art forms — “connoisseurs” and newcomers to the genres alike.
summary: SOSH strives to foster a community for students who are enthusiastic about science fiction and horror motifs in books, movies, or any other art forms. We welcome “connoisseurs” and newcomers to the genres alike.

name: Alternative Investments Group
description:
BU Alternative Investments Group (BUAIG) aims to enhance our community’s knowledge of non-public investments and create a networking platform for sharing ideas and interests. These investments include hedge funds, commodities, real estate, and private equity, all of which offer significant career opportunities. We find it to be very important for students to be educated on these topics, as it will play a huge role in their career paths.

 

BUAIG supports its members with a robust curriculum, centered around a semester-long case competition. Throughout the semester, members work in teams to develop an investment thesis for the case, while receiving feedback from mentors and professors. Members also gain professional development through keynote speakers, networking events, and a weekly job opportunities mailing list. Each semester concludes with students presenting their work to a panel of judges for constructive feedback.

 

Join our mailing list to stay informed about meetings and case competition opportunities. Note that requesting membership on Terrier Central does not automatically add you to the mailing list. If you have any questions, please contact us at buaig@bu.edu.
summary: BUAIG enhances the community's proficiency in non-public investments and develops well-rounded business professionals through a robust curriculum, a semester-long case competition, and professional development events.

name: The Beacon at Boston University
description:
The Beacon is a social literary journal which thrives on the imagination of Boston University's finest writers. Each outside-the-box issue uplifts student voices, offering writers a platform to share ideas and an inclusive space for creative works. Beyond our pages, the Beacon is the writer's hub of campus. Through events such as writer's workshops and open mic nights, the Beacon gathers literary-like minds together to discover, discuss, and celebrate creative writing. 

​​As Boston University's Premiere Student Publication, the original 1876 Beacon is as old as The Adventures of Tom Sawyer, and born in the same year as Jack London. Before the Beacon disbanded in 1951, its publications witnessed and marked both World Wars, the end of gendered segregation in higher education and, of course, the many infrastructural changes that have occurred at BU since its creation. In 2019, BU Student Jonathan Han decided it was time to bring the Beacon back, hoping to offer students a vital platform to share their writing with the Boston community. The Rebranded 2022 Beacon as you see it now is constantly evolving, but continues to fulfill the wishes of Jonathan and the long, long line of Beacon writers, providing a safe space to uplift student voices.

The Beacon strives to celebrate the creative writers of Boston University. Our team rallies around the goals of  building a flourishing writing community here at BU, and offering a safe space for students to feel supported in their writing endeavors. We do not seek perfection, and aim to actively break down the stereotypes of what writing should be. We hope to encourage students to raise their voices by helping writers hone their skills, increasing access to peer-review resources, and providing opportunities for writers to make social connections.

The Beacon team has big dreams for the fall 2024 semester.

We are re-envisioning the Beacon as more than just a magazine, hoping to bring writers across campus together for a series of exciting social events. Our vision for the semester includes hosting: 

  • Open Mic Nights 
  • Writer's Workshops (Speed dating with our Editors!)
  • Guest Speaker Lectures/Masterclasses 
  • Slam Poetry Competitions
  • 2025 Issue Release Party 

And more! In addition to holding events, we are working towards a physical magazine publication. Our magazine will be of professional caliber, curated to the highest standard, and feature a collaboration with the BU's graphic design students. Watch out for the 2025 Issue to be distributed throughout campus!​
summary: The Beacon is a social literary journal which thrives on the imagination of Boston University's finest writers. Each outside-the-box issue uplifts student voices, offering writers a platform to share ideas and an inclusive space for creative works.

name: Journal of History at Boston University
description: Boston University's only Undergraduate peer-reviewed history publication.
summary: The Journal of History is an independent, peer-reviewed publication that promotes intellectual curiosity and encourages students to exchange scholarly ideas. We aim to provide an opportunity for undergraduate students to showcase their historical work.

name: One Love Foundation
description:
"Our foundation was founded to honor the unnecessary and tragic death of Yeardley Love by engaging young people through compelling, relatable films and honest conversations around healthy and unhealthy relationship behaviors." 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BprdpyKwZyg 

Join the 2025-2026 GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/110016000/shHL71hz
summary: The goal of One Love Club @ BU is to educate all of the student body about healthy relationships, what the signs of healthy and unhealthy relationships are, and how to navigate both romantic and platonic relationships.

name: Burmese Student Association
description: The Boston University Burmese Student Association is an all-inclusive student association primarily aiming to help promote Myanmar’s culture and tradition to the BU community through exciting interest information sessions and cultural events.
summary: The Boston University Burmese Student Association is an all-inclusive student association primarily aiming to help promote Myanmar’s culture and tradition to the BU community through exciting interest information sessions and cultural events.

name: Migration Tales
description: Increasing polarization on migration is a pressing issue of our globalized world; however, real migrant experience lacks media representation. So our mission is to create a story-sharing platform for migration narratives. Leveraging the power of storytelling, we hope to debunk migration myths and celebrate diversity, inclusivity, and empathy  by publishing stories and research. We aim to provide an accessible platform where people of all ages and educational backgrounds can learn more about migration, acquiring knowledge that was exclusively circulated among scholars and policymakers. Our team consists of writers, researchers, podcasters, and community outreach members.
summary: Migration Tales is a multi-media platform that aims to amplify Migrant and first-generation American voices through storytelling, one story at a time.

name: The Central Asian Student Association
description: CASA is the university-wide student organization tailored towards students from the Central Asian countries like Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan, Tajikistan, Uzbekistan, Turkmenistan and other Turkic countries, as well as those interested in gaining a better understanding of the Central Asian culture as a part of their academic and personal life journeys at Boston University. This is a cultural club for responding to the social and cultural needs of the Central Asian population and those from other Turkic countries who share cultural similarities and historical ties. The interactions that would happen among undergraduate and graduate students, alumni, faculty members at Boston University, the local Boston community and the Central Asian diaspora,  are aimed to represent and unite Boston University students to celebrate their extremely unique cultural heritage. All the cultural, social, educational and professional events that happen as a part of the club activities are based on mutual-respect, understanding and shared values, with CASA being the common ground to stimulate healthy and educative dialogue on the topics of history, politics, cultural norms, etc. between Boston University students and faculty members. CASA at BU is a safe space for students who may have found it difficult to find a sense of community within BU, considering the rarity of meeting others from the region.
summary: CASA is the university-wide student organization tailored towards students from the Central Asian countries like Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan, Tajikistan, Uzbekistan, Turkmenistan and other Turkic countries.

name: Emergency Medical Service Club
description: We aim to bring together current and future students in EMS, or anyone else interested in emergency medicine. We aim to educate and inspire people about emergency medicine and life-saving techniques through collaborative events and regular meetings.
summary: We aim to bring together current and future students in EMS, or anyone else interested in emergency medicine. We aim to educate and inspire people about emergency medicine and life-saving techniques through collaborative events and regular meetings.

name: Beatboxing at BU
description: The core purpose of the BU Beatboxing club is to enhance the BU community through showcasing the under-appreciated art form/subculture of beatboxing through providing teaching and education to musically inclined students to continue practice.
summary: The core purpose of the BU Beatboxing club is to enhance the BU community through showcasing the under-appreciated art form/subculture of beatboxing through providing teaching and education to musically inclined students to continue practice.

name: Prison Outreach Initiative at BU
description: Boston University Prison Outreach Initiative is dedicated to serving and advocating for incarcerated individuals, both locally and nationally. The core tenets of our organization are represented by our three main activities: volunteering, fundraising, and raising awareness. Our volunteer efforts will include organizing book drives for prison libraries with Prison Book Project, virtually tutoring incarcerated folks, creating Holiday Gift Bags with Concord Prison Outreach, phone-banking to politicians and clemency boards to encourage commutation of death sentences, and becoming pen pals with incarcerated people. Our fundraising activities will raise money for those who have recently been released from prison, reputable halfway houses, and legal funds for wrongfully incarcerated folks. Our educational efforts will include documentary screenings and a book club about the prison industrial complex, procuring speakers such as Sister Helen Prejean or Bryan Stevenson to speak with us about prison reform, and raising awareness via social media about the US criminal justice system.
summary: BUPOI's mission is to serve and advocate for incarcerated individuals, both locally and throughout the US. We will do so by volunteering with and fundraising for incarcerated communities, as well as by educating fellow students about prison reform.

name: Quiz Bowl Club
description: Quiz Bowl is a competition where teams go head-to-head to answer trivia questions first and try to score the most points against the other team. Boston University Quiz Bowl club is an organization where people who are interested in academic trivia can come together to discuss their favorite topics, practice, and prepare for Quiz Bowl tournaments. Information about local or online tournaments will be discussed and practice/study sessions will be held at meetings. The club is open to anyone and no prior experience of Quiz Bowl is required. The mission of the Boston University Quiz Bowl club is to create a friendly environment where people can have fun competing against each other, study/learn for Quiz Bowl, and compete in intercollegiate tournaments.
summary: The BU Quiz Bowl club is a student organization where people who are interested in academic trivia can come together to discuss their favorite topics, practice, and prepare for team-based tournaments.

name: HackHardware
description: Founded in 2021, HackHardware is an organization that's focus is to provide students the opportunity to innovate and help in the creation of the next big thing! Every month, HackHardware will be hosting events such as tutorials in software such as Solidworks and AutoCAD, beginner & advanced workshops on using Arduino, and even collaboration with other Engineering & Computer Science clubs at Boston University. Alongside this, HackHardware will have an annual free Hardware-focused hackathon, where students from Boston University and other local universities can create their own innovations based on the tracks and limitations provided! HackHardware is open for students of all years, majors and skill levels, and focuses on catering to our innovational spirit!
summary: HackHardware is an organization that helps organize Hardware-focused hackathons and events for the Boston University community. We also host BU's first hardware-focused hackathon, open to all majors and all experiences!

name: Assisting Refugees through Initiative, Service & Education
description: A service and education club seeking to improve the lives of those affected by forced displacement. We facilitate service and foster discussions around topics relating to immigrants, refugees, asylum seekers, homeless populations, etc.
summary: A service club oriented towards improving the lives of those affected by forced displacement ie. immigrants, refugees, asylum seekers, homeless populations etc.

name: CDS Student Government
description: The Computing and Data Sciences (CDS) student government serves the interdisciplinary student community at CDS. Through advocacy, communication, and collaboration with our fellow peers, we aspire to make CDS feel like a tight-knit community. As we move into our new home in the Center, we hope to plan engaging events, build relationships with the administration, and always look out for the interests of CDS students. We always welcome new ideas and members in our meetings so feel free to come. Please let us know if you ever have any questions or concerns, and hope to see you at a CDS student government gathering. Feel free to follow our instagram: @cds.stugov
summary: The Computing and Data Sciences (CDS) student government serves the interdisciplinary student community at CDS. Through advocacy, communication, and collaboration with our fellow peers, we aspire to make CDS feel like a tight-knit community.

name: SHADES
description:
SHADES is a community-centered organization for Boston University’s queer and trans students of color.

 

SHADES is the home for BU’s QTBIPOC students on campus. We provide a safe space for these students to find community, express themselves and engage with events that center and cater to our experiences.

 

We host meetings where our members can both engage in enriching conversations about topics and issues related to our experiences and enjoy unique, dynamic social events to relax and have fun. Additionally, we love to collaborate with other student groups, organizations and businesses that align with our vision and mission!
summary: SHADES is a community-centered organization for Boston University’s queer and trans students of color.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc
description:
Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc. is a historical Black Greek-letter fraternity founded on the campus of Indiana University, Bloomington, Indiana on January 5th 1911. It was founded by 10 astute men with the fundamental purpose of the fraternity being "honorable achievement in every field of human endeavor. 
 
Chi Chapter was chartered on the Boston University campus on February 15, 1924, by the significant six: Maceo Hubbard, Julian Evans, Courtney Cooley, Theodore Nathaniel D. Scott, J. Logan Jenkins, and John Hunter. Chi Chapter is the 22nd undergraduate chapter of Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc. and the fifth undergraduate charter to be awarded in the then-Eastern Province (now Northeastern Province).
summary: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc. is a historical Black Greek-letter fraternity founded on the campus of Indiana University, Bloomington, Indiana on January 5th 1911.

name: PMReady
description:
Imagine turning your tech ideas into a reality, all without ever touching a line of code. That’s what product management is all about.
Learn about business and the process of software development and own your own product at PMReady.

PMReady hosts weekly developmental workshops and speaker events to provide experience to aspiring Product Managers (PM). PMReady's workshops guide members through 4 stages of Product Management: Research, Planning, Prototyping, and Pitching. Over the course of a semester, members learn all the foundational skills needed to become a successful PM. You’ll learn about:
• Conducting insightful user interviews
• Writing concise Product Requirement Documents
• Utilizing Jira and Figma to their full potential

Join PMReady
If you're interested in attending our meetings, sign up for our Email Newsletter, join the Slack, and follow our Instagram. If you have any additional questions or comments, feel free to reach out to us by email (pmready@bu.edu).
summary: PMReady provides students a hands-on product management experience through developmental workshops and speaker events. Students will learn about product management and create a product prototype by the end of the semester.

name: Undergraduate Sociology Association
description: The BU Undergraduate Sociology Association (BUUSA) strives to engage Sociology majors/minors and the BU undergraduate community on the themes, concepts, and skills central to the discipline of Sociology. Members of BUUSA are invested in contemplating sociological issues while applying differing sociological perspectives to the social world. Events and meetings sponsored by the BUUSA are meant to unify and engage students who are interested in sociology. BUUSA’s mission is to enhance undergraduate students’ understanding of the discipline, engage Sociology majors/minors, and encourage stronger relationships with Sociology faculty.
summary: The Undergraduate Sociology Association (USA) strives to engage Sociology majors/minors and the BU undergraduate community on the themes, concepts, and skills central to the discipline of Sociology.

name: BlackBox: Boston University's Black Film Society
description: BlackBox is Boston University's Black film society. BlackBox invites Black and P.O.C filmmakers to pitch stories that might be scarier to share with those that don't have the experience of being in the minority. This safe space will not only provide a home away from home to those that might be struggling to find their community in BU's massive student population, but facilitate networking opportunities, which is essential for a career field like film and television production. BlackBox wants to create a space where Black students can discover and develop interests and skills in the film space.
summary: BlackBox provides a place for Black as well as P.O.C filmmakers to come together and create films relevant to their unique experiences, facilitating a sense of community and providing career resources.

name: Etc. Choir
description: Etc. Choir provides members of the BU community with the opportunity to sing in an inclusive ensemble which performs SATB works. Unique from other choral ensembles at BU, Etc. Choir was founded and led by students studying Music Education. Our goals center around teaching our members how to sing any type or genre of music Efficiently, Tastefully, and Consistently. Rehearsals will provide knowledge about choral singing and acting techniques. Etc. Choir will concentrate on performing music from a broad spectrum, including but not limited to Western and Non-Western classical, musical theater, and popular styles of music in a choral setting. A primary goal of this choir is to be inclusive to the Transgender and Queer communities who often struggle in the choral world due to the gendered nature of vocal parts. As such, vocal parts are assigned per song based on a student’s most comfortable range rather than labeling a person to only one vocal part. Etc. Choir strives to be ensemble-centered and ensures that everyone is able to participate at all times.
summary: Etc. Choir is an inclusive, education-focused, member-centered choral ensemble that performs a wide range of music such as Western and Non-Western classical, musical theater, and popular styles of music in a choral setting.

name: SciBU: Journal of Undergraduate Science and Research
description:
Section 1. Purpose

SciBU is a life sciences journal at Boston University. Managed by undergraduates with guidance from a Student Group Advisor, this organization creates a forum for undergraduate students to discuss and exchange their perspectives on the life sciences and its role in society. SciBU regularly publishes articles on its website and produces an annual print edition available for in-person distribution. Undergraduate students are encouraged to submit original works of life sciences writing, news, research, and art for publication. 

 

Section 2. Current Objectives

  1. To provide the undergraduate students of Boston University with numerous opportunities to explore scientific editing and writing through events, seminars, and submissions.
  2. To provide Editorial Board members of SciBU with various opportunities for involvement in aspects of scientific publishing, writing, editing, funding, design, etc.
  3. To collaborate with BU academic departments, student organizations, UROP participants, and writing and biology classes to solicit submissions.
  4. To develop experienced science communicators and promote the value of effective science communication in academia, industry, journalism, etc.
summary: SciBU is a life science journal managed by undergraduates that creates a forum for students to discuss and exchange their perspectives on the life sciences and its role in society. SciBU regularly publishes online and produces an annual print edition.

name: Ghanaian Student Organization
summary: The Ghanaian Student Organization aims to build a strong sense of community between BU students of Ghanaian descent. Our community will be strengthened through events based on Ghanaian culture and collaboration with relevant African organizations.

name: National Society of Black Women in Medicine
description: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine, as a national student and professional based organization, thereby dedicate itself to the development of intensive programs for increasing the participation of Black women in the fields of healthcare and science. These programs will be initiated both within and outside the institution community and will serve to strengthen relationships between the professional industry and the black community. Members of this organization are encouraged to join and participate in their individual professional societies. This organization will endeavor to provide general counseling to all members. Applications are open in the Fall of each academic year.
summary: NSBWM strives to create a safe community space for individuals passionate about advancing black women in all stages of their medical journey. Our goal is to foster relationships by providing networking opportunities and support for young women.

name: Community Gardens at BU
description: Boston University has a dedication and an obligation to advance climate resilience goals such as strong communities and local food systems. Join this club to help build a greener campus ecosystem with fresh local produce and learn about food production!
summary: Community group growing a local food system and botanical/horticultural education on campus.

name: Kappa Theta Pi
description: Kappa Theta Pi is the nation's first technology-based professional fraternity, accepting students from all majors. KTP has five pillars: professional development, alumni connections, social growth, technological advancement, and academic support.
summary: Kappa Theta Pi is the nation's first technology-based professional fraternity, accepting students from all majors. KTP has five pillars: professional development, alumni connections, social growth, technological advancement, and academic support.

name: North African Student Organization
description: The North African Student Organization (NASO) at Boston University is a club open to all full-time undergraduate and graduate students – mainly supporting and empowering the North African community. The organization’s aim and purpose is serving as a hub for all North African scholars to connect and form meaningful connections with one another. Moreover, NASO will also serve as an opportunity for cultural rooting and awareness – for those willing to learn more about MENA culture.
summary: NASO at BU will continue on its quest to support & empower the currently underserved community of North African undergrads. Through cultural nights, alumni panels, networking fairs, and athletic events, NASO will be an active force of support and unity.

name: Artificial Intelligence Society
description:
BUAIS is an interdisciplinary academic club that brings together students with a passion for exploring the vast realm of artificial intelligence (AI). The club is open to all Boston University students, regardless of their major or academic year. We aim to foster an inclusive, innovative, and intellectually stimulating environment that encourages members to engage in discussions, develop projects, and participate in events related to AI.

The club's primary objective is to promote the understanding of AI, its potential applications across a range of disciplines, and its ethical, societal, and economic implications. BUAIS provides opportunities for students to gain hands-on experience with AI and machine learning technologies (with focus on Computer Vision and Natural Language Processing), foster connections with industry professionals and AI researchers, and actively contribute to a rapidly evolving field.

Motivation for the club:

The advent of AI has revolutionized our world, impacting a wide range of fields including healthcare, finance, transportation, entertainment, and many more. As AI continues to advance, so too does the demand for skilled professionals in this field. BUAIS aims to fill the gap between classroom learning and practical, real-world application of AI, equipping students with the necessary skills and knowledge to thrive in this emerging field.

In addition, BUAIS will serve as a platform for students to discuss and debate the ethical, societal, and economic implications of AI, encouraging members to consider these important issues from diverse perspectives. We believe that this multifaceted understanding of AI is crucial in shaping future AI policies, systems, and applications.
summary: BUAIS at Boston University is an inclusive club fostering AI passion among students. We facilitate discussions, project development, and AI events, promoting comprehensive AI understanding for students at BU.

name: Dodgeball Club
description: The Boston University Dodgeball Club will host weekly Dodgeball games, open to all full-time and part-time BU students. Courts will be rented for the duration of an hour, and we will play as many games as we can within that hour. We will follow a classic set of dodgeball rules, with two teams of equal strengths attempting to win by hitting all the players on the other team. We will use classic size, soft dodgeballs to prevent any kind of injury, and will maintain a safe environment for all of our members. These games will build teamwork, a love of sports, create lasting connections between BU students and will be, above all, very fun!
summary: A Dodgeball Club for BU open to everyone, with weekly games.

name: Graphic Medicine Club
description: Graphic Medicine Club is a student-led organization that explores the intersection between the medium of graphic narratives and the discourse surrounding healthcare. We aim to create a diverse community of BU students who are fans of comics and medicine in order to spread awareness of the field of graphic medicine by facilitating student collaborations with other student clubs, authors, artists, and art therapists. At the Graphic Medicine Club, students will learn more about graphic medicine’s use as a therapeutic and educational tool for patients and healthcare providers, and gain opportunities to share their own experiences through art.
summary: Discover the healing and teaching power of art in healthcare and medicine. BU’s Graphic Medicine Club welcomes students interested in healthcare and medical professions to explore visual storytelling and practice empathy in medicine. Join us!

name: Arché, Boston University's Student-Led Journal of Philosophy
description: Arché, Boston University’s Student-Led Journal of Philosophy, is edited and published by motivated, inquisitive undergraduate students from diverse academic backgrounds in the College of Arts & Sciences in coordination with the Department of Philosophy. We strive to highlight exceptional student work, providing undergraduates and graduates at BU and around the world with a platform to share their writing with a general philosophical readership. We are dedicated to helping students bridge the gap between the kind of writing that is expected of them in their coursework and the kind of writing that is expected of an academic philosopher. For more information and to attend a general meeting, please contact the editors at arche@bu.edu.
summary: Arché is a journal of philosophy edited & published by students of the CAS in coord. w/ the Dept. of Phil. We strive to highlight exceptional student work, providing some with the opportunity to publish their work for a general philosophical readership.

name: Youth Empowerment Ministry Boston University
summary: A non-denominational Youth and Young Adults Ministry striving to empower young people to live for and serve God in their local communities through fellowship, bible studies, and worship nights.

name: Alliance of Portuguese Speakers
description: A student-run organization that focuses on bringing together students of Portuguese heritage and speakers of Portuguese. We hope to connect students from a myriad of diverse cultural backgrounds to practice, learn, and enrich individual knowledge of Portuguese culture and language.
summary: A student-run organization that focuses on bringing together students of Portuguese heritage and speakers of Portuguese.

name: Songwriters Circle
description:
BU Songwriters Circle

Welcome to the BU Songwriting Circle! Our organization is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community of aspiring songwriters, musicians, and lyricists. Whether you're a seasoned songwriter or just starting out, our club provides a supportive environment where creativity and collaboration thrive.

Our mission is to inspire and empower students to express themselves through music. We believe that everyone has a unique story to tell, and through songwriting, we can share these stories with the world. Our club offers a variety of activities and resources to help you develop your skills and connect with like-minded individuals.

Key features of the College Songwriting Club include:

  • Workshops and Seminars: Led by experienced members, these sessions cover topics such as songwriting techniques, lyrical composition, music theory, and performance skills.
  • Collaborative Projects: Team up with fellow members to co-write songs, produce tracks, and perform live. These projects provide valuable hands-on experience and foster a sense of community.
  • Open Mic Nights: Showcase your original songs in a supportive environment. These events are a great opportunity to gain performance experience and receive constructive feedback.
  • Songwriting Challenges: Participate in themed songwriting challenges designed to spark creativity and push your boundaries.

Join us at the College Songwriting Club and be part of a community where your musical ideas can flourish and your voice can be heard!
summary: Our club fosters a community of songwriters, musicians, and lyricists. Whether you're experienced or just starting, we provide a supportive environment for creativity and collaboration. Our mission is to empower students to express themselves with music!

name: Brothers United
summary: Our mission is to foster meaningful connections and strengthen the bonds among men of color, providing them with a sense of belonging and a home away from home.

name: On Beat
description: BU on Beat is a student organization dedicated to promoting the vibrant culture of house music within our university community. Our mission is to provide a supportive and inclusive environment where aspiring DJs and electronic music enthusiasts can come together to learn, collaborate, and share their passion for house music. Through collaboration and mutual learning, we aim to empower our members of varying levels to develop their skills, express their creativity through mixing events, and contribute positively to the local music scene. Anyone is welcome, beginner or otherwise!
summary: BU on Beat is a student org that promotes the vibrant culture of house music within our university community. Our mission is to provide a supportive and inclusive environment where aspiring DJs can collaborate, mix, and share their love for music!

name: La Casa Peruana
summary: La Casa Peruana is BU’s home for Peruvian students and those interested in our culture! Whether you’re a fellow Peruvian feeling homesick, or someone interested in learning more about our country, La Casa Peruana is open to everyone in the BU community.

name: Sustainable Business Club
description: The Boston University Sustainable Business Club’s is dedicated to fostering a community of future business leaders committed to environmental and social responsibility. Our mission is to allow students to be involved with business opportunities that help inspire, educate, and empower students to integrate sustainability into their careers and contribute positively to the global business landscape. Through guest speakers we will inspire members of our club to become involved in the sustainable sector of business, showing jobs and functions of this path in the market. We are dedicated to raising awareness about the importance of sustainability all while engaging in meaningful discussions on environmental and social issues, which we will share through our monthly newsletter. In addition, members will be given the opportunity to gain hands-on experience with the aspects of merging business success while keeping sustainable practices through semester-long case competitions. Our vision for the Sustainable Business Club at Boston University is to be a pioneering force in reshaping the future of business through college students who are emerging into the workforce. We aspire to create a world where sustainability is ingrained in the DNA of every organization, where business serves as a catalyst for positive change, and where the next generation of leaders are unwavering champions of ethical, environmentally conscious, and socially responsible practices.
summary: The Sustainable Business Club is a place for students who care about doing well and doing good. We focus on businesses that can profit while also working to improve society. The club focuses on this crossroad and allows for experiences in this area.

name: Computer Science Club
description: BUCSC is an undergraduate club that provides a platform for students to enhance their coding and problem-solving skills through speakers, networking events, and guidance. We create an environment for students who are CS majors or are interested in programming to create camaraderie with each other and foster their computer science journey.
summary: BU CSC aims to help computer science students prepare for their careers by providing a supportive environment, resume workshops, as well as technical and behavioral interview help. In order to strive through the difficulties of the modern tech world.

name: Phi Gamma Nu
summary: PGN is a national business fraternity promoting diversity, equity, and entrepreneurial spirit, and integrity. Our goal is to foster professional development and provide a supportive community for young, motivated individuals preparing for future career.

name: Journalists of Color
description: A group for Undergraduate students at Boston University. JOC is a student organization dedicated to providing a space for conversation, community, advocacy, and mentorship for journalists who identify as people of color or for BU students interested in journalism. We intend on having bi-weekly meetings, special events, and lectures with guest speakers within the Boston community.
summary: JOC is a student organization focused on career development, advocacy, and mentorship for journalists of color at BU and BU students interested in journalism. We intend on having bi-weekly meetings, special events, and lectures with guest speakers.

name: Afrithms
description:
Afrithms proudly represents Boston University as its premier African dance troupe, celebrating a decade of captivating performances that fuse the rich rhythms and movements of diverse African cultures. Founded on a passion for cultural expression, Afrithms has not only entertained audiences but also competed and showcased their talent at numerous prestigious venues and events throughout the years. Their energetic performances have become a staple in the Greater Boston Area, leaving a lasting impression wherever they appear.

At the heart of Afrithms' success is their commitment to self-expression and creativity. The majority of their choreography is original, crafted to reflect the latest trends in African dance and music. This dedication to innovation ensures that each performance is both authentic and dynamic, captivating audiences with its blend of tradition and contemporary flair.

Beyond their performances, Afrithms serves as a cultural hub on campus, fostering a community where students from diverse backgrounds come together to explore and celebrate African and Caribbean heritage through dance. Their rehearsals and workshops not only hone dance skills but also promote cultural understanding and appreciation among participants.
summary: Afrithms is a primarily Afro dance team dedicated to fostering a disciplined environment characterized by excellence, accountability, effective communication, and inclusivity. The team also serves as a celebratory melting pot of cultures and ideas.

name: F1Tenth Autonomous Racing Organization at Boston University
description:
The F1Tenth Autonomous Car Racing Organization or ACRO at Boston University is a student-led organization dedicated to advancing research and innovation in autonomous systems, with a specific focus on the intersection of motorsports and computer vision algorithms. Our mission is threefold: to ignite passion for cutting-edge technologies, inspire curiosity in the field of robotics and artificial intelligence, and cultivate analytical thinking skills among our members.

At the heart of our club lies a passion for a fascination with the intricate algorithms that power autonomous vehicles on a competitive level where we are constantly pushing new grounds for research and development. We believe that the high-speed, dynamic environment of racing tracks provides a unique testbed for developing and refining vision algorithms, decision-making systems, and control mechanisms that can later be applied to real-world autonomous vehicles.

Our club members work collaboratively on designing, building, and programming 1/10th scale autonomous race cars, leveraging state-of-the-art computer vision techniques, machine learning models, and control systems. We strive to push the boundaries of what is possible in the realm of autonomous systems, exploring cutting-edge technologies such as deep learning, sensor fusion, and advanced perception algorithms.

Through these hands-on projects, workshops, and competitions, our goal is for our members to gain invaluable practical experience in areas such as robotics, embedded systems, and software engineering. We encourage interdisciplinary collaboration, fostering an environment where students from diverse backgrounds, including computer science, electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, and physics, can contribute their unique perspectives and skills.

Moreover, the F1Tenth Club at Boston University serves as a hub for knowledge-sharing and community-building. We organize guest lectures, seminars, and industry collaborations as well as collaborations with other schools such as MIT, providing our members with opportunities to learn from experts in the field and gain insights into the latest developments in autonomous systems.

By participating in the F1Tenth Club, students not only develop technical skills but also cultivate essential soft skills such as teamwork, problem-solving, and communication. Our club fosters an inclusive and supportive environment, encouraging members to explore their interests, take calculated risks, and learn from their failures.

Ultimately, the F1Tenth Autonomous Racing Club at Boston University aims to inspire the next generation of innovators and pioneers in the field of autonomous systems, contributing to the advancement of cutting-edge technologies that will shape the future of transportation, robotics, and beyond.
summary: The F1Tenth Autonomous Car Racing is dedicated to fueling innovation in the research and development of competition-grade autonomous racing ground vehicles. The goal of the club is to train an in-house team to annually compete in the F1Tenth Competition.

name: Cape Verdean Student Association
description: The Cape Verdean Student Association seeks to create a community on campus open to students of Cape Verdean ancestry to connect, engage in a shared culture, and bond with others through various events and general body meetings. Cape Verdeans are present throughout Massachusetts in areas such as Dorchester, Brockton, New Bedford, Fall River, etc. We believe that founding Boston University’s first Cape Verdean Student Association allows us to create a unified student group where students can represent and take pride in our country and culture, and set the stage for future Cape Verdean students to connect with others. However, CVSA is not catered to only Cape Verdean students, CVSA encourages all interested students to indulge themselves in a new culture and learn about CV culture, language, food, and history. This club aspires to collaborate with other cultural institutions on and off campus to highlight and acknowledge the beauty of other under-represented cultures around the world and promote cross-cultural unity. Through the organization, we hope to fundraise for the Cape Verdean islands for multiple causes and provide resources such as school supplies, clothing, canned goods, and other essentials. We are eager to host fun events such as dance classes and cultural food nights to spread our culture with the BU’s student body. Introducing a Cape Verdean Student Association to Boston University’s campus would undoubtedly give a voice to an underrepresented ethnic group, and help create a recognized diverse environment on campus.
summary: The Cape Verdean Student Association is committed to promoting inclusivity, diversity, and civic engagement by showcasing collaborative and cultural events, working with local Cape Verdean organizations, and amplifying Cape Verdean students’ voices.

name: SOON Movement
description: We are an interdenominational ministry committed to spreading the Gospel of Jesus Christ. Our mission is to help fulfill the Great Commission in our generation (Matt. 28:18-20) by winning, building, and sending in the power of the Holy Spirit. We hope to build the body of Christ through prayer, evangelism, and discipleship. It is our desire that we would be used by God to help reach students and faculty with the Gospel so that every person on campus will know someone who truly follows Jesus Christ. Isaiah 53:2 “He grew before him like a tender shoot, and like a root out of dry ground. He had no beauty or majesty to attract us to him, nothing in his appearance that we should desire him.” SOON is a Korean synonym for tender shoot (sprout).
summary: We are an evangelistic Christian ministry on campus that holds weekly large groups. Meetings include fellowship, prayer, and biblical discussions. We welcome anyone interested in getting to know more about Christianity and our purpose.

name: Students of East African Ancestry
description: SEAA is an organization providing the space for students to promote East African cultures and socio-political issues related to East Africa and the diaspora. East African countries (and their related ethnic groups and cultures) represented include: Burundi, Comoros, Djibouti, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Kenya, Madagascar, Malawi, Mauritius, Mozambique, Rwanda, Seychelles, Somalia, Somaliland, Sudan, South Sudan Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia, and Zimbabwe.
summary: SEAA is an organization dedicated to celebrating East African cultures, raising awareness of socio-political issues affecting the region and its diaspora, and creating a space for dialogue, advocacy, and community engagement.

name: Off The Hook
description: Our community service initiative meets weekly to crochet hand warmers and winter gear to donate to homeless groups within Boston through the community service centre. It is an excellent way to get community service hours in while learning a new transformative skill with mental and physical benefits.
summary: “Off The Hook” is a community initiative that aims to provide a realistic and relaxing way for college students to give back to the community whilst also learning a new hobby.

name: Sahelian Student Association
description:
The Sahelian Student Association (SSA) at Boston University is dedicated to uniting Sahelian students and raising awareness about issues facing the Sahel region in Africa. Through events like Sudan Night, where we spotlight the ongoing conflict in Sudan, we strive to educate our community about social conflicts within the region. 

 

Our commitment to cultural celebration is evident in our diverse array of events, from a Cultural Hot-Takes event to a Waist-bead Making Night. These activities provide opportunities for members to learn about the rich traditions, languages, and customs of the Sahel region.

 

SSA also acts as a bridge, fostering connections and friendships that transcend borders. We create a welcoming environment for Sahelian students and non-Sahelian students alike, by  promoting a sense of belonging and support. Additionally, we partner with local nonprofits for community outreach, contributing to the wider African community in the Boston area.
summary: The Sahelian Student Association (SSA) at Boston University unites Sahelian students, raises awareness about Sahel region issues, and fosters cultural celebration through events like Sudan Night, Cultural Hot-Takes, and Waist-bead Making Night.

name: FilmBiz: Boston University’s Entertainment Business Club
description: FilmBiz is Boston University’s first student organization dedicated to students interested in careers within the business and management areas of the entertainment industry (film, television, theater, music, etc). Our mission is to educate students about often-overlooked opportunities in management and behind-the-scenes roles that intersect the worlds of entertainment and business. To achieve our mission, we provide professional events such as industry professional panelists, career workshops, networking events, and social events.
summary: FilmBiz is Boston University’s first student organization dedicated to students interested in careers within the business and management areas of the entertainment industry (film, television, theater, music, etc).

name: Conscious Educators of Color
description: The Conscious Educators of Color club (C.E.C.) is dedicated to creating a welcoming and inclusive environment for Black and Brown educators and their peers to grow, have fun, and foster strong connections while advocating for justice-based education.
summary: The Conscious Educators of Color Club (C.E.C.) is dedicated to creating a welcoming and inclusive environment for Black and Brown educators to grow, have fun, and foster strong connections while advocating for justice-based education.

name: Rhythm n Beat
description: Rhythm N Beat Step Team is a team that promotes the beauty of Black culture through step dancing. As a group, we work to build a community within a community by connecting people across campus through this art. Aside from member responsibilities such as attending practice and practicing steps in their freetime, members will also have a fun and safe space to escape from academic, social, and even the internal stressors that they may deal with. RnB is open to anyone who is interested in joining! We serve a performance group, but we hold workshops and spread education about Black culture as well! Check us out on instagram @rnbstep!
summary: Rhythm N Beat Step Team is a team that promotes the beauty of Black culture through step dancing. As a group, we work to build a community within a community by connecting people across campus through this art.

name: The Film Production Club of Boston University
summary: The Boston University Short Film club is dedicated to educating passionate filmmakers and providing Boston University film students with an open, film-oriented community. We focus on producing short films, and hosting club events and outings.

name: Her Financial Future
description:
Welcome to Her Financial Future @ Boston University (@herfinancialfuturebu)! We started our organization as a safe space to teach all women and non-binary individuals (in all majors) about personal finance and investing. Despite there being great Questrom student organizations dedicated to stock trading, finance, financial modeling, and investing, we noticed a need for a club to teach women about personal finance and the basics of investing. So many of these clubs are open to all, but are heavily male-dominated, and it can be very daunting to join if you are a woman or if you aren't a Questrom major. 

We don't want HFF to feel intimidating to join at all. HFF is a safe space for women and non-binary individuals to learn more about how to sustain themselves financially and just about personal finance in general. 

In the Fall 2025 semester, we will be hosting bi-weekly workshops starting 9/9/25, and we will notify attendees of upcoming workshops, speaker events, or networking events via our Instagram (@herfinancialfuturebu) and via our newsletter. Sign up here! tinyurl.com/HFFbu2025

We hope to see you this semester! :)
summary: Her Financial Future focuses on teaching women about personal finance, investing, and becoming more financially independent. We host bi-weekly workshops on various topics, and are open to women & non-binary individuals of all majors & backgrounds! :)

name: Terrier Ride Engineering Club
description: To showcase the unique and vast career field that exists within the amusement and theme park industry to other engineers, provide networking opportunities to students, explore and practice with tools and techniques used in the industry, and create a community for theme park enthusiasts.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to showcase the unique career field that exists within the theme park industry and create a community for theme park enthusiasts

name: Pioneer Impact Consulting
description:
Pioneer Impact Consulting provides students a chance to consult on projects with a variety of businesses for social impact - providing skill support and powerful insights into various dimensions of the client's businesses, while gaining experience to help entrepreneurs grow and develop their businesses. Organization will connect with clients from a variety of businesses (including original focus area of ambitious refugees and immigrants) to develop their business ideas with the help of student members to work as consultants, providing skill support and powerful insights into various dimensions of the client's businesses. 

PIC Club will provide students with the opportunity to work with these clients and help them strengthen their business and consulting skills. Through the organization, students will have the opportunity to engage with diverse perspectives, gain real-world entrepreneurial experience, and contribute to the growth of businesses.

The purpose of the club is to train students in the fields of consulting, strategy, and business development, providing key insights into how real businesses work by connecting them with clients. The E-Board will connect and oversee the members to ensure effective coordination and efficient assistance. This oversight includes monitoring resource allocation, evaluating program effectiveness, and maintaining communication channels to address any emerging needs or challenges promptly. The goal is to optimize impact and uphold accountability in serving our clients and community stakeholders.
summary: Pioneer Impact Consulting provides students a chance to consult on projects with a variety of businesses for social impact - providing skill support and powerful insights into various dimensions of the client's businesses, whilst gaining experience

name: Campus Closet BU
description: Campus Closet (formerly BU Closet) is Boston University's shared closet, empowering students to become conscious consumers through free, sustainable, and accessible clothing swaps. We regularly host clothing drives and swaps, offering students a chance to connect, collaborate, and engage in circular consumption. More than a resource for sustainable fashion, Campus Closet is a platform for creative expression and community building, bridging campus organizations to foster inclusivity and environmental stewardship.
summary: At Campus Closet (formerly BU Closet), our mission is to cultivate a sense of community and environmental responsibility by offering a platform for individuals to participate in free, sustainable, & circular clothing consumption.

name: Khmer Student Association at Boston University
description: The Khmer Student Association at Boston University is dedicated to fostering a vibrant community of Khmer students on campus. It serves as a welcoming space for individuals to explore and engage with Khmer culture, providing opportunities for Khmer Americans to reconnect with their heritage and offering a supportive environment for international students from Cambodia. The association advocates for community and unity, reflecting this through cultural, educational, and social events. Membership is open to all BU students who have an interest in Khmer culture and related studies. The club hosts general body meetings at least once a month, coordinated by the President, along with regular executive board meetings to ensure smooth operation and planning of events. The organization is committed to inclusivity, with no membership fees and a policy against discrimination of any kind. The executive board oversees the club's activities and initiatives, aiming to cultivate a strong Khmer community within Boston University and beyond.
summary: The Khmer Student Association at Boston University fosters a community of Khmer students, offers a space to explore Khmer culture, and helps Khmer Americans reconnect with their roots. Open to all BU students interested in Khmer culture.

name: Gliding & Soaring at Boston University
description: The Boston University Gliding & Soaring Club is a recreational group offering gliding and soaring experiences, aviation events, and glider training.
