We provide a range of activities, including VR simulators, ground school, and affordable gliding opportunities for aviation enthusiasts. Our events include glider build nights, movie nights, resume workshops, and talks with experienced professionals in the aviation industry. Our club is open to anyone interested in gliding and soaring. Whether you're a seasoned pilot or new to aviation, join our community of like-minded individuals who share a passion for the skies!
summary: The BU Gliding & Soaring Club offers gliding and soaring experiences, aviation events, and training. We offer VR simulators, ground school, and affordable gliding for aviation enthusiasts. Join our community and share a passion for the skies!

name: Generational Black American Student Organization
description: SoulAAn, the Generational Black American Student Organization(GBA) at Boston University, is committed to advancing the cultural enrichment, academic excellence, and social empowerment of GBA students on campus. We aim to empower by providing resources and a supportive environment for GBA students to succeed academically and cultivate leadership skills. We celebrate our culture through hosting engaging events, workshops, and cultural celebrations that educate and enlighten the campus community about the richness of Black history and heritage. We seek to get involved both on campus and within the wider community while addressing pertinent issues impacting GBA students on campus and in admissions. In addition, we aim to work collaboratively with other student organizations in the Diaspora, faculty, and staff. In alignment with these principles, SoulAAn is dedicated to creating a space where generational Black American students can flourish, connect, and make a meaningful impact both on campus and beyond. While we are a “minority within a minority” on campus, this term doesn’t define us—it is through our intellect, cultural prowess, and rich history that we form a longstanding community of generational Black Americans on campus.
summary: SoulAAn is committed to advancing the cultural enrichment, academic excellence, and social empowerment of Generational Black American students(GBA) on campus. We aim to celebrate our culture by hosting events, workshops, and cultural celebrations.

name: Visual Journalists Association
description: We aim to promote and support student visual journalists in the advancement of their craft and career through portfolio reviews, connecting members with industry professionals, photography contest workshops, and more!
summary: BUJVA is a professional club to support student photojournalism and other types of visual journalism.

name: Global Tea Club at Boston University
description: From the sweet tea of the Southern United States, to British afternoon tea, to the Japanese tea ceremony, and to Moroccan mint tea and beyond, 「Tea」is an essential part of life in many different cultures around the world. The aim of this organization is to mutually come to a deeper understanding of the world's various cultures through the lens of tea from around the globe. This includes a focus on tea education and appreciation within a culture and between cultures, such as the different varieties of tea in Chinese tea culture and how the Chinese tea ceremony varies from the tea ceremonies present in Japan and Korea. We hope to create a space for people passionate about tea to be able to comfortably try tea from different facets of the world and gain knowledge of various aspects of different cultures that may otherwise feel inaccessible. In addition, we hope to be a conduit through which all members can share and educate other members on their own culture through tea. In this way, creating an organization where mutual continued learning is possible and sustainable, allowing members to gain a more well rounded understanding of Boston University's diverse student body in the process.
summary: The organization's main goal is promotion of tea as method to appreciate and gain a deeper understanding of the world's cultural diversity and create a safe space for that to occur.

name: Society For Biomaterials
description: We aim to identify students who demonstrate an interest in biomaterials, to generate student interest and interaction with biomaterials, and to aid the efficacy of students seeking research, education, and professional development opportunities.
summary: Boston University’s Student Chapter of the Society For Biomaterials promotes student research, education, and professional development in biomaterials and related disciplines while furthering the advancement of biomaterials research and education.

name: Dance Team
description: The team performs at men’s and women’s basketball games, on-campus events, community service events, and local dance showcases, and competitions. The team competes in the Division I category at the NDA Collegiate National Championship in Daytona Beach, FL. The team has finished in the Top Ten since 2013 and won National Titles in 2014 (Hip Hop) and 2016 (Team Performance).
summary: Boston University Dance Team (“BUDT”)  is comprised of a group of talented and dedicated athletes.

name: Cheer Team
description: The Boston University Cheerleading Team is a competitive NCA Division I Team. BU Cheer brings Terrier spirit throughout Boston University and in the community. The team cheers and performs at all men's and women's home basketball games, brings their cheerful spirits to hockey games where they participate in activities such as t-shirt tosses and face painting/tattoos and provides spirit for other on-campus and community events.
summary: The Boston University Cheerleading Team is a competitive NCA Division I Team.

name: Venture Group BU
description:
Founded to bring innovators from around the world together and thrive in the vibrant atmosphere of Boston's Startup Scene, BUVCG's core values are "Innovate, Inspire, and Impact".

We teach our members what it means to be in the VC industry through our rigorous Sourcing Analyst program: a semester-long program that includes workshops, deliverables, speaker series, tutoring hours, and a final presentation on the Term Sheet they create along the way. Our program allows members to experience both the Founder's and Investor's side of VC. In addition, we encourage our top Analysts to explore and continue their passion outside of work by inviting them to speak on our bi-weekly Podcast named "Venture Capital Today". This not only incentivizes members to dive deeper into independent research but also allows us to engage with a broader audience on social media and spread awareness of what VC truly is.

BUVCG also aims to break barriers for marginalized groups to enter. We host an Annual Conference called "Women in VC" with the goal of changing the current VC landscape where women are only 2% of the industry.

The group does not require any prior experience in VC to join, anyone with any level of knowledge is welcome.

To learn more, visit our website: https://buvcg.org/

Follow us on social media for updates and reach out if you have any questions!
Email: buvcgroup@gmail.com
Instagram: bu_vcg
LinkedIn: Boston University Venture Capital Group
summary: We provide a professional and social learning environment to students who are passionate about Venture Capital or want to learn more about the industry. In our organization, we train sourcing analysts, host conferences, and engage in VC competitions.

name: Jewish on Campus at Boston University
description:
Jewish on Campus (JOC) is the movement of the Jewish future. We seek to revive pride in Judaism by encouraging a new generation of Jews through education, social media campaigning, and grassroots organizing. As a movement powered by students, we seek to end antisemitism on college campuses and beyond.

 

Join our WhatsApp
summary: Jewish on Campus is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering Jewish students through social media campaigning, education, and grassroots organizing. We strive to provide Jewish students with a voice and resources to combat antisemitism.

name: Healing Hearts for Hope
description:
Healing Hearts for Hope is dedicated to building a compassionate, informed, and engaged community in support of individuals and families impacted by cancer. Through meaningful volunteer service, fundraising initiatives, advocacy, and awareness efforts, we collaborate with hospitals, community partners, and cancer-related organizations to make a tangible difference in the lives of those affected.

Recognizing that cancer can often isolate patients and their loved ones from everyday life, our organization strives to open doors to connection, joy, and hope. We aim to create opportunities that foster human connection, promote well-being, and allow those affected by cancer to experience enriching activities beyond clinical settings. At the same time, we cultivate an inclusive community rooted in empathy, service, and action, bringing people together to support, uplift, and advocate for those facing cancer.
summary: Our purpose is to have a positive impact in the lives of individuals and families affected by cancer. We aim to do this by fostering togetherness in our community through supporting and spreading awareness of the reality of cancer.

name: Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated
description: The Beta Chi chapter of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Inc. was chartered on March 31, 2023. Beta Chi is a university-based, undergraduate chapter that seeks to serve the students and community of Boston University while upholding the sorority's principles of Scholarship, Service, Sisterhood, and Finer Womanhood.
summary: An action-oriented, community conscious organization

name: Urban Birding Club
description: We organize weekly trips always either within walking distance or via public transit that take us to local parks and birding hotspots. While doing this we make an effort to teach new birders how to make use of the free resources available to them, and how to access the locations and tools we use on trips later on their own. While we make that effort for new birders we also encourage avid birders to join us both for a sense of community, as well as to better learn how and where to look for birds and natural places around our very urban campus.
summary: BU's local birdwatching club. Travel to local parks all around the greater Boston area via public transit in order to learn more about the city, and practice birding in an urban environment.

name: Retail, Luxury & Consumer Association
description:
The Retail, Luxury & Consumer Association (RLC) brings together students passionate about the luxury industry through speaker events, brand-partnered consulting programs, retail experiences, and an alumni mentorship program. RLC provides members with direct exposure and opportunities to learn, connect, and grow across fashion, beauty, hospitality, and beyond. 

             

Fill out the Membership Form to receive updates on applications and upcoming events.

(Note: Requesting membership on Terrier Central does not automatically add you to the mailing list.)
summary: The Retail, Luxury & Consumer Association brings together students passionate about the luxury industry through speaker events, brand-partnered consulting programs, retail experiences, and an alumni mentorship program.

name: Latin American Business Association
description: The Latin American Business Club (LABA) is a student organization that explores the role of Latin America in global business. Open to all majors, LABA seeks to connect students with leaders, from a wide variety of industries, who have vast experience working in the Latin American region. Through speaker events, mentorship opportunities, and workshops, LABA offers a unique platform for students to gain the necessary exposure to learn about Latin America and its business ventures.
summary: The Latin American Business Club (LABA) explores Latin America's role in global business. It is an organization, open to all majors, that connects students with industry leaders who have experience working in Latin America through events and workshops.

name: Survivor: Boston University
description:
The Game:

Survivor as a show has a simple format. Every week there is a “challenge” (see challenges section for further details), where the winner(s) of the challenge are immune and safe from being “voted out” of the game. Throughout the week, players can talk and strategize to vote off  one of the players that is not immune from being voted out, culminating in the votes being read. The game can be full of twists and turns and surprise “double vote offs”, all until a winner is crowned at the end of the semester, dubbed “sole survivor”. 

 

The Challenges:

Each challenge in Survivor BU typically follows one of 3 categories: mental, physical, or social. Mental challenges tend to be memory games like brain-teasers or puzzles, like a jigsaw or slide puzzle. Physical challenges tend to involve running and endurance tests. For example, we have had players run to grab puzzle pieces, toss bean bags and run to pick them up, or on the endurance side, hold up one arm for as long as possible or stand on one foot for as long as possible. Social challenges include games that test a players ability to make connections and draw conclusions about said connections. The main social challenge we have done every semester is called “Touchy Subjects” where players are asked questions about each other in the large group, for example, everyone is asked “Who is most likely to sleep through their 9AM class?” and everyone will answer a fellow player, and the people who answer the player who has the most votes will get a point. Therefore, challenges are fun, quick, and a large part of what makes the game like the CBS show, though at a much less extreme level. 

 

Our Production Team:

Survivor: BU’s production team is an ever growing team of students who are interested in Survivor, reality TV, and film and TV production as a whole. Our group of students range from those who have played our club’s game, to those who want to help out without the pressure of signing up to be a contestant. More significantly, contestants who are voted out before the jury section of the game, (around halfway through the semester), are encouraged to join production in order to join our greater community outside of the competitive game and help our production team run smoothly. Production members of Survivor: BU gain knowledge of still/moving camerawork through smartphones and standing cameras, general footage sharing and dynamic editing, and collaborative set design and management when an episode is recorded. Many production members know that the amount that you put in for the show is how much entertainment value you will get out. This means our production members can individually choose how much to put in, but because of that choice, there’s a general consensus that production has to be a strong backbone to ensure contestants have an enjoyable and fair game.

 

Our Community & Events:

Survivor: BU has many outside activities beyond the show production happening during the semester. For one, every Wednesday we meet to play board games and watch episodes of the show Survivor, so we build a community around our status as fans of the show and play board games together to bond. Additionally, many players who are voted out of the game stick around afterwards! For example, every single player from our last season either joined our production crew, attended future production shoots or attended the season finale party, so everyone is encouraged to return to club events regardless of status in the game. We also host minigames of Survivor and other similar game shows during the semester, so even when the game is not being played there are other social strategy games offered to club members. Overall, we encourage everyone to stick around during the season and actively host events like Survivor watch parties, plan board game nights and host other strategy shows for club members to participate in!

 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4Hj_Fwv5ejk
summary: Based on the reality TV show Survivor on CBS, Survivor BU emulates a “season” of the show in a semester-long social strategy game full of challenges and tribal councils, all while cultivating a wide and diverse community!

name: Interface of Science, Technology, Engineering, and Policy
description: INSTEP is a new student organization at Boston University that bridges the gap between engineering, science, and public policy. Our mission is to empower students to explore how science and innovation interact with regulation, advocacy, and public impact. Through workshops, case studies, advocacy training, and interdisciplinary collaborations, we aim to create a platform that promotes informed, civic-minded innovation. INSTEP is committed to fostering connections between BU's engineering community—including all disciplines such as mechanical, electrical, computer, systems, and materials—and broader societal needs.
summary: INSTEP is a student organization that bridges the gap between engineering, science, and public policy. Our mission is to empower students to explore how science and innovation interact with regulation, advocacy, and public impact.

name: The Pinky Toe Paper
description: The Pinky Toe is a multimedia, intersectional-feminist satire publication that regularly and consistently produces content for and about the Boston University community. The Pinky Toe aims to amplify the voices of those who identify as part of the LGBTQ+ and/or BIPOC community to provide a reliable outlet for students at a challenging university in a fast-paced city to smile, laugh and know that other students are going through similar struggles and experiences. In achieving this mission, The Pinky Toe actively seeks out and initiates collaboration between Boston University students across a wide range of interests including but not limited to writing, editing, web design, photography, social media, filmmaking, computer science, management and all forms of comedy. The Pinky Toe provides opportunities for students to understand the inner workings of a multimedia publication, learn and practice a wide variety of types of satire, participate in the editing and revising of their contributions and gain active leadership and management experience on a large, hardworking staff. The Pinky Toe aims to seek out new, creative ideas to determine the best ways to encourage dialogue about pop culture, politics, and current events.
summary: The Pinky Toe is a multimedia, intersectional-feminist satire publication that seeks to uplift marginalized students through laughter and creating a safe space to build community with other students are going through similar struggles and experiences.

name: American Civil Liberties Union at BU
description:
The ACLU @ BU is a student-led organization committed to educating and empowering students on civil rights, civil liberties, and civic engagement. Through thoughtful discussion, advocacy initiatives, and community action, we aim to foster a deeper understanding of the legal, political, and social issues that shape our world. Rooted in the mission of the American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) Massachusetts, our club provides a space for students to explore pressing issues—such as voting rights, racial justice, reproductive freedom, LGBTQIA+ rights, criminal legal reform, and more—and equips them with the tools to become agents of change in their communities.

As a collegiate organization affiliated with the ACLU of Massachusetts, ACLUBU offers students the opportunity to engage in real-world advocacy and civics education beyond the classroom. Members gain hands-on experience to learn the fundamentals of nonprofit and legislative work, and build meaningful connections with ACLUM staff, community partners, and fellow student advocates.

Whether you're passionate about justice, interested in legal systems, or simply looking to make a difference, ACLU @ BU is a welcoming space to learn, grow, and take action.

Please join our GroupMe link for the most up-to-date information about current events! https://groupme.com/join_group/109667380/gEhferik
summary: ACLU @ BU educates students on civil rights, civil liberties, and civic engagement through discussion, advocacy, and action. We foster understanding of legal, political, and social issues and empower students to become agents of change.

name: Prevent at BU
description:
BU Prevent

Mission & Vision Statements

 

Who We Are

BU Prevent is an undergraduate student association based in preventive medicine and service. We aim to provide targeted preventive health services to the Boston University community in order to improve overall wellness.

 

Our preliminary polling and conversations with the community have revealed some deeply unsettling facts. Among these are that 82% of BU undergraduates do not have a primary care physician in Boston, 61% do not meet health recommendation guidelines on good eating habits, and 38% have faced a serious health concern since beginning their college careers. Through thoroughly researched and approved wellness campaigns, naloxone and alcohol poisoning awareness trainings, and preventive care education, we will create a safer, healthier BU.

 

Mission Statement

The primary purposes of this organization are:

 

  1. Instill a preventive care mindset in the next generation of healthcare professionals.
  2. Create and communicate productive service initiatives for Boston University undergraduate members to participate in. 
  3. Increase health and wellness at large in the Boston University community.

 

Vision Statement

BU Prevent aims to be the biggest targeted service initiative in the undergraduate community, providing a network of like minded, driven students who come together to improve Boston University and serve their peers.
summary: BU Prevent aims to improve student health through prevention and instilling the principles of preventive medicine in the next generation of healthcare providers. Programming includes guest lecture events, student targeted service, and health campaigns.

name: Palestine Student Association at BU
description: The BU PSA is a cultural group that will celebrate Palestinian culture and traditions, and engage with education on Palestinian history and the current way of life in Palestine. It will be a space for students to learn and create a community of BU students with a shared background or interest in Palestine.
summary: The BU PSA is a cultural group that aims to foster a community for Palestinians, Arabs, and anyone interested in celebrating Palestinian culture and traditions and learning about Palestinian history and current living standards.

name: Women in Data Science
description:
Women in Data Science (WiDS) at Boston University is a chapter of the worldwide WiDS initiative, originally launched by Stanford University to inspire and support women in the field of data science. Our mission is to foster an inclusive community that empowers women and gender-diverse individuals pursuing careers or interests in data science, analytics, AI, and related fields.

At BU, WiDS provides a platform for students to gain hands-on experience through workshops, speaker events, hackathons, and collaborative projects. We connect members with industry professionals, alumni, and researchers to build strong networks and open doors to mentorship and career opportunities. Whether you're just beginning to explore data science or already deep in the field, WiDS is a space where you can learn, lead, and grow.

As part of a global network that spans more than 150 countries, our members benefit from access to international conferences, global datathons, and educational resources. We are committed to closing the gender gap in tech and believe that diverse perspectives drive innovation.

Everyone is welcome—WiDS is open to all students regardless of gender identity or experience level. Join us as we learn, build, and shape the future of data together!
summary: Women in Data Science (WiDS) at BU is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering women and gender-diverse individuals in data science through workshops, networking, mentorship, and community events. All are welcome to join!

name: Undergraduate Engineering Society
description:
We operate with the goal of bringing together engineers from all fields of engineering and helping them find the opportunities and connections they want at BU! Our mission is to foster interdisciplinary collaboration, create a welcoming community, and serve as a central hub for engineering undergraduates across all disciplines.

We are in the process of developing a mobile application which can be expanded but at its base will show BU students an easy to browse and constantly updated list of BU Engineering clubs, what they are doing, and how to contact them/get involved. This platform will serve as a live map of BU’s engineering ecosystem, making it simpler than ever for students to find organizations aligned with their passions, whether they’re looking to join competitive teams, advocacy groups, or professional societies. As the app grows, we hope to incorporate features like club event calendars, notifications, and student-submitted feedback to continually improve usability and engagement.

Additionally, we will be talking to professors to have a list of much of the research going on at BU and how students can contact those professors. This resource will help demystify the research process, especially for younger students, by offering transparency and clarity into lab goals, required skills, and open positions. By highlighting current projects and making mentorship more approachable, we aim to lower the barriers to entering academic research and help students build long-term relationships with faculty.

Aside from the mobile application, we will coordinate with other more specific academic engineering societies (like IEEE, MechE, Biomed Society, etc...) to hold combined social events where we can provide undergraduate engineering students with food, games, and the opportunity to meet students of similar or different backgrounds and interests, creating a more diverse and wider net for BUs blossoming engineers. These events are more than just casual gatherings—they are networking opportunities, collaborative brainstorming spaces, and launching pads for future projects, friendships, and career exploration. We hope to create a culture where cross-disciplinary dialogue is both encouraged and celebrated.

We also plan to be an accessible and knowledgable tool for newer and incoming students, offering up meetings or help to any students who request it. Whether it’s help navigating course selection, understanding major requirements, choosing electives, or preparing for internships, we want to be a go-to peer support system. We may direct them to where they need to go to get help, or give them the advice they need ourselves (from our own experiences). This could include student-run Q&A sessions, mentorship matching, or even one-on-one office hours led by upperclassmen.

All around we plan to provide social cultivation for all students, and to provide assistance to anyone who needs it! By building a culture of openness, engagement, and support, we aim to uplift all members of the BU engineering community — academically, professionally, and personally. We want to make it easier to navigate BU's wealth of opportunities.
summary: We operate with the goal of bringing together engineers from all fields of engineering and helping them find the opportunities and connections they want at BU!

name: Mu Epsilon Delta
description:
Mu Epsilon Delta is Boston University’s first and only nationally affiliated pre-health fraternity. Our mission is to create an inclusive, supportive, and multidisciplinary space where students pursuing any pre-health path, medicine, dentistry, veterinary, PA, public health, and more, can thrive together. Unlike other pre-health organizations on campus, Mu Epsilon Delta is built on the foundation of brotherhood, offering Bigs and Littles, family lines, and a close-knit network that supports academic, emotional, and professional growth.

We are open to all students regardless of GPA, academic standing, or specific career goals. Whether you’re pre-med, pre-dental, or still figuring it out, you’ll find a home with us. Through weekly meetings, bonding events, hands-on clinics (like suturing and CPR certification), guest speakers, and service projects, we prepare our members not only for professional school, but for a life of purpose and connection in healthcare.

Our national affiliation allows us to provide access to an extended network of students, alumni, and practicing professionals. We've already hosted events with speakers like Dr. Anthony Schena (Boston Celtics team physician), and our members have gained internships, shadowing, and job opportunities through fraternity connections.

We also uplift first-generation and underrepresented students by providing accessible resources like shared MCAT prep materials, EMS job support, and peer mentoring.

Joining Mu Epsilon Delta means gaining a family of future healthcare professionals who study together, grow together, and push each other to succeed. We’re not just preparing for careers, we’re building lifelong friendships and a stronger, more inclusive healthcare community.
summary: Mu Epsilon Delta is BU’s first pre-health fraternity, dedicated to building an inclusive, supportive community across all healthcare tracks through brotherhood, mentorship, and professional development. We welcome students of all backgrounds and academic

name: Dominican Student Association at BU
summary: DSA at BU brings Dominican students and anyone interested in Dominican culture together to build community through builds community through dance, food, events, and educational opportunities that highlight the richness of Dominican culture at BU.

name: Untangle
description: Untangle is a multimedia artist collective founded in the aftermath of the 2021 Atlanta Spa Shootings. Through our art, we seek to untangle our identities and lived experiences, build community, and spark conversation around Asian American sociopolitical experiences. Throughout the academic year, we collaboratively work on the creation of a print zine and host community events. Based on a collectively envisioned theme, Untangle members and the BU community are welcome to submit responses and creative work for the zine. Examples of submissions have included interviews, photography, embroidery, short stories, public art, and more. Untangle members will then compile submissions and design the zine to be printed and released in time for the end-of-year launch. To further build community, we also host events related to the magazine’s theme where everyone, no matter their artistic ability or background, is encouraged to reflect, engage in dialogue, and create together. Our mission is to create a welcoming and creative space where people can challenge themselves as artists and citizens within our complex sociopolitical fabric.
summary: Untangle is a multimedia artist collective that aims to explore the sociopolitical experiences of Asian American communities by creating and publishing a print magazine and hosting community events that encourage learning, reflection, and dialogue.

name: The Orthodox Christian Fellowship of BU
description:
Our organization, the Orthodox Christian Fellowship of Boston University, will be affiliated with the national Orthodox Christian Fellowship (OCF). With many chapters throughout the United States, OCF seeks to be a home for Orthodox Christian college students – a place where they can find like-minded people and grow spiritually, emotionally and academically. 

 

We want to offer college students a rich community in Christ, one that inspires “a life-long love of God and neighbor” (​​https://ocf.net/about-us/). Our mission is to create a space and community that will guide Orthodox college students “along the path to Jesus Christ through His church, cultivating a campus community of worship, witness, service, fellowship, and education” (​​https://ocf.net/about-us/). 

 

Our vision is the following: “To be the loving presence of Christ and the Orthodox Christian Church on college campuses. To be an integral part of students’ spiritual formation by challenging them to explore the Faith. To inspire students to commit to living an Orthodox Christian way of life daily” (https://ocf.net/about-us/).

 

While there are other Christian clubs on the BU campus, OCF will be different in that it is specifically Orthodox, thus more effectively meeting the unique needs of Orthodox Christian students, who are a growing demographic.

 

We welcome all students, including current Orthodox Christians as well as inquirers. We will hold weekly meetings which will consist of community time, a prayer service, and group discussion. In addition, we will invite speakers and participate in activities such as trips, movie nights, and volunteer service projects. 

 

For more information, we invite you to visit: https://ocf.net/
summary: Our Orthodox Christian Fellowship, or OCF, is part of the national OCF organization. OCF offers Orthodox Christian students and anyone interested in learning more “a rich community in Christ, inspiring a life-long love of God and neighbor.”

name: Sports Analytics Group at BU
description:
Our club, Sports Analytics Group, aims to bring together students who are passionate about sports and teach them about analytics and the world of sports statistics. We plan to do this through bi-weekly meetings and various professional and recreational events, like speaker panels, networking events, sports viewing parties, bracket-making, and field visits.

Further, we are providing a structured curriculum for those members interested in learning more technical skills involved in sports analytics. We aim to build this curriculum to introduce concepts such as statistics and basic programming to assist those who may be unfamiliar with concepts outside of their pursued majors. The culmination of this curriculum is to allow club members to create a research project, with the ability to collaborate with others and leverage their knowledge into presentable and thought-provoking insights. This will allow club members to build a professional portfolio.

Overall, we want this club to be a space for those who enjoy sports and statistics to come together and collaborate both on projects and research, but also in casual conversation and on fun events to meet new people and connect!
summary: SAG@BU is a club that unites sports and statistics lovers, catering to both recreational and professional interests by bringing our members together in projects, research, and fun events to make new connections.

name: Urbanism Club at BU
summary: We are Boston University's only student organization for Terriers interested in anything related to urbanism. We hold weekly meetings on topics relating to urbanism, host field trips and social events, and collaborate with other local organizations.

name: LatinasBU
description: LatinasBU is a student-led organization at Boston University dedicated to empowering, supporting, and celebrating Latina and femme-identifying students. Our mission is to create a safe and affirming community where members can connect through shared cultural experiences, personal growth, and leadership development. Through social events, cultural programming, workshops, mentorship, and advocacy initiatives, LatinasBU fosters a strong sisterhood rooted in identity, empowerment, and community. We aim to uplift Latina voices on campus, build meaningful relationships, and inspire future Latina leaders within and beyond Boston University.
summary: LatinasBU empowers Latina and femme-identifying students at BU by building community, celebrating culture, fostering leadership, and creating a supportive sisterhood.

name: Los Mariachi Terriers
summary: Los Mariachi Terriers celebrate mariachi music and Mexican culture at BU through inclusive performances, workshops, and events. We welcome all students to explore music, build community, and challenge stereotypes through cultural expression.

name: Ahlul-Bayt Student Association at Boston University
description: The Ahlul-Bayt Student Association’s mission is to cater to the spiritual and educational needs of Muslim students belonging to the Shia sect at Boston University, particularly those who have been marginalized in other spaces. It is a medium to connect those well-versed in Islam to expand their knowledge to those who wish to seek that knowledge. Toward the general community of Muslims and Non-Muslims its responsibility is to foster mutual-respect, to promote growth and evolution in intellectual and spiritual areas, and to stand up for truth, justice and peace. Toward the greater Muslim community, its responsibility is to actively promote unity, mutual-respect and cooperation between different schools of thought within Islam.
summary: The Ahlul-Bayt Student Association at BU supports Shia Muslim students, fostering spiritual and educational growth. It promotes unity, mutual respect, and cooperation within Islam, while advocating for truth, justice, and peace in the broader community.
